description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
2. TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.
3. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_XDGPath asserts Path returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx for a project without .ddx/ and with `git remote get-url origin = git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`.
4. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_LocalFallback covers the no-remote `local/<basename>-<sha>/` form.
5. TestDDxRoot_URLParsing covers https, ssh, custom-host, missing-remote cases (table-driven).
6. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapInitsGitRepoInXDG asserts the XDG dir is created with `git init` on first bootstrap and a HEAD commit exists.
7. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapIdempotent asserts two concurrent calls do not race-create.
8. TestWorktreeRegistry_FirstWorktreeIsMaster asserts auto-master on first access.
9. TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
10. TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
11. Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.
12. Specs listed under SPEC IMPACT are updated in the same commit as the code refactor. Each updated spec references this bead ID.

14. lefthook run pre-commit passes (including the new no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths rule).
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS
None. This is the foundational refactor.

description:
PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.
title: C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result

description:
PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: server: launch managed worker subprocesses with persisted PID/PGID

description:
PROBLEM
The watchdog reaper still mirrors the Stop path's pid-limited cleanup. A stalled managed worker can be marked reaped and have its bead claim released, yet still leave descendants behind unless reap uses the same managed-tree cleanup path as Stop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1347-1414 selects reaping candidates and passes only pid into reapWorker.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1417-1482 emits bead.reaped, releases the bead claim, and then calls terminateProcessGroup(pid, grace) only when pid > 0.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 shows the same pid-only limitation that exists in Stop, but the reap path is separate and can drift if it does not reuse the shared cleanup helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route watchdog reaping through the same managed-worker tree cleanup helper used by Stop.
- Keep bead.reaped emission, claim release, and terminal-state persistence intact.
- Make the reap path idempotent so repeated sweeps do not double-clean the same worker.
- Add a process-tree test that proves watchdog reap follows the SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL path and releases the claimed bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add restart policy logic in this bead.
- Do not change Stop beyond the shared helper reuse already required by the stop bead.
- Do not touch external workers or CLI commands.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree proves watchdog reap follows the same SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL cleanup path and releases the claimed bead.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: server: reap managed worker process trees through watchdog

After wiring: spawn a worker, drain a few beads, verify execution evidence shows the new code paths firing. 4. description:
CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
notes: superseded by REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482); the 4 unwired functions (escalation.Ladder.Next, EvaluateRetryPolicy, RunWithLockBackoff, DispatchPostAttempt) surfaced as initial production-reachability violations and are absorbed into child bead ddx-83b8994f (internal/agent backfill).
title: audit + wire (or delete) the unwired escalation/retry/triage code from recent landed beads

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

description:
PROBLEM: execute-bead workers hold git locks across the entire LLM subprocess invocation, blocking concurrent tracker operations.

FIX: Refactor to acquire locks only for the duration of git mutations, not across subprocess waits. Specifically:
- Scope .git/index.lock acquisition to actual git commands
- Scope .ddx/.git-tracker.lock acquisition to tracker file writes
- Never hold locks while waiting on claude/codex/gemini subprocess

CROSS-REF: Parent ddx-57c40485; original Fizeau incident AR-2026-05-17-repo.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing)
- cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go (if needed)
- Test: git_index_lock_test.go
title: Fix lock scoping: release .git/index.lock and .ddx/.git-tracker.lock before harness subprocess

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go exports withHeartbeat(ctx, beadID, interval, store, fn) with the signature defined above.
2. execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 replaced with a single withHeartbeat call; no inline goroutine + WaitGroup remains in the loop body.
3. Drain loop caller of withHeartbeat is the production site (execute_bead_loop.go or its successor after C13 rename).
4. TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn in cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go: injects a stub ticker; verifies Heartbeat called at least once before fn returns.
5. TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates: cancels ctx before fn returns; verifies goroutine exits without blocking.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.

description:
PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: heartbeat timing and lifecycle belong in one helper, not inline with candidate execution.
- P4: one bead heartbeat bug must not leak into the rest of the drain loop.
- P5: extracted heartbeat behavior supports always-visible worker state.

acceptance:

2. A written decision exists (in this bead's notes, a linked bead, or a docs/ file) stating whether the main-worktree-dirty-during-phase=running behavior observed in #2 is intentional; if intentional, it names where that state is now surfaced to an operator/agent (e.g. a new `ddx work status` field) rather than requiring a bare `git status` read.
3. A written decision exists stating whether execute-bead's default merge/acceptance gate now runs (or explicitly does not run, with a stated reason) a full workspace build/test independent of a bead's own narrower acceptance scope.
description:
PROBLEM
Observed directly during a live human-in-the-loop session against a real project (pqueue, /Users/erik/Projects/pqueue), not synthesized. Three related, concrete observations from one session:

1. DUPLICATE CLAIM RACE: a project-scoped `ddx work --server-managed` daemon that had been running for hours, and a foreground `ddx work --json` the operator started manually, both claimed the SAME bead within ~40 seconds of each other on two separate occasions (pqueue-70c85ac1 at 15:26:50/15:27:32, pqueue-5c5353f7 at 16:18:07/16:18:44), each spinning up its own `.ddx-exec-wt` worktree. The second occurrence produced a `pre_claim_intake.system_unready` warning ("project root mutation rejected... revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment") twice on the bead's event log, warn-only, non-blocking. This looks like the same class of issue tracked by ddx-eea2dfca/ddx-69672921 (repeated pre-claim system-unready warnings not surfaced as a clear operator-visible signal) -- filing this as a concrete, reproducible real-world instance rather than duplicating that epic's scope.

2. MAIN WORKTREE LEFT DIRTY DURING A LIVE ATTEMPT: while a project-scoped server-managed worker was `phase=running` on a bead (pqueue-6f8d85db, worktree=.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-pqueue-6f8d85db-...), the PROJECT'S MAIN WORKTREE (not the isolated attempt worktree) showed 17 uncommitted, modified files spanning many unrelated crates, sitting dirty for several minutes across repeated `git status` checks with no new commits landing in between (HEAD stayed at the same `chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead ...` commit throughout). An operator or an agent (Claude Code, in this case) working in that same main worktree during an active attempt has no reliable signal for which uncommitted changes are "the live attempt's WIP, do not touch" versus safe to edit/commit -- a `git status`-only view is ambiguous. If this dirty-main-worktree state during `phase=running` is intentional (e.g. a staging step before a checkpoint commit), it should be documented and/or surfaced by `ddx work status`/`ddx bead show` so a human or agent sharing the worktree can tell not to touch it; if it is not intentional, it looks like an isolation leak from the attempt worktree back into the main one.

3. MERGE GATE DID NOT CATCH A WORKSPACE-WIDE COMPILE BREAK: a bead landed to `main` (pqueue commit 75190c3, "feat: add change-record emission foundation") that changed a shared struct (`ProjectionImageItem` in pqueue-projection) without updating one of its several construction call sites (`crates/pqueue-sqlite/src/relational.rs:5410`), leaving `cargo build --workspace` (and `--all-features`) broken on `main` for every subsequent bead attempt forked from that point, including a completely unrelated one (pqueue-postgres) the operator was validating by hand at the time. This suggests that bead's own acceptance/merge gate ran a narrower build/test scope than `cargo build --workspace --all-features`, so a cross-crate breakage in a sibling crate was not caught before landing on shared `main`.

PROPOSED FIX
Not prescribed here -- filing as observations/evidence for ddx maintainers to triage against existing related epics (ddx-eea2dfca, ddx-69672921, ddx-2189a07f, ddx-693d504f) rather than guessing at implementation. Concretely worth deciding:
- Whether #1's warn-only pre_claim_intake outcome should escalate after N repeats within a short window, per the existing part-3 epic.
- Whether #2's dirty-main-worktree-during-phase=running is by design; if so, expose it via `ddx work status` (e.g. an explicit "main worktree has in-flight attempt WIP, do not modify" flag) rather than leaving it to a bare `git status` read.
- Whether execute-bead's default merge/acceptance gate should require a full `cargo build --workspace --all-features` (or project-equivalent) regardless of the bead's own narrower test scope, specifically to catch cross-crate breakage like #3.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement any of the three fixes here -- this bead is the observation/triage request only.
- Do not touch the pqueue repository or its bead tracker from this bead.
title: Concurrent-worker race left a dirty main worktree + duplicate claim during live pqueue session

description:
PROBLEM
  The run/try/work dispatch paths must not set Profile, Harness, or Model directly on ResolvedConfig or Runtime structs — those are durable knobs that belong in config.LoadAndResolve. Without a mechanically-enforced lint rule, future edits can silently reintroduce the anti-pattern and bypass the fizeau routing contract.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go (package routinglint) currently forbids retired compensating-routing identifiers (ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, etc.) and flag literals (--escalate, profile_ladders). It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.
  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.
  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changes to agent_run or agent_run_overrides command behavior.
  - Changes to the fizeau or ladder routing logic.
  - Lint rules for non-routing fields on Runtime structs (covered by runtimelint).

acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyReapsOnlyStaleAttemptProcessGroups` using injected scanner/killer fakes where needed, proving `--apply` terminates only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
2. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyPreservesLiveAndForeignProcessGroups` proving live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and uncertain candidates are preserved with operator-visible warnings when appropriate.
3. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyTerminatesGracefullyThenBoundedKill` or an equivalent assertion proving the killer uses graceful terminate before bounded kill when a process group remains alive.

5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'ExecutionCleanupManager_Apply.*Process|ProcessReap|AttemptDescendant|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx cleanup --apply` can delete stale DDx execution files, but it cannot terminate stale DDx-owned attempt-descendant process groups that are provably orphaned. Heavy leftover processes can continue burning CPU or holding ports after their original attempt is gone.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` applies file cleanup for stale temp dirs, worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, logs, worker dirs, and refs, but contains no safe process-group termination path.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` has no process reaping counts or warnings in `ExecutionCleanupSummary`, so apply mode cannot report terminated, preserved, blocked, or unsafe process groups.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:82-100` and `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:155-168` intentionally gate startup harness reaping on top-level harness names and dead bead lease owners; that does not cover descendant process groups whose harness root has already exited.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` sets process group and parent-death attributes for normal harness children, which cleanup can use only when stale ownership and liveness evidence prove the group is DDx-owned and safe to reap.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend execution cleanup apply mode with an injectable process-group killer and conservative stale ownership checks. Apply may terminate only process groups associated with the current project's DDx execution root where run-state cleanup liveness is expired or missing, the recorded owner PID is gone, the worktree is under the current project execution root, and no live worker sidecar or active registered worktree owns it. Preserve live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and any uncertain candidate. Prefer graceful terminate followed by bounded kill where feasible. Surface blocked warnings instead of silently deleting files when a process is detected but cannot be killed safely.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.

acceptance:
1. `ddx init --global --silent` exits 0 and creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ with the bundled library content.
2. `ddx init --global` is idempotent: running twice does not error and does not destroy local-only files under global/.
3. TestInitGlobal_PopulatesXDGGlobalDir asserts the directory structure post-install matches what convention mode expects.
4. TestInitGlobal_IsIdempotent asserts a second run does not error and re-syncs the plugin content.
5. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.
6. ddx doctor's "Global Install" check exists and reports the install path + status.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.
ddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.
title: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx-28f3cf37` still reads like a broad federation UI follow-up, but the real gap is the owner-targeted worker-control path. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338` keeps TC-017 planned, while `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177` already defines the write-routing contract and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214` already spells out the expected start/stop/offline/idempotency behavior at the UI level.

ROOT CAUSE
The current bead is still coupled to the broader federation UI story in `ddx-28f3cf37`, but the concrete contract is already isolated in FEAT-026 and the dedicated worker-control spec. What is missing is a narrower bead body that only tracks the live owner-targeted write-routing implementation and the Go-side tests that prove `startWorker` and `stopWorker` reach exactly one owning spoke.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-28f3cf37` so it covers only worker start/stop owner-targeted writes, not read-federation smoke.
- Name the missing implementation tests explicitly as `TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Keep the bead aligned to FEAT-026 and TC-017 instead of duplicating the read-only federation tests.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new `/federation` route read coverage.
- Do not touch bead/document writes here.
- Do not add ts-net or cross-host networking beyond the local loopback gate.
- Do not modify the FEAT-026 text.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-a038a090`. Upstream stale bead context: `ddx-0793ac75`.

   are already satisfied. `ddx bead show ddx-28f3cf37` reads as a worker-control
   owner-targeted write bead (title "federation: owner-targeted worker-control writes"),
   names TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner,
   TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner,
   TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke, and
   TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID in its description, and its `notes` field
   cites FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177, TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338, and
   federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214. This reconciliation was already
   committed in 523e03b80 ("chore(beads): update ddx-28f3cf37 [ddx: doc-stamp]") prior
   to this execution. No further tracker edit is needed to satisfy AC1-3.

2. AC4's Go clause passes as-is:

   -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql (27.1s)
   -> ok github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation (2.2s)

   -> 4 failed (starts a spoke worker from the hub, stops a spoke worker from the hub,
      refuses commands for an offline spoke, deduplicates retried forwarded worker commands)
   The spec file's own header (federation-worker-control.spec.ts:7-8) states: "These
   tests MUST FAIL until the hub federation workers view implements forwarded worker
   start/stop for spoke-owned projects." Making them pass requires implementing
   owner-targeted write forwarding across the GraphQL resolvers, the federation
   write-routing layer, and the Svelte workers-federation UI -- a feature-sized change
   that is exactly the work ddx-28f3cf37 exists to track, not something a bead titled
   "narrow the worker-control follow-up bead" is scoped to build. ddx-28f3cf37 remains
   open (unimplemented) as of this run, and this bead's NON-SCOPE section confines the
   work here to the bead-tracker text, not the write-routing implementation.

Recommendation: this bead's tracker-narrowing objective is already complete (AC1-3,
plus the Go half of AC4). The remaining playwright gap in AC4 will close naturally
once ddx-28f3cf37 is implemented and its four named Go tests plus the e2e spec go
green -- that is out of scope here. No code or bead-tracker change is being committed
by this run.

description:

`ddx-359e812b` is still too broad for what remains. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` keeps bead, spec, and document writes planned, but the current Playwright suite already covers the mocked forwarded paths in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, while `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

FEAT-026 says forwarding is owner-targeted and never broadcast (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`), so the bead should only track the missing live bead/document write persistence and refusal behavior, not the already-covered smoke paths.

acceptance:
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves StartExecuteLoop creates a server-managed drain worker with nonzero PID and PGID in WorkerRecord and persisted status.json.
2. TestManagedWorkerServerManagedFlagRunsWorkLoop proves the hidden ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> entry point reads the persisted spec and runs the same execute-loop path used by current server-managed workers.
3. TestManagedWorkerProcessBoundarySkipsExternalReportedWorkers proves process-boundary recording is applied only to server-owned work workers, not external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup|TestManagedWorkerServerManagedFlagRunsWorkLoop|TestManagedWorkerProcessBoundarySkipsExternalReportedWorkers' passes.
5. make install is run after the code change lands.

description:
PROBLEM
Server-managed drain workers are still started as in-process goroutines, so their WorkerRecord usually has PID zero and no PGID. The server cannot reliably target the ddx work process tree that later launches claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:123-127 records only PID and has no persisted PGID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as managing in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:461-462 starts drainProgress and runWorker goroutines instead of a server-owned OS process. cli/internal/server/workers.go:414-436 writes the initial WorkerRecord before any managed worker process boundary exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a server-managed subprocess launch path for StartExecuteLoop in cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-464. Persist the ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as today, then start the current ddx binary as ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> or an equivalent hidden internal entry point in its own process group. Record nonzero PID and PGID on WorkerRecord and status.json. Keep the existing in-process path only where tests explicitly inject it, and do not make externally reported workers look server-owned.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go was named when the command was 'ddx agent work'. The command is now 'ddx work' (with ddx agent work as a back-compat alias), and the FEAT-010 refactor (ddx-5cb6e6cd) moves to a work package. The filename no longer matches the command surface or the package intent.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: the file contains the Drain loop logic. Its name derives from the pre-refactor 'execute-bead-loop' command, which has been superseded by 'ddx work'.
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) plans 'file/identifier rename per refactor proposal §3'. If C13 only renames identifiers (not the file), this bead handles the file rename. If C13 subsumes the file rename, this bead should be closed as duplicate — verify after C13 lands.

PROPOSED FIX
- After C13 (ddx-387a0178) lands: confirm whether execute_bead_loop.go was renamed or only its identifiers.
- If file was NOT renamed: rename cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go → drain_loop.go (preferred: matches 'drain the bead queue' conceptual model) or work_loop.go.
- Update all import paths and references.

NON-SCOPE

2. `TestInstallGlobal_WritesXDGAndAgentTierLinks` (install_global_test.go:48) asserts the same XDG + agent-tier link layout for AC1.
3. `TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:211) asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves the project install untouched.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 2 of 4)

Operators who want one Claude Code / Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so a plugin is written once to the machine-wide tree and linked into the home-level harness skill directories, available across every project without per-project setup.

ROOT CAUSE
- `runInstall` (cli/cmd/install.go:54) reads the `--global` flag (install.go:49,57) and routes target resolution through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184). When global is true it sets `installRoot = ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and skill dirs to `~/.agents/skills` and `~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-192); state file selection is via `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202-215). Local installs route the same way in `installLocal` (install.go:358, global branch install.go:450-507).
- Update: `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300) operates exclusively on `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/` via `registry.InstallPackage(adjustedPkg, ddxroot.GlobalDir())` (update.go:337) and saves the global state (update.go:346); the `--global` flag is parsed in `extractUpdateOptions` (update.go:395) into `UpdateOptions.Global` (update.go:35) and dispatched from `runUpdate` (update.go:65).

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name> --global` writes the plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and creates symlink-or-copy entries under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`, and does NOT touch the project's `.claude/skills` / `.agents/skills`. Ensure `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and global state, never the project tree. Expose `--global` consistently on the plugin query surface (`ddx plugin list` / `installed`).

NON-SCOPE
- The no-flag project install in either mode (slice 3).
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) — global installs are resolved via `ResolvePlugin`'s global layer.
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — depends on `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.

acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerWatchDoesNotRetryExecutionIneligibleAfterPreservedNoChanges` proving a watch-mode worker whose first attempt records a preserved/no-change decomposition result and sets `execution-eligible=false` does not dispatch the same bead a second time in the same watcher run.
2. Add or extend coverage proving a decomposed parent with label `decomposed` and `execution-eligible=false` is skipped after a fresh store read even when an older queue snapshot still contains that bead.
3. Add or extend coverage proving the worker either selects the next execution-ready bead or idles with a clear skip event when the stale candidate becomes execution-ineligible.
4. The emitted result/event names the skipped bead and reason without marking the bead blocked, closed, or claimed.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadWorkerWatchDoesNotRetryExecutionIneligibleAfterPreservedNoChanges|TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead' -count=1` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` retried parent bead `ddx-c191e91a` after the parent had already been decomposed and marked execution-ineligible. On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

DEPS
None. This is a worker-loop correctness bug independent of the child implementation beads for `ddx-c191e91a`.
title: work: stop retrying execution-ineligible decomposed parents

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)
- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl
- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -> pre_claim_intake.start -> pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle.
- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:
  {
    "bead_id":"fizeau-5b2a8950",
    "decision":"warn",
    "decision_source":"pre_claim_intake",
    "detail":"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string",
    "fingerprint":"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f",
    "outcome":"intake_error",
    "policy_mode":"warn-only",
    "reason":"system_unready",
    "rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready",
    "suggested_action":"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry",
    "system_reason":"unavailable"
  }
- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.
- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.
- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.
2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 50s) but never claims anything. Operator has no signal beyond tailing the agent-loop log.
3. The 'suggested_action' text ('check the
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- pre_claim_intake decoder accepts readiness_checks.verdict as JSON bool, JSON string, or absent; coerces to a documented canonical form. Unit test covers all three. Verified by a focused decoder test in the pre-claim intake package.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: pre_claim_intake decoder rejects readiness_checks.verdict=bool; entire f: part 1

acceptance:
1. `TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent` covers `verdict:true`, `verdict:false`, string verdicts including `"ready"`/`"not_ready"`, `null`, and absent verdict, and asserts the canonical decoded values.
2. `TestReadinessChecksSchema` or an equivalent focused decoder/schema test proves the same verdict forms decode through the pre-claim readiness classification payload without producing `intake_error`.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent|TestReadinessChecksSchema'` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -> `pass`, `false` -> `fail`, string -> trimmed string, null/absent -> empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau route or harness configuration. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
None.
title: Fix pre-claim intake verdict decoding for bool/string/absent readiness checks

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go Land() invokes no git fetch and no git push; LandingGitOps interface no longer declares FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, or FetchOriginAncestryCheck methods.
2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go file-header comment (formerly lines 27-29) describes a network-free flow with no push step.
3. landPushAutoRecover function and any LandResult fields naming push outcomes (PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered) are removed; fetchOriginError type is removed.
4. withMainGitLock in Land() wraps only local-index operations (merge or update-ref + evidence commit + worktree sync); a unit test asserts the lock is released within 1s under a fake LandingGitOps with simulated 5s of post-Land work in the caller (proving the lock does not extend beyond Land() return).
5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.
6. Existing tests that asserted push-race recovery (e.g. TestLandPushAutoRecover_*) are removed or rewritten; no test references LandResult.PushFailed.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.
title: agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)

description:

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:71 defines AxonBackend around BeadsFile, EventsFile, and LockDir instead of a GraphQL client/session.

PROPOSED FIX

- Implement bead row and event entity encode/decode helpers against the generated GraphQL shapes, preserving the two-collection model ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events.
- Keep JSONL as the default backend and keep the existing experimental gate behavior unchanged.
- Add unit tests for client construction/mapping that do not require a live Axon server.

NON-SCOPE

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_PreClaimDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, pre-claim fingerprint/threshold facts, and operator-attention reason for the repeated warning family.
- Existing related behavior from `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered by reuse/alignment with its helpers or by waiting on that dependency; this bead does not add a duplicate pre-claim warning escalation implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. `TestFederationForwardedWriteIdempotency_WorkerStart` or an equivalent e2e assertion proves retrying the same hub-to-spoke worker start request id returns the original worker and does not create a second spoke-local worker.
2. `TestFederationForwardedWriteIdempotency_BeadOrDocumentWrite` or an equivalent e2e assertion proves retrying the same hub-to-spoke bead or document write request id does not append duplicate bead events or duplicate file writes.
3. The tests assert the dedupe key/request id is present in the forwarded envelope or audit metadata.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/... ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... -run 'Idempotency|ForwardedWrite|Federation' -count=1` passes.\n5. If Playwright is used, `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or the new sibling e2e file passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Forwarded federation writes need an autonomous idempotency proof. Retrying the same hub-issued command with the same request id must replay the original result and must not launch duplicate workers, append duplicate bead events, or write the same document twice.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:338 defines TC-017.5 idempotency for federated worker commands.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:379 defines TC-020.6 idempotency for mutating managed-node commands.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarded commands to carry request id, target node/project, and expected version metadata.
- Existing GraphQL idempotency tests such as cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_runs_test.go:101-154 and resolver_operator_prompt_test.go:218-231 cover local idempotency surfaces, but the federation owner-write path needs its own forwarded-command coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add focused Go tests or live multi-process tests for forwarded federation write idempotency.
- Use the same request id/idempotency key for two identical hub-issued worker, bead, or document write commands.
- Assert the second response returns the original result/deduplicated state and does not duplicate spoke-local state.
- Cover at least two command classes: worker start and one persistent data mutation (`beadCreate` or `documentWrite`).
- Keep the test deterministic with loopback servers and synthetic request ids; do not rely on wall-clock sleeps beyond short polling for process readiness.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not build a new global idempotency framework unless existing request-id plumbing cannot support the tests.
- Do not add real network/tailnet tests.
- Do not change unrelated local run/operator-prompt idempotency behavior.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. Depends on live owner-targeted write coverage so the idempotency assertions attach to a proven forwarding path.
title: federation: prove forwarded write idempotency

acceptance:
1. New test TestServerStartupReconcile_ReportsStaleDesiredProjectAudit seeds a stale desired-state or ServerState project entry and proves startup reconcile exposes an audit/error event for the skipped or pruned project.
2. TestServerStartupReconcile_ReportsStaleDesiredProjectAudit proves the audit/error payload includes the exact stale project path.
3. TestServerStartupReconcile_ReportsStaleDesiredProjectAudit proves no audit/error is emitted for valid desired worker state for active projects.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When startup reconcile encounters stale desired-state or persisted ServerState project entries, operators need observable evidence of the skip or prune decision. Without an audit/error event carrying the project path, stale entries are hard to distinguish from worker launch failures or normal restart recovery.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/server.go:384-416 discovers desired worker project roots from server WorkingDir and persisted ServerState projects, but stale ServerState/desired-state entries can be ignored, retried, or failed without a dedicated observable audit/error event tied to the stale project path.
- cli/internal/server/server_startup_reconcile_test.go:96-204 does not assert that stale desired-state/project entries produce audit/error output with the affected project path.

PROPOSED FIX
Emit a startup-reconcile audit/error event whenever a desired project is skipped or pruned as stale. The event must include the stale project path and enough reason text/status for operators to diagnose missing, invalid, or non-live desired projects.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement GraphQL worker count/cap changes from ddx-c713d75e. Do not change bead claim semantics or Fizeau routing. Do not remove valid desired worker state for active projects such as /Users/erik/Projects/ddx and /Users/erik/Projects/sesame. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-operator-workbench. Parent bead: ddx-8674fc2d.
title: server: audit stale desired project skips during startup reconcile

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_SerializesGitOperations asserts both instruction variants contain a direct prohibition on parallel git/staging/commit operations and require sequential git add/git commit handling.
2. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_RequiresStagedPreCommitGate asserts both instruction variants say lefthook run pre-commit must be rerun after staging when staged files drive hooks and that no-staged-files runs are insufficient evidence.
3. Prompt snapshot or guardrail coverage is updated so future prompt edits preserve both rules.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadInstructions_(SerializesGitOperations|RequiresStagedPreCommitGate)|TestExecuteBeadInstructionsText' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, two Codex execute-bead workers recovered from avoidable tool-sequencing errors. For ddx-b6859802, the worker staged ordinary files and ignored evidence with parallel git add calls; one failed with: fatal: Unable to create ... index.lock: File exists. For ddx-bb8e7d07, the worker ran lefthook run pre-commit before staging and got a no-staged-files skip, then had to rerun after staging; it also attempted to interrupt a completed test-poll session. These recoveries waste worker time and can look like DDx dirty-tree failures even when the implementation is otherwise fine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2337-2341 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2369-2373 tell rich-prompt and embedded-agent workers to run tests, stage specific paths, and commit once. They do not explicitly prohibit parallel git/index operations, do not say that lefthook run pre-commit must be run after staging when hooks are staged-file-sensitive, and do not warn that a no-staged-files hook run is insufficient acceptance evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText to require git/staging/commit commands to run sequentially, not through parallel tool calls.
- Require workers to rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging the exact commit set when pre-commit depends on staged files; an earlier no-staged-files hook run is not sufficient AC evidence.
- Add or extend prompt guardrail/snapshot tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_prompt_snapshot_test.go so the rule cannot regress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change core gitlock recovery.
- Do not change execute-bead lifecycle, result schema, or merge policy.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: execute-bead: serialize worker git commands and staged hook gates

2. Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
3. Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

2. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for an error wrapping `TrackerLockTimeoutError`.
3. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for the literal substring "tracker lock timeout" as a defense-in-depth fallback.
4. A unit test of `finalizeDurableAuditOrStop` injects a tracker-lock-timeout error and asserts: (a) `result.OperatorAttention` is NOT set, (b) the durable_audit_transient event is emitted, (c) the function returns false (don't stop).
