description:
PROBLEM: When two or more `ddx work` workers run concurrently against the
same project and worker B reaches `FinalizeDurableAudit` at the same moment
that worker A holds the tracker lock, worker B times out, and the loop
classifies the timeout as `operator_attention` and exits — even though
nothing requires operator action. The other worker just needs to finish.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain with two concurrent workers:
- Worker1 (pid 424293) was committing tracker state for niflheim-996afcc8.
- Worker2 (pid 681801), simultaneously finalizing niflheim-e43f04bb (an
  ADR-051 cold-tier PUT concurrency implementation that had already run to
  completion — 66091 tokens, 32m8.5s), hit `tracker lock timeout (max
  elapsed, lock: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock,
  owner pid: 424293)` while trying to commit durable audit outputs.
- Worker2 exited with `operator attention required`, releasing
  niflheim-e43f04bb back to `open` without committing the audit trail,
  and leaving these paths dirty: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/attachments`.

The work was successful — 32 minutes of agent time produced a clean
implementation and commit. The lock race nuked the audit attribution and
forced the worker to stop. Re-running it would either repeat the agent run
(burning more tokens) or hit `already_satisfied` (with the worktree-leak
documented in `ddx-274943fc`).

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1309`
(`finalizeDurableAuditOrStop`) calls `runtime.FinalizeDurableAudit` and
branches on `isTransientGitContention(err.Error())` to decide retry vs
operator-attention. The matcher at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1019-1034` covers `.git/index.lock`
(via `gitlock.IsIndexLockError`) and the strings "unable to write new index
file", "cannot lock ref", "unable to update the ref" — but **does not cover
the DDx-specific tracker-lock error format** emitted by
`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:243`:

  "tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)"

The tracker lock is a DDx-internal ordering lock around tracker writes
(`.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`), held briefly during tracker-state mutations.
Concurrent worker contention on this lock is structurally identical to
the `.git/index.lock` case the existing matcher handles, and the comment
on `isTransientGitContention` explicitly states the design intent:

  "A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying the
   bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain."

PROPOSED FIX: Extend `isTransientGitContention` to recognise the DDx
tracker-lock-timeout string class. Recommended implementation:

  1. Export a stable marker constant (or recogniser) from
     `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go` rather than string-matching
     across packages. Suggested: a sentinel error type
     `TrackerLockTimeoutError` wrapping the existing fields (lockDir,
     ownerPID), with an `Is(err)` helper.
  2. Update `FinalizeDurableAudit` and its callers to wrap the timeout
     error with this sentinel, so callers can use `errors.Is` instead of
     substring matching.
  3. In `isTransientGitContention` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1019`, add a
     branch that returns true for `errors.Is(err, TrackerLockTimeoutErr)`.
     Keep the existing substring fallbacks for defense in depth.
  4. Confirm via test that worker2 in the observed scenario survives the
     lock timeout, re-enters the loop, and successfully commits on a
     subsequent iteration when the lock releases.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing the tracker-lock retry policy itself (timeout duration, backoff
  curve) — only changing how the loop classifies the lock-timeout outcome.
- Changing concurrent-worker safety guarantees on tracker writes.
- Adding queue-level fairness or worker coordination (out of scope for
  this bead).
- Fixing the orphan-worktree leak that compounds this issue — tracked
  separately in `ddx-274943fc`.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (export sentinel error type)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (wrap timeout with sentinel where
  it bubbles up — verify by reading line 41 and 127)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (extend `isTransientGitContention`)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go (new test for tracker-lock
  classification)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package (already correct for .git/index.lock; do not modify)
- cli/cmd/work.go (exit-reason rendering unchanged)
- Any other isTransientGitContention call sites — only the durable-audit
  branch matters for this bug.

description:
PROBLEM

DDx has a live bead-store split-brain across bead commands, work planning, and targeted execution. On 2026-06-13 after installing 75d5c4e84, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx-provider-failure-20260613: `ddx work` executed and closed JSONL-backed bead ddx-81e3adcd, and `rg ddx-81e3adcd .ddx/beads.jsonl` finds it at .ddx/beads.jsonl:316, but `ddx bead show ddx-81e3adcd --json` returns `bead: not found`. Conversely, `ddx bead show ddx-b53eb508 --json` sees a newly filed bead, but `ddx try ddx-b53eb508` returns `bead not found`. Operators cannot trust queue state, cannot target priority work, and cannot force provider/process failure bugs to the top if read/write/execution paths observe different stores.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- cli/cmd/bead.go:291-322 resolves bead commands through CommandFactory.beadWorkspaceRoot() and beadStore(). In this worktree it resolves to a store that sees ddx-b53eb508 but not JSONL bead ddx-81e3adcd.
- cli/cmd/work_plan.go:82-91 uses f.beadStore() for planning, but live `ddx work plan --json` still listed JSONL-backed ddx-81e3adcd while `ddx bead show` could not inspect it.
- cli/cmd/try.go:177 constructs `bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot))`; live `ddx try ddx-b53eb508` failed with `bead not found` despite `ddx bead show ddx-b53eb508 --json` succeeding from the same cwd. Additional try call sites at cli/cmd/try.go:263, :384, :444, and :515 also construct stores separately.
- cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go:41-48 is the shared root helper, but commands are still mixing beadWorkspaceRoot(), resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot), and direct ddxroot.JoinProject-style paths elsewhere.

PROPOSED FIX

Centralize bead store resolution for bead show/list/ready/export/update/create, work plan/work picker, work status/focus, and try behind one project-aware resolver that returns both the selected state path and a diagnostic explaining why. Add regression coverage for a repository with both an in-tree .ddx JSONL store and an alternate configured/global store so show, ready, plan, work, and try all see the same bead IDs and lifecycle state. Ensure `ddx try <id>` can execute every bead visible in `ddx bead show <id>`, or fails with a typed store-mismatch diagnostic that includes both physical store paths.

NON-SCOPE

Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl. Do not remove support for alternate bead backends. Do not rewrite execute-bead history. Do not treat this as only a display bug; command behavior must converge.
notes: 2026-06-13 dogfood rerun of ddx-ef9df563 failed in ExecuteBeadWithConfig before dispatch: result.json reported 'loading bead ddx-ef9df563 from worktree snapshot: bead: not found'. The command-level store fix landed in e2012ddbd, but execute-bead runtime still did not receive/pass DDX_BEAD_DIR or canonical BeadStoreRoot into prepareArtifacts/loadBeadContext.
title: work: make bead show, plan, and try use one canonical store

acceptance:
1. A transient .git/index.lock contention failure during the durable-audit/tracker commit does NOT exit the worker: it is classified transient, the bead is released, and the drain loop continues. Verified by a new test (e.g. TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal in cli/internal/agent) that injects an index.lock failure on the tracker/audit commit and asserts the worker continues rather than returning the operator-attention exit.
2. A genuine, non-lock git failure during the audit commit still surfaces as operator attention (regression).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Under multi-worker operation, a ddx work worker EXITS with "operator attention required: DDx could not commit durable audit outputs; resolve the git failure before continuing autonomous work" when the durable-audit/tracker commit hits transient .git/index.lock contention. Observed 2026-05-27: with 2 ddx workers + a fizeau worker + the server sharing one project .git, a worker died on `git add` tracker -> ".git/index.lock: File exists ... no owner found but lock is fresh (age 19s < 30s)". A transient lock conflict stops the whole worker; the more workers, the more often this fires, directly defeating unattended steady-state drain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- git .git/index.lock carries no pid, so gitlock.IndexLockOwner finds no owner. RecoverGitIndexLock (cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:144-158) only reaps an ownerless lock once age >= StaleAge (30s, gitlock.go:26); below that it returns Removed=false ("no owner found but lock is fresh").
- RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery retries only RecoveryAttempts=3 (gitlock.go:30) with LiveOwnerWait=500ms (gitlock.go:34) ~= 1.5s total, far short of the 30s reap floor, then returns the index.lock error.
- commitTrackerLocked (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2153) calls runGitWithIndexLockRecovery for `git add`; its error becomes "staging tracker: ...". That propagates to the durable-audit-commit exit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1175, which stops the worker (operator attention) instead of treating transient lock contention as retryable.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Do NOT exit the drain on transient .git/index.lock contention during the durable-audit/tracker commit. Classify index.lock contention as transient (mirroring the pre-claim tracker-contention class from ddx-df77e668), release the bead, and continue the loop so the next iteration retries. A transient lock must never stop a worker.
2. AND/OR raise the recovery patience for tracker/audit commits to a bounded backoff that can outlast typical contention (up to ~the StaleAge window) before failing.
3. Optional: reap a provably-orphaned lock sooner (e.g. confirm no live git holds the repo via lsof) while keeping the 30s floor when liveness is unknown.
Recommend (1) as the primary fix: transient lock contention is retryable, not operator-attention-worthy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken safety against removing a live git process index.lock.
- Do not change the landing-path read-tree retry (already has backoff, ddx-7e659c95).

CROSS-REF: same multi-worker-resilience theme as ddx-57c40485 (lock scoping) and ddx-df77e668 (pre-claim tracker-contention). This is the post-fix gap on the COMMIT side.

Landed 48631b2ed; TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal + regression pass; full internal/agent gate green; reinstalled.

acceptance:
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
3. TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree proves watchdog reap follows the same SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL cleanup path and releases the claimed bead.
4. TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees proves server/manager shutdown cleans all server-owned worker process groups.
5. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup|TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree|TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent' passes.
7. make install is run after the code change lands.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A server-managed worker must be fully cleanable. Stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown must not leave ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 says WorkerManager manages in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, but no OS process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID and :1282-1286 terminates a process group only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 has the same limitation in watchdog reap. Because server-owned workers are often goroutines with PID zero, the server cannot guarantee child harness cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a server-managed process boundary. Preferred implementation: spawn a child ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group and record PID/PGID in WorkerRecord. If a transitional in-process path remains for tests, every harness child process group must be registered on the worker handle and killed through the same cleanup helper. Stop/reap/shutdown should send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, SIGKILL remaining descendants, release bead claims, persist terminal status, and be idempotent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

description:
PROBLEM
The drain-queue golden tests are failing because the workers page now exposes more than one heading named `Workers`, and the Playwright spec is using a non-unique role query. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` calls `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Workers' })`, but the page also renders `Reported workers`, which makes the selector ambiguous in strict mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` uses a heading locator that collides with the page’s section heading.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:1-120` and its rendered output define the page structure that exposes both the main page heading and the workers subsection heading.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the drain-queue test target the unique page heading or otherwise disambiguate the query so the golden flow can verify the worker dispatch and terminal-state assertions. Keep the fix confined to the drain e2e spec and any minimal accessibility label adjustment required to make the heading unique.

NON-SCOPE
- Worker dispatch semantics.
- GraphQL or backend worker state transitions.
- Other e2e specs.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Both drain-queue golden tests in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` pass.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Observed on 2026-06-29 in this repo while draining the queue. Worker pid 4014720 hit 4 consecutive 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes within ~2h on different beads (ddx-9aa2151d, ddx-95d80f7e, ddx-1c865bdb, ddx-aef568e7) without any successful merge. Load average was 38-52 throughout (8-core host). After each transport failure the worker advances to the next ready bead and the cycle repeats; closed counter unchanged across the period.

Specific log signature:
  do response complete in <N>m
  tracker_lock section=durable_audit wait=1ms hold=<seconds>
  ✗ ddx-<id> → transport: exit status 1

Some attempts had very short responses (1m37s) suggesting codex died early; others completed long (32m+) then died at durable_audit. Common factor: the provider subprocess exits status 1, which DDx classifies as 'transport' (not tests_red, not no_changes, not timeout).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The outcome classifier maps any non-zero provider exit (codex/claude/etc.) to 'transport: exit status 1' regardless of cause (OOM, kill, network error mid-stream). Inspect cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around the outcome-mapping path for the codex/provider exit code (search 'transport: exit status'). The classifier does not distinguish 'load-killed' from 'genuine bug' from 'provider API error'.
- Under high system load (load avg > ~3-5x CPU count), the provider subprocess gets starved during the durable_audit / land phase (which is git+disk heavy), bumps into kernel OOM-killer or hits its own internal heartbeat/timeout, and exits non-zero. DDx then walks the queue trying the next bead which also dies, burning context+tokens with zero forward progress.
- There is no backoff/cooldown when transport failures cluster (e.g., 3+ in N minutes).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add load-pressure detection: before each claim, sample /proc/loadavg and compare to runtime.NumCPU(); if 5-min loadavg > k * cores (k=2-3 reasonable), emit a structured backoff observation and sleep proportional to overload before claiming.
2. Add transport-failure cooldown: when N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes occur within the last M minutes, the worker should pause for a longer interval (e.g., 5-15 min) before next claim instead of immediately consuming another bead.
3. Capture and surface the provider subprocess's exit reason where possible: check WaitStatus for signal-killed (SIGKILL → OOM-likely, SIGTERM → graceful), and distinguish in the outcome string (e.g., 'transport: provider killed by SIGKILL (likely OOM)').

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (outcome classifier + claim-loop pacing)
- cli/cmd/work.go (worker pacing/backoff)
- cli/internal/workerstatus/ (if load sampling needs a place)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not change the queue ordering.
- Do not change the durable_audit commit flow itself.
- Do not implement a general-purpose host load manager.

AC
1. New helper LoadPressureSnapshot() in cli/internal/workerstatus or cli/internal/agent returning normalized loadavg/cores; unit test covers >threshold and <threshold paths.
2. Worker pre-claim path checks the snapshot; when pressure exceeds k (default 2.5), logs a structured 'load_pressure_backoff' observation and sleeps before claim. Unit test asserts the path is invoked with k configurable.
3. Worker tracks last N (≥3) 'transport: exit status 1' outcomes by wall-clock timestamp; when 3 occur within 10m, the next claim is delayed by an extended cooldown (>=5m). Unit test simulates 3 failures within window and asserts the delay.
4. Outcome classifier distinguishes signal-killed (SIGKILL/SIGTERM) provider exits in the outcome string. Unit test asserts the distinct outcomes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/workerstatus/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

description: codex/claude blocking: failed and (later) local attempts must run in isolated git worktrees with a defined cleanup/revert contract; the worker must NOT drain a dirty/WIP repo (helix churned on this). PROBLEM: lifecycle dispatch rejects dirty roots (correct) but there is no clean isolation/cleanup contract for failed-then-escalated attempts, and dirty WIP repos churn the queue. PROPOSED: ensure every attempt (incl. escalation retries) runs in an isolated worktree; on failure, revert/clean the worktree before the next attempt; never operate on a dirty canonical checkout; define artifact retention. This must land BEFORE escalation + local mixin. cross-ref FEAT-004 addendum.

acceptance:
1. 5 fixture beads chosen and listed in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/comparison-fixtures.md with: bead ID, base-rev, original closing commit, characterization (trivial/refactor/test/multi-file/docs).
2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.
5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.
6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.
7. Hypothesis verdict section: H1 / H2 / both / inconclusive.
8. Recommendations section: which path for which bead-shape.
9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.
10. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).
description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.

description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-43b6b5fe is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 4 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode m
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section documents global vs project install and references ddx-937de9fc.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/registry/... green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. AGENTS.md contains a Default Interactive Mode section before Bead Policy that routes broad DDx questions to queue_steward and states that execution-ready beads belong to ddx work unless the user explicitly asks for direct implementation.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines queue_steward, bead_execution, direct_user_implementation, and review modes, including DDX_MODE names, precedence, allowed actions, prohibited actions, and worker-capacity suggestion rules.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md defines how the ddx skill routes broad interactive prompts versus explicit ddx work/try/bead execution prompts.
4. Add or update TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract to assert the guidance names all four modes and includes the ddx work carve-out.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract -count=1 passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.
title: agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes

description:
PROBLEM
A 2026-06-19 doc/impl alignment audit surfaced several internal inconsistencies in docs/helix that are pure documentation defects (no code change). They are bundled here because each is a small, independently verifiable doc edit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
1. Triplicate TDs: docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-035-artifact-search.md, TD-038-artifact-search.md, and TD-039-artifact-search.md are three near-identical "Artifact Search Semantics" designs (same title, same depends_on [TD-026, TD-029]), all produced by the closed bead ddx-23cbcb4b from non-idempotent execution attempts each grabbing a fresh TD number. None is cross-referenced elsewhere.
2. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:11 has a self-contradictory status: '**Status:** Proposed (Accepted after operator review)'.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:18 cites the upstream contract as CONTRACT-003-fizeau-service.md, while audit docs (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md, AR-2026-05-02-fictional-upstream-deps.md) name it CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md.
4. FEAT status vocabulary across the 26 docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-*.md is internally inconsistent: 'Frame' vs 'Framing' (two spellings of one state) and 'Complete' vs 'Implemented' (used interchangeably).
5. A TD-030 design doc cites '/tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md' as load-bearing background, violating CLAUDE.md (no /tmp/ plan citations; they do not survive between machines).

PROPOSED FIX
Make each edit; no code changes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the canonical FEAT Status enum to match HELIX's [Draft|Specified|Approved] (DDx intentionally tracks build progress); only make the existing vocabulary self-consistent. Do not touch implementation.
title: docs: reconcile spec inconsistencies surfaced by 2026-06-19 doc/impl alignment audit

acceptance:
1. Add TestWorkerSet_RetriesDuringRestartListenerReadiness covering cli/cmd/worker.go: it simulates a local server URL that refuses the first connection or two during restart, then accepts PUT /api/agent/workers/desired, and asserts `ddx worker set` succeeds without operator retry.
2. Add TestWorkerSet_ReadinessRetryBudgetExpires or equivalent coverage asserting persistent connection refused returns a clear error naming the local server readiness timeout.
3. If the fix moves server address publication instead of client retry, add or update a server test proving `server.addr` is not advertised as ready until the listener is accepting connections.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Immediately after `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` on 2026-06-16, `ddx worker set --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx --count 1 --restart` failed with `server request: Put "https://127.0.0.1:7743/api/agent/workers/desired": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:7743: connect: connection refused`. Retrying after the listener accepted requests succeeded. Operators should be able to issue worker control commands immediately after a managed service restart without a manual sleep/retry loop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/server.go:351-363 writes `server.addr`, calls `reconcileDesiredWorkersAfterStartup`, and only then enters `http.ListenAndServeTLS`. During that window clients can resolve the restarted server URL but the listener is not accepting connections yet.
- cli/cmd/worker.go:103-110 sends a single PUT to `/api/agent/workers/desired` and returns the raw connection error without bounded readiness retry when the recorded server is alive but not yet listening.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:373-382 intentionally delays startup reconcile passes, so service startup is already asynchronous; the worker-control CLI needs a matching readiness contract or the server needs to write/advertise readiness only after bind.

PROPOSED FIX
Make server restart readiness deterministic for worker-control commands. Either bind/listen before writing the advertised ready address, or add a bounded retry/readiness wait in worker control for connection-refused failures against the local server immediately after restart. Keep failures fast for non-local, TLS/auth, or persistent server-down cases, and surface an actionable error if the readiness budget expires.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change desired worker reconciliation semantics, worker count caps, or GraphQL worker mutations. Do not mask real HTTP 4xx/5xx responses. Do not alter startup desired-worker restore behavior except as required to make listener readiness observable.
title: server: tolerate listener readiness after restart

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.

1. `README.md` includes a section for pre-claim intake and silent-idle diagnosis that documents the canonical verdict forms, warn vs operator-attention vs hard-error mapping, and operator commands/logs to inspect when a ready queue idles.
2. `TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract` asserts the README mentions `readiness_checks`, bool/string/null-or-absent verdict handling, `loop.operator_attention`, same-fingerprint or repeated idle escalation, and claim-success-rate warning knobs.
3. The docs preserve the parent cross-references to AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context, ddx-57c40485, and ddx-8f2e0ebf as related reliability context without making them part of this implementation scope.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestReadmeDocumentsPreClaimIntakeContract` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators lack a concise, durable guide for diagnosing a `ddx work --watch` loop that appears alive but does not claim ready beads. The parent incident's suggested action pointed to route/harness configuration even though the actionable fault was DDx's readiness decoder/worker behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The user-facing README is the natural top-level diagnostic surface, but the pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle escalation behavior need to be stated there near the quick-start operational commands in `README.md:40-45`. Worker behavior is implemented in the readiness classification and work loop, but operators should not need to read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` to know what warn-only, operator-attention, and hard errors mean.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the README to name the `readiness_checks` contract, canonical verdict forms, failure-class mapping, same-fingerprint/operator-attention behavior, claim-success-rate warning knobs, and first diagnostic commands/logs for a full ready queue that is idling. Keep the guidance implementation-neutral enough to remain valid as the specific worker internals evolve.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change runtime behavior, decoder behavior, schema validation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not add long incident narrative or depend on the external Fizeau log path. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
Depends on the decoder, same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warning, and schema-lock children so the docs describe landed behavior.
title: Document pre-claim intake contract and silent-idle diagnosis

2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadLifecycle.Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error while preserving Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error.
3. All Go callsites for Store.Create, Store.Update, and Store.Close pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
4. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(Create|Update|Close)_RejectsCanceledContext' passes.

description:
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.
title: bead store: migrate mutation APIs to ctx-first signatures

description:
PROBLEM
The unattended ddx work drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on next pre-claim found .crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. A stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (added by ddx-df77e668). Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are not covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (implementer/operator picks)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink materialized into a dir whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink). RECOMMENDED.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills), analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these paths (currently committed as symlinks; deliberate structural change).

NON-SCOPE: do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits; do not change the external skill tooling.
CROSS-REF: extends the tracker-file allowlist from ddx-df77e668.
title: execute-bead: unattended drain halts on tool-managed skill-symlink churn (.crush/skills/*); make pre-claim resilient

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM: The ddx prose checker requires an EXACT Vale version string and neither accepts a compatible range nor installs Vale itself. Operators without precisely that build are hard-blocked, with no bootstrap.
ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/docprose/vale_runner.go:13 SupportedValeVersion="vale version 3.13.0"; cli/cmd/doctor.go:24 const supportedValeVersion + :631 errors when version != supportedValeVersion (exact-string equality); vale_runner.go:62 exec.LookPath("vale") only — no install/bootstrap. No version-range tolerance and no self-install.
PROPOSED FIX: either (a) accept a documented compatible version range (e.g. >= 3.13.0 within the 3.x line) instead of exact-string equality, or (b) bootstrap the pinned Vale (download the pinned release into a ddx-managed cache dir and invoke that) so the check is self-sufficient on a clean machine. Prefer (b) with (a) as fallback.
NON-SCOPE: changing Vale styles/rules.
title: doctor/prose: Vale check requires exact pinned version and self-installs nothing — make it flexible or bootstrap Vale

acceptance:
1. `TestCleanupCommand_JSONIncludesProcessFindings` proves `ddx cleanup --json` includes process findings/counts and still exposes the stale temp dir, run-state, and scratch fields that already existed.
2. `TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsProcessFindingsInText` (or equivalent) proves dry-run text includes process findings/counts and still prints the existing stale temp dir/run-state/scratch summary lines.
3. The existing cleanup command regression tests, especially `TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting` and `TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape`, continue to pass with the widened output contract.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'CleanupCommand|Cleanup.*Process|AttemptDescendant' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes and `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx cleanup` can already report stale temp dirs, run-state files, scratch dirs, and evidence retention, but it has no way to show stale attempt-descendant process findings to the operator. A dry-run can therefore look clean while the host still has DDx-owned CPU/port pressure.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/cleanup.go:11-30` defines `cleanupCommandReport` with only file/resource counters, warnings, blocked errors, and observations; there is no process field in the JSON/text contract.
`cli/cmd/cleanup.go:69-72` constructs `agent.NewExecutionCleanupManager` and runs it, but `runCleanup` only copies the current file/resource summary fields into the report and prints those fields in text output.
`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` currently exposes file/resource counts only, so the CLI has nothing process-related to forward.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend `cleanupCommandReport` with process counts and structured process findings from the cleanup summary, keeping the existing resource counters intact.
- Update `runCleanup` so both `--json` and the text report include process findings/counts without dropping stale temp dir, run-state, scratch, or evidence fields.
- Keep dry-run semantics unchanged: the report should show what `--apply` would terminate, but dry-run must not kill anything.
- Add focused command tests that assert the widened JSON/text shape while preserving the current resource-reporting assertions.

NON-SCOPE
- Process detection/reaping logic itself lives in the sibling `agent` bead.
- Startup/pre-claim cleanup wiring lives in the sibling `work startup` bead.
- Documentation updates in `docs/` are handled separately.
- No change to the command name, flag set, or non-cleanup subcommands.
title: cmd: surface cleanup process findings in JSON and text

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
2. The regression shows the worker preserves or reconciles land state instead of rerunning implementation when the land result is already effectively landed or blocked by dirty staged evidence.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'LandCoordination' -count=1` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Landing failures that are really ref-lock, already-landed, or staged-evidence coordination issues still look like ordinary implementation failures to the worker. That causes fresh implementation reruns and the wrong retry lane instead of reconciliation or operator attention.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:292-312` wraps every `LandingAdvancer` error as a generic `land advancer` failure, which loses whether the candidate was already landed, whether `refs/heads/main` could not be locked, or whether the landing worktree was dirty. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report_test.go:57-62` only proves the current detail string records land failure; it does not distinguish the coordination class from implementation failure.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures into explicit retry actions: reconcile, retry-land, or operator-attention.
- If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and continue without rerunning implementation.
- If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, preserve or retry the land/evidence step according to local policy rather than escalating the model.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating, decomposition detection, or power escalation policy in this child.
- Do not reimplement low-level tracker/index contention fixes owned by sibling work.
title: agent: classify land coordination failures as reconciliation

description:
PROBLEM
CLAUDE.md:211-221 already describes the persona system and plugin/persona precedence, and library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md:21-54 already describes install locations, but neither file explicitly ties the install-topology language back to ddx-937de9fc. The top-level library/README.md overview is also missing.

ROOT CAUSE
The install precedence story is split across the agent-facing guide and the skill doc, while the repo lacks a top-level library README that summarizes the project/global/baked-in topology in one place.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the Persona System section in CLAUDE.md, update the ddx skill doc's "How this skill works" section, and add library/README.md as the install-topology overview. Keep the text aligned with the project > global > baked-in precedence already used elsewhere.

NON-SCOPE
No behavior changes to install resolution or the underlying plugin filesystem layout.
title: Update install topology docs and add library README

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:dep, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.
title: D: Dependencies (ctx) + Queue helpers + Operations + remove 3 *Store queue methods (TD-027 §6, §14)

At least one child build bead is created with command-based ACs and wired via ddx bead dep add <child> <this-epic>. 3. ddx bead dep tree <this-epic> shows the children.
description:
PROBLEM
When a bead in one repo is blocked on work in ANOTHER repo (e.g. niflheim beads blocked on heimq), operators hand-carry 'SKIP these heimq-blocked beads' as prose across sessions and compactions. They wanted to mark them blocked but had to fall back to priority-4 + notes.

CURRENT STATE (from adversarial review — gap is NARROWER than first thought)
- An external-blocker primitive ALREADY exists: cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go has StatusBlocked + a required ExternalBlockerReason, a LifecycleBucketBlockedExternal bucket, an ExternalBlocked() store query (store.go), and a manual recheck workflow (operator reopens when cleared).
- The RESIDUAL gap: the blocker reason is FREE TEXT with MANUAL reopen. There is no STRUCTURED cross-repo reference (<repo>#<bead>) and no AUTOMATED recheck.

WHAT TO PRODUCE (design, not implementation)
- A short design doc specifying: a structured cross-repo blocker reference field (repo + bead id) layered on the existing ExternalBlockerReason, and an automated recheck that flips the bead back to open when the referenced bead closes. Cover: how the recheck observes another repo's tracker (the referenced repo may be local or remote), failure modes, and backward compatibility with free-text reasons.
- Decompose into child build beads with command-based ACs depending on this epic.

NON-SCOPE (for THIS bead)
- No implementation in this bead — design + child beads only.
- Do not replace the existing external-blocker primitive; build ON it.
- No new lifecycle statuses.
title: DESIGN: structured cross-repo blocker ref + auto-recheck

2. TestServiceFinalFailureClassifiesProviderRouteHealth uses fake final events for provider timeout, quota/rate-limit, model unavailable, and harness unavailable; each is normalized into the provider failure taxonomy with retryable vs hard-pin semantics preserved.
3. TestUnpinnedWorkerFallsBackAfterTypedProviderFailure proves a zero-config/unpinned worker records failed-route evidence and asks Fizeau for a different eligible route or higher floor rather than parking the bead on the first provider failure.
4. TestPinnedWorkerReportsHardPinExhaustedWithoutWidening proves --harness/--provider/--model pins do not get silently removed, and the report names the typed provider failure plus operator remediation.
5. TestProviderFailureEvidenceNamesFallbackDecision proves the bead/run evidence includes requested constraints, resolved route if any, typed failure, retryability, fallback_attempted, and fallback_stop_reason.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestServiceExecuteErrorClassifiesProviderConfigFailures|TestServiceFinalFailureClassifiesProviderRouteHealth|TestUnpinnedWorkerFallsBackAfterTypedProviderFailure|TestPinnedWorkerReportsHardPinExhaustedWithoutWidening|TestProviderFailureEvidenceNamesFallbackDecision' passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx/FIZ worker failures can still surface as underspecified execution failures when provider availability, model availability, credentials, endpoint reachability, or harness configuration differs across machines. DDx is the abstraction that should let unattended workers continue through provider variance; a generic provider failure that gives no typed cause, no fallback action, and no durable route-health evidence is a product bug.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:287-290 returns pre-dispatch Fizeau Execute errors as a generic `agent: execute: %w`, leaving the execute loop without a typed provider/model/configuration failure contract.
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:332-376 copies final service error text into Result.Error/ExitCode, but downstream classification still depends on free-text heuristics rather than a required typed route-health outcome for provider unavailable, model unavailable, auth missing, quota/rate limit, endpoint timeout, or harness misconfiguration.
cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:516-544 and :588-612 contain the unpinned route-exclusion/escalation path, but that path only works when the failure is already recognized as provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider; untyped provider/configuration failures can stop or preserve the bead without proving fallback was attempted or intentionally impossible.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define a typed provider failure taxonomy at the DDx/Fizeau boundary: provider_connectivity, provider_auth, provider_rate_limit, provider_quota, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_config_invalid, no_viable_provider, and unknown_provider_failure.
- Normalize both pre-dispatch Execute errors and final failed events into that taxonomy before ExecuteBeadReport construction.
- For unpinned workers, convert retryable provider/model/harness availability failures into route-health evidence and continue fallback/escalation without parking the bead.
- For explicit operator pins, fail loudly with a typed hard-pin exhausted/operator-action report that names the pin and available remediation, without silently widening pins.
- Persist durable evidence on the bead/run showing requested constraints, resolved route if any, typed failure, retryability, whether fallback was attempted, and why fallback stopped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not move concrete route selection into DDx; Fizeau still owns routing, aliases, model discovery, and provider catalog behavior. Do not remove explicit operator pins. Do not call real providers in tests.
title: agent: type provider failures and enforce fallback semantics

description:
PROBLEM
The execution/runtime packages still build project-state paths with literal `.ddx` joins, so run-state, evidence, worker liveness, and exec attachments will not follow convention-mode roots even after the CLI layer is fixed.

ROOT CAUSE
Representative direct joins remain in `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:57`, `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:216`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1790`, `cli/internal/exec/store.go:54`, `cli/internal/exec/bead_runtime.go:421`, `cli/internal/workerstatus/liveness.go:42`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:667`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go:235`, and `cli/internal/persona/loader.go:77`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add package-local helpers that route runtime/project-state paths through `ddxroot.Path` for `internal/agent`, `internal/exec`, `internal/persona`, `internal/workerstatus`, `internal/processmetrics`, and `internal/agentmetrics`.
- Replace the remaining literal `.ddx` joins in those packages.
- Add focused tests proving run-state/evidence and exec attachment paths resolve against the DDx root chosen by `ddxroot.Path`.

NON-SCOPE
- `cli/cmd`, config/git, and server/graphql packages.
- Test-only files and the final full-tree hook gate.

DEPS
- No deps.
title: ddx home: route agent and exec state paths through ddxroot

description:
PROBLEM
Spec saves from the GraphQL API can still overwrite a document when the caller's optimistic concurrency token is stale, and the current mutation schema exposes only path and content. The resolver does rebuild the graph after writes, but there is no explicit stale-write gate or conflict response before the filesystem is mutated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 defines DocumentWrite with only path and content and writes directly with os.WriteFile.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without an expected hash/version input.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:62-89 rebuilds the graph after the write, but only after the filesystem has already been changed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an expected hash/version argument to documentWrite in schema.graphql and thread it into resolver_mutation_docs.go.
- Compare the caller's expected token against the current document before os.WriteFile; if it mismatches, return a conflict instead of overwriting.
- Keep ResolveDocumentPath as the confinement gate for absolute paths, traversal, and symlink escapes.
- Preserve the post-save graph rebuild and return the refreshed document/graph state after a successful write.
- Add focused resolver tests for stale-save refusal, confinement refusal, and graph refresh.

NON-SCOPE
- Hub-to-spoke forwarding is handled by sibling bead ddx-aa88732d's forwarding child and is not part of this child.
- Playwright spec-editing coverage stays with ddx-e951255c.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql
- cli/internal/server/graphql_mutation_docs_test.go

Out-of-scope (file a separate bead):
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts
title: server: add stale-safe documentWrite version checks and graph refresh

2. Human-readable output has separate sections for Requires human, Blocked/planning, Worker-ready summary, Worker recommendation, and Unknowns; ready implementation beads are not listed as primary intervention work by default.
3. --json returns stable keys human_required, blocked_or_planning, ready_summary, worker_recommendation, and unknowns.
4. Worker recommendation logic is conservative: it can suggest another worker when ready depth is high and active/in-progress capacity is observable, and it reports unknown capacity instead of inventing a blocker when signals are absent.

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92.
title: work focus: expose human-required queue intervention

acceptance: 1. A Go test in cli/internal/agent/ reproduces an attempt whose candidate commit would be rewritten after the first commit and asserts it is prevented or reconciled (candidate preserved), NOT discarded-and-rerun. 2. description:
PROBLEM
Agents keep tripping the execute-bead one-commit/no-amend contract (the implementation commit is rewritten/amended/rebased after the first commit). Today this is DETECTED post-hoc and the whole attempt is discarded and re-run, wasting long, expensive runs (observed 3047s / 15.3M-token attempts thrown away).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (divergence/ancestry resolution, ~line 187 and ~910-927): a diverged/rewritten candidate is resolved by aborting the claim (detect-then-fail), not by preventing or correcting the rewrite.

PROPOSED FIX
- Prevent or auto-correct the contract violation rather than discarding the attempt: e.g. detect a post-first-commit rewrite before land and reconcile it (or instruct/guard the harness to not amend) so a successful candidate is preserved instead of re-run from scratch.
- This is DISTINCT from the mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale detector in execute_bead.go:~1419 (commit+rationale mismatch). Do NOT target that path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not relax the audit-trail rule (never squash/rebase/filter landed attempt history).
- Do not touch the mixed_commit detector.
- Do not change Fizeau routing.

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
title: Make execute-bead auto-comply with the one-commit/no-amend contract

description:
PROBLEM
After syncing main at afc8b6408 and reinstalling ddx, the focused server worker sanity command fails in four existing tests. Server-managed worker supervision cannot safely land while the worker-manager baseline is red.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The failing tests are cli/internal/server/workers_resolve_route_test.go:152 TestWorkerExecutionDoesNotCallResolveRouteForPinnedProfileOrModel, cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:799 TestWorkerLiveCounters, cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:890 TestWorkerLandsCommitViaCoordinator, and cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:1012 TestWorkerLandsEvidenceViaCoordinator. cli/internal/server/workers.go:361 starts the worker goroutine path and cli/internal/server/workers.go:718 runs the worker; current behavior no longer reaches Execute promptly, no longer updates live counters before finish, and no longer advances the landing repository/evidence as those tests require.

PROPOSED FIX
Diagnose and repair the worker test regression without weakening the assertions. Keep the tests as executable contracts for route bypass, live counters, coordinator landing, and evidence landing. If the failure is environmental, isolate the test fixture so it is deterministic.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement desired-state supervision in this bead. Do not delete or relax the four named tests. Do not touch unrelated frontend files currently dirty in the operator worktree.

description:
PROBLEM
When readiness honors a prior operator promotion and skips a downgrade, the intake.blocked entry it writes is indistinguishable from any other warning. Operators cannot tell that readiness deliberately deferred to a prior acceptance.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 constructs the intake.blocked entry without an operator_override boolean; the sibling-guard branch that detected operator promotion does not propagate that signal into the appended entry.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the intake.blocked entry schema with an operator_override boolean. Plumb the sibling-guard's operator-promotion determination into the entry construction site so operator_override=true is written when readiness skipped a downgrade because the bead was operator-promoted, and false otherwise. Consume the operator-promotion detection function provided by the sibling implementation bead.

NON-SCOPE
- rule_fingerprint field and dedup (sibling child bead).
- `ddx bead show` rendering (sibling child bead).
- Adding new readiness rules or changing rule evaluation logic.
- Changing the readiness terminal flow itself.
- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text.
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on the sibling implementation bead for the operator-promotion detection function it consumes.
title: bead-lifecycle: add operator_override signal to intake.blocked entries
