description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `work` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.
notes: Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. This plan retains ddx agent compatibility aliases and legacy cli/internal/agent package targets, so it must not dispatch.
title: agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature

acceptance:
1. TestBeadShowSurfacesFingerprint asserts `ddx bead show` output for a bead with intake.blocked entries renders the rule_fingerprint for each entry.
2. TestBeadShowSurfacesOperatorOverride asserts `ddx bead show` output renders operator_override for each intake.blocked entry, distinguishing true and false.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passing.
description:
PROBLEM
Even once intake.blocked entries carry rule_fingerprint and operator_override, operators cannot see those fields because `ddx bead show` does not render them in the intake.blocked list section.

ROOT CAUSE
- The intake.blocked rendering path in `ddx bead show` (cli/cmd/bead_show.go and its formatter helpers) prints only outcome and detail strings per entry; it does not read or print rule_fingerprint or operator_override.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the `ddx bead show` formatter for intake.blocked entries to read and render rule_fingerprint and operator_override per entry. Keep the rendering compact and stable so existing snapshot/golden tests can be updated deterministically.

NON-SCOPE
- Defining or computing rule_fingerprint (sibling child bead).
- Defining or propagating operator_override (sibling child bead).
- Adding new readiness rules or changing rule evaluation logic.
- Changing the readiness terminal flow itself.
- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text.
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on both sibling child beads (fingerprint+dedup, operator_override) so the fields exist on entries to render.
title: bead-lifecycle: surface rule_fingerprint and operator_override in `ddx bead show`

acceptance:
1. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_WorkerStartStopRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued start/stop for a spoke project creates/stops exactly one spoke-local worker and no hub-local phantom worker.
2. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_BeadMutationRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued bead create/edit for a spoke project mutates the spoke project and not the hub project.
3. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_DocumentWriteRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves hub-issued `documentWrite` for a spoke `docs/helix/**` path writes only under the spoke root and preserves/returns forwarding metadata where available.
4. At least one live write path includes an offline-spoke refusal assertion with no phantom hub-local row.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/... ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... -run 'Federation|FanOut|RouteMutation|ForwardMutation|Spoke' -count=1` passes.\n6. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` passes, or the new sibling e2e file named in this bead passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The federation suite proves hub/spoke registration, federated reads, offline badges, restart recovery, and mocked UI mutation states, but it does not yet prove the most important live owner-targeted write contract: commands issued from a hub for a spoke-owned project must execute on the spoke and must not create hub-local phantom state.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:34-39 says FEAT-026/ADR-007 require owner-targeted federation writes and that hub/spoke writes must never broadcast.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarding for `startWorker`, `stopWorker`, bead mutations, and `documentWrite`, with the hub never creating local fallback state for a spoke write.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:335-337 requires spoke worker start/stop forwarding and offline refusal.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:350 and :363 require federated bead and document writes.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307 currently exercises live two-node reads/offline/restart but not live owner-targeted write persistence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the live multi-process federation coverage, preferably in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or a sibling e2e file, to start one hub and one spoke with isolated temp roots and per-node XDG state.
- From the hub, issue a worker start/stop for a spoke project and assert the worker exists in the spoke project worker registry, not the hub registry.
- From the hub, create or edit a bead in the spoke project and assert the spoke `.ddx/beads.jsonl` changes while the hub project does not receive a phantom local bead.
- From the hub, write a `docs/helix/**` document in the spoke project and assert the file changes on the spoke root only and origin/forwarding metadata is observable where the API exposes it.
- Include an offline-spoke refusal assertion for at least one write path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add real ts-net/MagicDNS coverage; loopback hub/spoke is the autonomous local gate.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028; this bead covers FEAT-026 inbound spoke owner writes only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. Depends on the stale-bead reconciliation bead so the implementation target is narrowed before coding.
title: federation: prove live owner-targeted writes route to spoke

acceptance:
1. TestBeadAutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock (or equivalent) runs two or more concurrent bead update/create operations with auto_commit=always against the same repo and proves all complete without .git/index.lock errors.
2. The same regression asserts commit history is audit-correct: each auto-commit either contains only the operation named in its subject or is an explicitly named combined commit that lists every included tracker mutation.
3. Existing stale-lock recovery tests TestBeadCreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, TestBeadUpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, and TestBeadCloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBead(AutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock|CreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|UpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|CloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock)' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/gitlock/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, a worker executing ddx-bdc0065e cancelled several stale beads. Multiple ddx bead update commands ran close together from the execute-bead context. The tracker ended clean, but the auto-commit audit trail was noisy: one commit subject said it updated ddx-387a0178 while it also picked up additional bead-row changes, another update failed with .git/index.lock, and another commit reported nothing to commit. That makes tracker history misleading and is the same class of dirty/index-lock noise the operator wants eliminated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:134-154 builds bead auto-commit config and calls bead.AutoCommitFilesWithRecovery directly. cli/internal/bead/git_autocommit.go:10-17 wraps git add/commit with gitlock.RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery, but that only recovers from stale/transient .git/index.lock; it does not serialize the whole add/commit pair under the process-shared main git lock. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:98-118 documents and implements withMainGitLock/withTrackerLock for primary-checkout git writes, and cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:22-24 says DDx writers serialize through that lock, but the bead CLI auto-commit path is outside it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route bead CLI auto-commit add/commit operations through the same process-shared main git lock used by landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
- Preserve existing stale index.lock recovery inside the lock, so stale locks are still repaired and live owners still fail or wait according to policy.
- Add a concurrent regression where multiple bead update/create auto-commits run against one repo and produce either deterministic serialized commits or one explicitly combined commit; no index.lock failures, no misleading commit subjects that omit paths actually included.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change .ddx/beads.jsonl format.
- Do not remove auto-commit recovery for stale index locks.
- Do not squash, amend, or rewrite existing execute-bead history.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: bead: serialize auto-commit tracker git writes

description:
PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.

status=proposed
reason=bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885): 1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead"; "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.") and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead." 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149

   And in archive.go:

   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external

   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs

   That build covers every package in the module, including all the

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.

       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,

     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:

         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that

Children 1-3 relax it only for callsites of the methods each child migrates; Child 4 preserves it completely."},{"parent_ac":"NON-SCOPE: Event method ctx threading (AppendEvent, Events, EventsByKind).","coverage":"Preserved in every child NON-SCOPE section."},{"parent_ac":"NON-SCOPE: Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.","coverage":"Preserved in every child NON-SCOPE section."},{"parent_ac":"Governing artifact reference: TD-027 / spec:TD-027.","coverage":"Preserved in every child GOVERNING ARTIFACT section and labels array."},{"parent_ac":"Dependency/reference ID: ddx-464feba2.","coverage":"Preserved in every child PARENT/DEPS section as the parent/split source."}],"child_ids":["ddx-3e8ba14b","ddx-cc298105","ddx-38801c65","ddx-c887a63f"],"rationale":"The failed attempt showed that changing Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures while forbidding caller-package edits cannot satisfy lefthook, because Go has no overloads and existing zero-argument or legacy-argument callsites stop compiling. Each method-family child can update only the callsites for its own method group, keep tests local to cli/internal/bead, run the parent verification gates, and avoid the repo-wide sweep that made ddx-464feba2 too large. The final child restores the original local-only constraint by proving the completed foundation contract after the callsite migrations have landed."}

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/ -timeout=180s -count=1 -run='^(TestListProviders|TestShowProvider|TestProviderUnknownStateContract|TestMCPProvider)' ` completes under the timeout (previously hung indefinitely on TestListProviders alone).
2. `internal/agent/serviceconfig.go` exports `NewServiceFromWorkDirCtx(ctx context.Context, workDir string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error)` that sets `agentlib.ServiceOptions{QuotaRefreshContext: ctx}` so fizeau-spawned goroutines terminate when ctx is cancelled.
3. All callsites in `internal/server/` and `internal/server/graphql/` that previously called `agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir` / `agent.NewStatusProbeServiceFromWorkDir` / `agentlib.New(agentlib.ServiceOptions{})` for ad-hoc service construction are migrated to ctx-aware variants. Verified by `grep -rn 'NewServiceFromWorkDir\b\|agentlib.New(agentlib.ServiceOptions{})' cli/internal/server/` returning only the test-stub callsite and doc-comments.
4. `cli/internal/server/providers.go` adds a `providerHandlerTimeout = 10 * time.Second` and wraps `svc.RouteStatus` / `svc.ListHarnesses` calls in a detached-goroutine + ctx.Done() race so a slow / hung pi subprocess does not block the handler beyond the bound.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -timeout=180s -count=1` green on an unloaded system (suite completed in 224s under loadavg 37 locally, vs hang at 600s previously).
6. `cd cli && go vet ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` clean.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: `go test ./internal/server/...` hits the 600s test timeout. The goroutine dump shows hundreds of leaked fizeau aliveness / quota-refresh / refresh-scheduler goroutines waiting in `select { <-ctx.Done() }`, and the test driver goroutine itself is stuck in `svc.RouteStatus(ctx)` → `subprocessHarnessAutoRoutingModels` → `pi --list-models` subprocess wait. Under CI / loaded systems the synchronous subprocess wait inside `RouteStatus` exceeds any reasonable per-test budget, compounding with the goroutine accumulation.

ROOT CAUSE:
1. `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:88` (`NewServiceFromWorkDir`) and `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:96` (`NewStatusProbeServiceFromWorkDir`) construct the fizeau service with `agentlib.ServiceOptions{}` — leaving `QuotaRefreshContext` as `context.Background()`. Inside fizeau (`service.go:1079`), this drives `startAlivenessProbeLoop`, `startQuotaRecoveryProbeLoop`, `startPrimaryQuotaRefreshWorker`, and `refreshScheduler.Start(startupCtx)`, all of which spawn goroutines that only terminate on ctx cancel. Every short-lived HTTP / MCP / GraphQL request that constructs a fresh service leaks ~6 goroutines for the lifetime of the test binary.
2. `cli/internal/server/providers.go:108`, `providers.go:113`, `providers.go:130`, `providers.go:142`, `providers.go:391`, `providers.go:411` — `listHarnessInfos` and `liveRouteStatusReport` use the bare-`Background` factory. `svc.RouteStatus(ctx)` internally invokes `pi.DefaultModelSnapshot` at `fizeau@v0.14.33/internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go:352`, which hard-codes `context.Background()` when shelling out to `pi --list-models` — meaning the caller's ctx deadline does not propagate.
3. Same pattern leaks at `cli/internal/server/agent_models.go:38`, `agent_models.go:138`, `cli/internal/server/workers.go:853`, `workers.go:868`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:83`, `resolver_providers.go:99`, `resolver_providers.go:193`, `resolver_providers.go:309`, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_provider_models.go:176`.

PROPOSED FIX:
- Add `agent.NewServiceFromWorkDirCtx(ctx, workDir)` and `agent.NewStatusProbeServiceFromWorkDirCtx(ctx, workDir)` in `internal/agent/serviceconfig.go` that pipe ctx into `ServiceOptions.QuotaRefreshContext`. Document the goroutine-lifecycle caveat on both the legacy and ctx-aware factories.
- Update all 11 server-package callsites (HTTP handlers, MCP wrappers, GraphQL resolvers, worker manager) to use the ctx-aware factory. HTTP / MCP handlers get a bounded 10s timeout (`providerHandlerTimeout`) so a slow upstream subprocess cannot stall the request indefinitely.
- Wrap `svc.RouteStatus` and `svc.ListHarnesses` calls in `internal/server/providers.go` in detached goroutines bounded by ctx, since the upstream library still ignores ctx in some subprocess paths (pi/runner.go:352).
- Fix the one in-test direct `agent.NewServiceFromWorkDir(dir)` call in `internal/server/providers_test.go` to use the ctx-aware variant with a `t.Cleanup`-bound cancel.

NON-SCOPE:
- Upstream fizeau context-propagation bug (pi/runner.go:352, `DefaultModelSnapshot` ignoring ctx) is not fixed here — it would require a fizeau release. The detached-goroutine wrapper compensates.
- The agent-dispatch flow used by TestMCPAgentDispatchTrustedAllowed has the same upstream blocker for its own reasons; not in this bead.
- Pre-existing perf-budget flake `TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget` (p95 > 50ms under load) is unrelated.
title: fix(server): pipe ctx into fizeau service to cancel probe goroutines and bound RouteStatus subprocess hang

acceptance:
1. ADR-027 no longer presents global plugin install/state as the forward marketplace-plugin model; it explicitly states that registry installs are project lock metadata plus cached payloads plus generated adapters, with local overlays as the symlink exception.
2. TestDDxInstall_TopLevelCommandRetired or an equivalent cmd test proves top-level ddx install is no longer a canonical parallel install surface and points users to ddx plugin install.
3. TestPluginInstallHelp_TeachesLockCacheAndGeneratedAdapters or an equivalent cmd test proves ddx plugin install --help references project metadata, XDG cache payloads, generated .agents/.claude adapters, and does not include ddx install helix or --global examples.
4. TestInitHelp_DoesNotClaimDefaultPluginCopy or an equivalent cmd test proves ddx init --help describes project bootstrap/metadata and does not claim to install the default plugin payload into the project.
5. The ddx skill references are updated so ddx install <name>, ddx installed, and ddx uninstall <name> are not taught as active plugin lifecycle commands; run ddx skills check cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/ddx .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx if those paths are touched.
6. Existing materialization tests continue to pass: TestPluginInstall_RegistryWritesLockNotProjectPayload, TestPluginSync_MaterializesAgentSkillShimsFromCache, TestPluginConsumer_LazilySyncsMissingShims, and TestPluginInstallLocal_PreservesCheckoutOverlay.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestDDxInstall_TopLevelCommandRetired|TestPluginInstallHelp_TeachesLockCacheAndGeneratedAdapters|TestInitHelp_DoesNotClaimDefaultPluginCopy|TestPluginInstall_RegistryWritesLockNotProjectPayload|TestPluginSync_MaterializesAgentSkillShimsFromCache|TestPluginConsumer_LazilySyncsMissingShims|TestPluginInstallLocal_PreservesCheckoutOverlay" passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
HELIX is now a marketplace plugin, and DDx already has the npx-like materialization path from ddx-eaf02420: registry installs write project lock metadata, cache payloads outside the repo, and recreate generated agent adapters on demand. The remaining docs and CLI surface still teach or preserve older global/top-level plugin concepts, which makes operators think repositories must vendor plugin assets or that machine-global plugin state is the preferred path.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md:64-74 still documents a global plugin install mode and global plugin state after FEAT-015 was amended to move away from home/global plugin management.
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:618-623 still registers top-level install/installed/uninstall/outdated/verify plugin-era commands even though docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-plugin-cli-rationalization.md:85-90 says these should be retired or replaced by plugin-noun commands and doctor.
- cli/cmd/install.go:22-49 constructs plugin install help from the legacy top-level command and still teaches ddx install plus --global examples.
- cli/cmd/command_factory_commands.go:14-17 says ddx init installs the default DDx library plugin, and :35 exposes ddx init --global machine setup.
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md still teaches ddx install <name>, ddx installed, and ddx uninstall <name> as active plugin surfaces.

PROPOSED FIX
- Align ADR-027 and FEAT-015 around one forward model: project plugin intent/lock metadata plus XDG cache/global-compatible payload cache, generated .agents/.claude adapters, and no checked-in marketplace payload trees.
- Update CLI help and user-facing messages so ddx plugin install/list/show/sync are the taught surfaces; do not teach top-level ddx install or --global for marketplace plugins.
- Either retire top-level legacy plugin commands or make them explicit compatibility shims that fail with actionable messages pointing to ddx plugin install/list and ddx doctor --plugins. Do not leave them as parallel canonical surfaces.
- Update the embedded ddx skill source and generated adapters so agents learn plugin install as project intent + cache + generated adapters, not as checked-in plugin payloads.
- Preserve local developer overlays: ddx plugin install <name> --local <path> may symlink to a checkout and must not rewrite the registry lock.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rework the already-closed payload-cache implementation from ddx-eaf02420 except where tests reveal it regressed.
- Do not make HELIX built into DDx.
- Do not require project repositories to commit marketplace plugin payload trees.
- Do not remove local overlay support.

description:
PROBLEM
The lifecycle dispatch path still returns the generic `project root mutation rejected` error and the caller path continues to log it as a warning/continuing condition. When that happens in the same run that already exhibits a parent-back-edge state, the operator only sees a soft warning instead of the structural failure that actually occurred.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:130-165` rejects root mutations but does not include the offending bead ID in the failure shape, and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:595` plus the surrounding loop code consume the error as a warning path. The result is a muted signal even though the run is already in a bad structural state.

PROPOSED FIX
When the parent-back-edge state and a root-mutation rejection coincide, upgrade the log/event severity from warning to error and attach the offending bead ID to the emitted message. Add the regression `TestDispatchLogsErrorOnRootMutationWithBackEdge` so the severity change cannot drift.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the store validator, doctor scan/repair, or decomposition prompt text in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
title: Escalate lifecycle root-mutation rejection with back-edge context

acceptance:
1. TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsWorkspaceScopedOrphanHarness plants a fake execute-bead harness process group whose recorded owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1, runs the startup reaper for the current workspace, and asserts the orphaned harness process group is gone within a bounded grace.
2. TestWorkStartupReaper_KillsOrphanGrandchildProcessGroup plants a fake orphaned harness that forks a long-lived grandchild in the same process group and asserts both child and grandchild are reaped.
3. TestWorkStartupReaper_DoesNotKillLiveOwnedHarness plants a fake live-owned execute-bead harness and a fake orphaned one, runs the startup reaper, and asserts only the orphan is killed.
4. TestWorkStartupReaper_DoesNotKillOtherWorkspaceHarness plants a fake orphan tied to another workspace/project and asserts the current workspace startup reaper leaves it alive.
5. TestWorkStartupReaper_RecordsOperatorAttention verifies cleanup emits durable operator-attention or equivalent lease-release evidence carrying bead-id or attempt-id when available, owner PID status, and diagnosis.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkStartupReaper_(KillsWorkspaceScopedOrphanHarness|KillsOrphanGrandchildProcessGroup|DoesNotKillLiveOwnedHarness|DoesNotKillOtherWorkspaceHarness|RecordsOperatorAttention)' -count=1 green.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A parent-death signal is not sufficient as the only guard: existing orphaned execute-bead harness trees from prior dead workers can remain under PID 1, and platforms without Linux Pdeathsig need a startup backstop. `ddx work` needs to clean up orphaned harness children from prior dead workers without killing harnesses owned by live workers or unrelated projects.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 starts harness children for execute-bead attempts, but cleanup is tied to the current parent process running stopProcess.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 can kill a process group only when called by a live executor path.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 creates a signal.NotifyContext for graceful signals, but there is no `ddx work` startup scan for orphaned execute-bead harness processes whose owner PID is gone.
- The parent bead identifies the needed integration with lease-release/operator-attention behavior as cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf, but no startup reaper currently adopts that responsibility.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a `ddx work` startup reaper that identifies execute-bead harness processes from prior dead workers, scoped to the current project/workspace using durable ownership evidence rather than broad command-name matching alone.
- Kill only orphaned process groups whose recorded owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1 and whose workspace/attempt metadata ties them to the current project.
- Preserve live-owned harnesses, including other concurrent workers in the same project.
- Record or surface operator-attention evidence compatible with the existing lease-release/operator-attention machinery when orphan cleanup occurs.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add Linux Pdeathsig handling in this child; that is covered by the executor parent-death child bead.
- Do not change docker-clone process/container cleanup; that is covered by the docker-clone child bead.
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child remains at the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS / CROSS-REFS
- docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md
- docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md
- fizeau-50b6f055
- ddx-8f2e0ebf

title: work startup: reap orphaned execute-bead harness process groups scoped to the workspace

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBeadRejectsTopLevelSampleChildBeads simulates an execute-bead Step 0 agent running `ddx bead create "Sample: lint cleanup"` without parent metadata and proves the real tracker is not polluted with an open top-level bead.
2. TestExecuteBeadDecompositionRequiresCurrentParent creates a legitimate child through the execute-bead decomposition path and proves the child parent equals the current bead ID, labels/spec-id are inherited, and invalid parentless children are rejected or quarantined.
3. TestWorkStatusDropsStaleRunStateWhenWorkerPidGone creates a run-state record for a dead worker pid and proves `ddx work status --json` does not report it as active work after cleanup.
4. Invalid execute-bead child creation emits durable operator_attention evidence with child IDs, attempted title, parent value, and rejection reason.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadRejectsTopLevelSampleChildBeads|TestExecuteBeadDecompositionRequiresCurrentParent|TestWorkStatusDropsStaleRunStateWhenWorkerPidGone' passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-06-13 dogfood run for ddx-aef128f7, an interrupted `ddx work` execution polluted the real DDx tracker with 20 top-level open beads titled `Sample: ...` (`ddx-5d26dc27`, `ddx-37dd1bef`, `ddx-e7f8ad72`, `ddx-2a77ac3f`, `ddx-d369d255`, `ddx-9646569a`, `ddx-3cf17faf`, `ddx-541bf722`, `ddx-e9384053`, `ddx-2a5286ae`, `ddx-fb8f9a90`, `ddx-552ec9c5`, `ddx-86e2a4ce`, `ddx-0b369a91`, `ddx-2eed7ae3`, `ddx-8c6abcc2`, `ddx-e64dea54`, `ddx-6479485f`, `ddx-8fbd3aba`, `ddx-10c17c2d`). I cancelled those bogus beads through `ddx bead update --status cancelled`, but the worker should never have been able to create them in the landing tracker.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1885-1903 tells execute-bead agents that Step 0 may run `ddx bead create` for decomposition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1981-1984 reiterates that Step 0 allows bead creation while current-bead lifecycle stays orchestrator-owned. The prompt requires `parent=<parent-id>`, but DDx does not enforce that an execute-bead-created child has the current bead as parent, inherits required labels/spec-id, or writes a valid `no_changes_rationale.txt` before mutating the real landing tracker. The dogfood attempt created top-level sample fixtures instead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guarded decomposition command/path for execute-bead Step 0, or validate all bead-create mutations made from an execution worktree before they reach the landing tracker.
- Reject or quarantine execute-bead-created beads whose title/description/AC look like sample fixtures, whose parent is empty or not the current bead, or whose required inherited labels/spec-id are absent.
- Add cleanup/recovery that parks or cancels invalid child beads automatically and records an operator_attention event naming the invalid IDs.
- Ensure stale run-state from a killed worker does not keep `ddx work status` reporting active work after the owning worker process is gone.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove legitimate Step 0 decomposition. Do not rewrite or delete tracker history manually. Do not change normal operator-invoked `ddx bead create` behavior outside execute-bead/work execution contexts.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-aef128f7: the same aborted run also proved process-tree cleanup failures.
- ddx-6d9a8bdb: the same run also proved pinned-provider escape.
title: work: prevent execute-bead from polluting queue with sample child beads

acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig` or equivalent, using a real primary repo plus execute-bead worktree, that contaminates the worker attempt with commands such as `git config --local core.bare true`, `git config --local user.email fixture@ddx.test`, and a nested test subprocess with Git-local env vars set.
2. The test asserts after the attempt that the primary checkout satisfies `git status --short`, has no `core.bare`, has no stray `core.worktree`, and does not contain fixture `user.name`/`user.email`.
3. The fix ensures linked worktree attempts have per-worktree config isolation and/or scrubbed Git env for all agent/test subprocesses that DDx launches.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/git/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig|TestGitFixtureHelpersStripHookEnv|TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare" -count=1` passes.
5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression runs.

description:
PROBLEM
During live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Do not rewrite execute-bead history or skip worker isolation; the fix must preserve isolated worktree execution while protecting the primary checkout.
title: execute-bead: prevent worker attempts from mutating primary git config

acceptance:
1. TestPreCommitGoTestFailsWhenAnyPackageFails or an equivalent hook-script test proves a failing first package followed by a passing package returns non-zero.
2. The hook implementation in lefthook.yml:206-214 or a referenced script preserves all package output and exits non-zero on the first or accumulated failure.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` no longer reports success when `go test ./cmd` prints FAIL.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestPreCommitGoTestFailsWhenAnyPackageFails -count=1` (or the actual new test path) passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` behavior is documented in the bead evidence, including how unrelated existing cmd failures are handled.
description:
PROBLEM
During ddx-42124c23 verification on 2026-06-13, `lefthook run pre-commit` exited 0 even though its `go-test` step printed FAIL for `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd`. That makes the required automated gate a false green and lets broken verification pass.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
lefthook.yml:206-214 builds a package list from staged paths and runs `go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"` inside a shell `for pkg in $packages; do ... done` loop. The script does not accumulate failures or `set -e`, so when an early package fails and a later package passes, the shell returns the last command status. In the observed run, `cmd` failed but `internal/activework` and `internal/agent` passed, and Lefthook reported a successful hook.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the staged-package go-test hook fail if any package test fails. Preserve per-package output. Add a deterministic test or script-level check that simulates two packages where the first fails and the second passes and proves the hook exits non-zero.

NON-SCOPE
Do not fix the unrelated existing `cmd` package failures in this bead unless they are needed to prove the hook status propagation. Do not relax the staged package test gate.
title: test: make pre-commit go-test fail when any package fails

acceptance:
1. `TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins` verifies that `ddx init` with no flag creates `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest but does not write `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, or any project-local `plugins/` tree.
2. `TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution` verifies the initialized project can lazy-resolve the default `ddx` plugin from the global layer through `registry.ResolvePlugin` when the sibling resolver behavior is present.
3. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins|TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution'` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Plain `ddx init` eagerly installs the default `ddx` plugin into every project. The intended model is that project init only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and the project manifest; plugins should lazy-resolve from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` currently routes plain init through eager plugin setup.
- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 calls `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)` unconditionally, producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` in the project.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Remove the default plugin install from the no-flag `ddx init` path.
2. Keep project-local initialization of `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest intact.
3. Ensure the no-flag path does not create or modify any project `plugins/` tree or agent-tier skill links.
4. Leave actual plugin lookup to `registry.ResolvePlugin`, preferring project over global, from the sibling registry-resolver bead.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- sibling registry-resolver bead.

PARENT
- ddx-937de9fc.
title: init: stop project-local default plugin installation in plain ddx init

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the identical warn-only rejection ("decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string"), so the worker logs a warning and skips. With a full ready queue and a single fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue goes idle indefinitely. Fizeau observation 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC: identical fingerprint 15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f recurred across distinct bead_ids, confirming a schema mismatch rather than a bead-specific issue.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict field in the readiness-check struct is typed as a plain Go string, so encoding/json rejects a bool payload. See cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:77-82 (preClaimReadinessCheck struct) and the decode entry point decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult / decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:709-725. Producers emit verdict as a JSON bool (true/false) while the consumer parsed string only.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix A)
Introduce a custom readinessVerdict type at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:95-125 with an UnmarshalJSON that accepts JSON bool, JSON string, and null/absent, coercing each to a single documented canonical form (e.g. bool true -> "pass", false -> "fail", string lowercased, null/absent -> empty). Wire the preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict field to the new type so the array/object payload decoder at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416 succeeds for every verdict shape.

NON-SCOPE
- CI schema drift gate (sibling child: schema lock).
- Same-fingerprint warn escalation / warn-only 100%-failure guard (sibling child: escalation).
- claim-success-rate operator signal (sibling child: claim-rate window).
- README documentation (sibling child: docs).

CROSS-REF
Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads (blocked by this idle). Adjacent silent-failure bugs in this session: ddx-57c40485 (index.lock held minutes), ddx-8f2e0ebf (worker wedges on route resolution). Shared theme: silent failures inside the worker that look healthy from the outside.
title: pre-claim intake decoder: accept readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent

description:
PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode model to every doc that references .ddx/ paths or project state. Each spec update added in this bead's commit cites principle-numbered tags so future readers see the link:
   - TD-031 bead state machine — "Storage anchors via ddxroot.Path(); see ddx-06cbaa90."
   - SD-019 multi-project-server-topology — Project identity from `host/owner/repo`; reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - SD-020 multi-machine-coordinator-topology — same.
   - ADR-021 operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path — reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - workers-sessions-operator-surface.md — worker status paths through ddxroot.Path().
   - plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture — FEAT-010 paths through resolver.
   - library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md — install precedence per ddx-937de9fc.
   - library/README.md — install topology overview.

NON-SCOPE
- New behaviours (every scenario in the matrix is already covered by ddx-06cbaa90 or ddx-937de9fc per-feature tests; this is the cross-mode equivalence layer).
- Migration tooling for existing in-tree repos (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
ddx-937de9fc (skills/personas/plugins).

Verified by TestStoreReleaseClearsClaimAtomically in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go.
2. After release, ddx bead status reports the bead as not in_progress with no owner and no lease sidecar remains. Verified by TestStoreReleaseClearsClaimAtomically.
3. A concurrent (multi-goroutine/process) test inspects status mid-claim: while claimed the bead reports in_progress with an owner; after release it reports open with no owner. Verified by TestClaimReleaseStatusReflectsLease in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go.

description:
PROBLEM
When a worker abandons a bead (route-resolution timeout, watchdog fire, or any failure release), the bead's claim/lease must be cleared atomically and `ddx bead status` must reflect the lease state. Claiming a bead today atomically transitions it to in_progress, but there is no symmetric release path that, in a single locked write, transitions the bead back and clears the lease. The parent bug (ddx-8f2e0ebf) observed "bead has a claim but ddx bead status shows In progress: 0" — claim and lifecycle status not atomically coupled on the release side.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go: Store.Claim (~lines 863-930) is atomic — under WithLock it calls transitionLifecycleInPlace(StatusInProgress) + sets Owner + WriteAll, then writeClaimHeartbeat writes the lease sidecar. There is NO symmetric Store.Release that, under the same WithLock, transitions status back (default open), clears Owner, WriteAll, AND removes the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar in one critical section. Failure-release paths can leave a stale lease sidecar while status reports not-in_progress, producing the phantom-lease inconsistency.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add Store.Release(id, assignee, targetStatus) mirroring Store.Claim: under WithLock, read beads, transitionLifecycleInPlace to target status (open by default), clear Owner, WriteAll, and remove the claim-heartbeat lease sidecar — all in one locked operation.
2. Route the worker's failure-release paths through Store.Release so claim clearing and status are coupled.
3. Ensure `ddx bead status` counts reflect lease state: a released bead is not counted in_progress and has no owner; a claimed bead is in_progress with an owner.

NON-SCOPE
Route-resolution timeout (sibling child A), heartbeat watchdog (child B), same-bead-twice guard (child D), operator-attention query surface + docs (child E). This bead only establishes atomic claim/release symmetry and status accuracy. This is a decomposition child of ddx-8f2e0ebf.
title: bead: add atomic Store.Release so failure-release clears the claim and status reflects the lease

description:
PROBLEM
When an operator wants to retry a bead currently in cooldown (work-retry-after set in the future), the only way today is to manually run ddx bead update <id> --unset work-retry-after before each ddx try / ddx work invocation. With 14 beads in cooldown today the operator ran 14 such commands in sequence (and even then a single ddx bead clear-cooldown --all doesn't exist yet — that's ddx-63688d7b). There is no CLI surface for "I know this bead is cooled; run it anyway because I just resolved the underlying issue."

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/work.go and cli/cmd/try.go both honor work-retry-after via the standard ready-queue filter. There is no override flag. The escalation ladder and route-exclusion machinery is correct (and ddx-63688d7b will narrow when cooldowns even apply), but operators still need a per-invocation escape hatch for the cases where cooldown was set by a now-resolved condition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add --force-claim to ddx try <id>: ignore work-retry-after for the specified bead during this attempt. Emit a kind=force-claim event with actor=<assignee> and the timestamp the override was used. The cooldown field is NOT cleared; if the attempt fails again, the original cooldown remains in effect for queued workers.
- Add --ignore-cooldown to ddx work: drain mode, ignore work-retry-after across the queue for this worker session. Same event semantics.
- Both flags emit a structured event so the audit trail shows "operator forced through cooldown at time T".
- Both flags require an explicit --reason "<text>" string (operator must explain why). The reason is captured in the event body.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the cooldown-setting policy (ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Do not auto-clear cooldowns after a successful forced attempt (the bead's success implicitly invalidates any stale cooldown via its own state, but we don't mutate the field).
- Do not expose this flag in passthrough constraints; it's an operator override only.

DEPS
ddx-63688d7b (the cooldown-policy rework). The --ignore-cooldown semantics depend on what cooldowns mean post-63688d7b.
title: work/try: --force-claim flag to ignore work-retry-after for operator-driven retry

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.

description:
PROBLEM
The auto-epic decomposition path also uses the legacy park-open pattern. Even if pre-claim and recovery decomposition close parents correctly, auto epic decomposition can still leave a losslessly decomposed parent open and execution-ineligible.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go:182-204 uses the same park-open behavior for auto epic decomposition instead of closing the decomposed parent.
- Existing coverage does not require the auto epic path to close the parent while retaining `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Update `AutoDecomposeEpic` in cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go so, after generated children are durably created, the decomposed epic parent is closed through the lifecycle API as completed-by-decomposition. Preserve `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence and include child IDs in durable event/extra evidence. Clear claim metadata and heartbeat from the parent on close. Preserve child readiness semantics: Parent metadata is traceability only and must not create a dependency gate.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.

- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS

description:
PROBLEM
DDx cleanup dry-run can remove or report stale execution files, but it cannot tell an operator that a stale DDx-owned descendant process is still alive under a current-project execution worktree. This leaves orphan shells, cargo test or benchmark processes, and tiny long-lived test servers invisible during recovery.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` defines `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with file/resource counts only, so process findings, process-group counts, stale reasons, and `would_kill` status cannot be represented.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` cleans temp dirs, registered worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, agent logs, worker dirs, and candidate refs, but has no process census tied to DDx attempt worktrees.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` covers only PID-1 children whose command basename looks like a top-level harness and whose owner PID is gone; it does not report non-harness descendants left under stale execution worktrees.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` already gives normal harness children process-group and parent-death behavior, but cleanup has no later operator-visible census for descendants that survive after the harness root exits.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an internal, injectable stale attempt process scanner to execution cleanup. On Linux, collect `/proc` facts equivalent to the existing process-scanning style: pid, ppid, process group when available, command or truncated command, cwd, start time when available, and enough project ownership context to tie the process to the current project's DDx execution root or a known attempt worktree. Extend `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with process findings, counts, unavailable status, blocked warnings, stale reason, and `would_kill=true` for dry-run candidates. Dry-run must report candidates and must not call the killer. Add an unsupported-platform no-op/report-unavailable implementation. Detect non-harness descendants by cwd/worktree/liveness ownership evidence, not by broad command-name matching.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- `spec:FEAT-010`
- `spec:SD-025`

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.

description:
PROBLEM
Hidden lifecycle model calls in `ddx work --watch` can mutate the operator's project checkout before a bead attempt starts. Observed on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: readiness/decomposition for `fizeau-930b498b` dispatched `MODE: intake` / `MODE: preclaim-decompose` with the request workdir set to the Fizeau project root, and the main checkout was left dirty with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, `website/benchmark_responsive_test.go`, and a newly-created release-blocker bead (`fizeau-b5b6dff2`). Lifecycle calls are supposed to classify, rewrite, or propose children; they must not have a writable project-root execution context.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:60-70` does the same for pre-claim decomposition, and `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:150-161` does the same for post-ladder decomposition.
`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:172-176` turns an empty runtime workdir into the project workdir, and `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-252` sends that workdir to Fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest.WorkDir`. With normal project permissions, Codex/Claude/Gemini lifecycle calls can edit files in the checkout even though DDx only needs their JSON response.

PROPOSED FIX
Run all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Keep reading bead/project context through DDx code, parse the model JSON response, and apply any tracker mutations through DDx store APIs after validation. Add a post-dispatch guard that fails the lifecycle call if the project root becomes dirty from anything other than DDx-owned tracker mutations performed by the orchestrator itself.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change normal `ddx try` / execute-bead worktree execution. Do not let lifecycle agents commit code or edit project files. Do not weaken decomposition output validation or tracker API usage.
title: work: isolate lifecycle model calls from project root

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
- `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
- Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.
- TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The Story-16 run-detail page lacks a cross-cutting end-to-end guard proving that a worst-case (p95) dataset renders every tab and becomes interactive within a single render frame. Without it, regressions in the run-detail page, evidence browser, or tool-trace view can ship undetected.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts:1159-1164 declares `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95`, the cross-cutting guard that must drive a p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) through the full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list flow and assert single-frame interactivity. This child owns making that guard real and green.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (Story 16.1 run-detail page) and ddx-4cd64068 (Story 16.2 evidence tab); both must be landed for the page and evidence browser the guard exercises to exist.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement/finish `Story16RunDetailFullStackP95` in cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts so it loads a run with 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files, opens all tabs (prompt, response, stderr, tool-trace, evidence list), and asserts the page reaches interactive state within one requestAnimationFrame budget.
- Use the existing run-detail page (Story 16.1) and evidence tab (Story 16.2) without modifying their component contracts.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend resolver, spec-doc (FEAT-008/010/019), and 'raw transcript viewed' audit-event work — owned by sibling/parent beads.
- New auth or role-based access control beyond the documented trust boundary.
- Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads (the Go perf/timeout fixes for parent AC 4).
