description:
PROBLEM
  TD-031 §3.1 defines ClosureGate (store.go:1245) as the gate for evidence-bearing 
  execute-bead closes. However, applyReconcilePlan at reconcile.go:208-254 with 
  CloseSatisfied=true intentionally bypasses ClosureGate for meta-closes where 
  all transitive dependencies are closed. This distinction is not explicitly 
  documented in the spec.

PROPOSED FIX
  Amend TD-031 §3.1 to add a normative paragraph explaining:
  - ClosureGate applies only to evidence-bearing execute-bead closes via CloseWithEvidence
  - reconcile-close (applyReconcilePlan with CloseSatisfied=true) is a distinct 
    meta-close that intentionally bypasses ClosureGate
  - The bypass is safe because every transitive dependency is already closed 
    (each having individually passed ClosureGate or been a prior meta-close)
  
  Cross-reference the existing language at reconcile.go:208-254.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the ClosureGate logic itself
  - Changing reconcile-close behavior
  - Adding new persisted statuses
title: amend TD-031 to declare reconcile-close vs evidence-close semantics

acceptance:
1. cli/cmd/work.go newWorkCommand sets SilenceUsage: true (and SilenceErrors: true) on the cobra.Command literal, matching cli/cmd/try.go:46 and cli/cmd/run.go:26.
2. cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go no longer returns a bare context.Canceled error to RunE after a SIGINT-cancelled run; the operator-cancel path returns nil or an exit-130 sentinel after writing the terminal summary, while context.DeadlineExceeded and all other errors still propagate unchanged.
3. New test TestWork_CtrlC_ExitsCleanlyNoUsageNoRawError (in cli/cmd, e.g. work_test.go or execute_loop_shared_test.go): drive the work RunE / root command with a context that is cancelled mid-run, capture stderr, and assert the stderr contains NEITHER "Usage:" NOR "Error: context canceled" (it may still contain "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully" and "worker exited: stopped by signal").
4. Same or a sibling test asserts the cancelled run maps to exit code 130 (assert NewExitError code 130, or that Execute returns an *ExitError with Code==130 for the context.Canceled path), confirming the clean SIGINT exit code is preserved.
5. Existing TestRootSignal_FirstInterruptCancelsContextAndPrintsGracefulMessage and TestRootSignal_MessagePrintedOnce in cli/cmd/root_signal_test.go still pass.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM

Cancelling a long-running 'ddx work' with Ctrl-C (SIGINT) prints noisy
error spam after the graceful-shutdown lines. Observed output:

    Cancel received, shutting down gracefully
    project: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim
    worker exited: stopped by signal
    attempts: 0  |  closed: 0  |  changed: 0  |  already-satisfied: 0  |  failures: 0
    Error: context canceled
    Usage:
      ddx work [flags]
      ddx work [command]

    Examples:
      # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
      ddx work
      ... (full usage/examples dump)

The "Cancel received..." line and the terminal summary are fine. The
"Error: context canceled" line AND the entire "Usage:"/"Examples:" block
are spam. A clean Ctrl-C of a long-running command should exit quietly
(exit code 130), not print a raw error and the help text.

ROOT CAUSE

There are two compounding defects, both on the 'ddx work' RunE -> Cobra path:

1. The execute loop returns the raw context.Canceled error up to RunE.
   cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:662-666: after worker.Run returns with
   err wrapping context.Canceled, line 662-664 writes the terminal
   summary, then line 665-666 does `if err != nil { return err }`,
   returning context.Canceled to runWork.
   - runWork (cli/cmd/work.go:134-157) returns that error unchanged via
     `return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")` at work.go:156.

2. The 'work' command does NOT set SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors, and neither
   does the root command, so Cobra's ExecuteContext prints both the raw
   error and the usage block before the error can be mapped to a clean exit.
   - newWorkCommand (cli/cmd/work.go:22-127) builds the cobra.Command at
     work.go:23-73 with NO SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors field (contrast
     cli/cmd/try.go:46 `SilenceUsage: true` and cli/cmd/run.go:26
     `SilenceUsage: true`, which is why ddx try / ddx run do NOT dump usage).
   - The root command, NewRootCommand (cli/cmd/command_factory.go:136-226,
     command spec at 143-168), also sets neither field.
   - Because SilenceErrors is unset, Cobra prints "Error: context canceled".
     Because SilenceUsage is unset and context.Canceled is a runtime
     (non-arg-parse) error, Cobra also dumps the full Usage/Examples block.

3. The clean-exit mapping happens too late to suppress the spam.
   cli/cmd/root.go:52-56: executeRootCommand returns, and Execute maps a
   context.Canceled error to NewExitError(130, "") so cli/main.go:33-38
   prints nothing and exits 130. But this mapping runs AFTER
   rootCmd.ExecuteContext (cli/cmd/root_signal.go:47) has already printed
   the "Error:" line and the Usage block. So exit code 130 is correct, but
   the spam is already on stderr.

WORKING REFERENCE: ddx try (cli/cmd/try.go:46) and ddx run
(cli/cmd/run.go:26) set SilenceUsage:true on their command structs, so a
runtime error never dumps usage for those commands. ddx work just needs
the same treatment plus suppression of the raw context.Canceled error.

PROPOSED FIX

- cli/cmd/work.go:23-73  add `SilenceUsage: true` (and `SilenceErrors: true`)
  to the cobra.Command literal in newWorkCommand, mirroring try.go:46 /
  run.go:26, so a runtime error from runWork never dumps the usage block.
- cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:662-666  when err wraps context.Canceled
  (operator SIGINT), do NOT return the raw error up to RunE after the
  terminal summary has been written; return nil (or a sentinel that
  main.go maps to exit 130) so Cobra prints no "Error: context canceled"
  line. DeadlineExceeded / other errors must still propagate as today.
  Preserve the existing exit-code-130 behavior: if you return nil here,
  ensure SIGINT still yields exit 130 (e.g. keep the root.go:52-56
  context.Canceled -> NewExitError(130,"") mapping reachable, or return an
  ExitError(130,"") sentinel from this layer). Do not regress the exit code.
- Keep the "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully" line and the
  "worker exited: stopped by signal" terminal summary; only the
  "Error: context canceled" line and the Usage/Examples dump are removed.

NON-SCOPE

- ddx try and ddx run already set SilenceUsage (try.go:46, run.go:26); do
  not touch them beyond what is needed to share a helper if you choose to.
- Do not change the graceful-shutdown announcer (cli/cmd/root_signal.go)
  or the "stopped by signal" summary text (cli/cmd/work.go:260-261).
- Do not alter DeadlineExceeded handling or non-signal error propagation.
title: ddx work: exit cleanly on Ctrl-C without printing 'context canceled' + usage dump

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go exports Diagnose(b, childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex) BlockReason and BlockReasonCode constants for every code in FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation.
2. cli/internal/bead/diagnose_test.go has table-driven TestDiagnose with one case per BlockReasonCode, including the no_diagnosis fallback and the parent_child_state_conflict case.
3. TestDiagnose covers the priority-match behavior: a bead matching both superseded_pending_close and dep_blocked_by reports superseded_pending_close (first match in FEAT-010 order).
4. The function is pure: no calls to store.Get, no file I/O, no global state mutation. Verified by code reading.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestDiagnose -count=1 green.
6. cd cli && go test ./... green (no regressions in other packages).
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx work's idle path (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315) reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail and then sleeps or exits. There is no per-bead diagnosis of WHY each non-ready bead is non-ready, so the loop cannot fire targeted auto-remediations. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires a closed reason taxonomy with one code per non-ready bead.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnoser exists. ReadyExecutionBreakdown returns bucket lists (Epics, EpicClosureCandidates, DepWaiting, OperatorAttention) but does not annotate each entry with why-it-is-blocked detail at a granularity the work loop can switch on.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go with a pure function Diagnose(b Bead, childIndex map[string][]Bead, openChildIndex map[string][]Bead, depIndex map[string][]Dependency) BlockReason. BlockReason is a typed struct: { Code BlockReasonCode; Detail string; CitedIDs []string }. BlockReasonCode is a typed-string enum with exactly the codes listed in FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation (superseded_pending_close, closure_candidate_misclassified, dead_intermediate_all_children_closed, dead_intermediate_open_children_pending, epic_of_epic, dep_blocked_by, genuinely_needs_decomposition, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, stale_graph_index, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis). The function is pure — no I/O — and takes pre-built indexes so it can be called from both the loop and the CLI.

Detection rules per code are specified in the FEAT-010 table. For superseded_pending_close, read the bead's superseded-by field OR a superseded-by:<Y> label, then verify Y is closed in the index. For closure_candidate_misclassified, check IssueType==epic AND len(openChildIndex[b.ID])==0 AND len(childIndex[b.ID])>0. For genuinely_needs_decomposition, check IssueType==epic AND len(childIndex[b.ID])==0 AND no manual-hold/no-auto-decompose/container label. For dep_blocked_by, cite every open dep's ID and status. Match codes in the order they appear in the FEAT-010 table; first match wins.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring Diagnose into the work loop (separate bead).
- Inventing new reason codes beyond the FEAT-010 taxonomy.
- Mutations of any kind.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
title: diagnose: per-bead idle-scan reason classifier

acceptance:
1. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject (cli/cmd/update_test.go): with both a global and a project plugin tree seeded, `ddx update --global` modifies only `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/` and leaves the project plugins/ dir byte-identical.
2. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode (cli/cmd/update_test.go): `ddx update` (no flag) updates the in-tree `<project>/.ddx/plugins/` when an in-tree root exists, and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/` dir when it does not, without touching the global tree in either case.
3. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx update` must mirror the install topology: `--global` updates only the machine-wide plugin tree under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/`, while the no-flag form updates the active project install in whichever mode (in-tree or convention) is currently in effect, leaving the other tier untouched.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go:58 `runUpdate` branches on `--global` (comment at update.go:65) to cli/cmd/update.go:300 `performGlobalUpdate`, otherwise to cli/cmd/update.go:204 `performUpdate(workingDir, opts)` which operates on `ddxroot.Path`-resolved targets and `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets` (update.go:122).
- Without locked-in tests, a `--global` update could touch the project tree (or a project update could touch global), violating tier isolation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Ensure `ddx update --global` writes only under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/` and never mutates any project plugins/ dir.
- Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install resolved by `ddxroot.Path()` — `<project>/.ddx/plugins/` in in-tree mode, the XDG `projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/` dir in convention mode — and does not mutate the global tree.
- Tests sandbox `XDG_DATA_HOME`/`HOME`, seed both tiers, run the command, and assert mtimes/content changed in exactly one tier.

NON-SCOPE
- Install/init surfaces — sibling beads.
- doctor reporting and spec/docs updates — sibling bead.
- Removing the per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) for `ddxroot.Path()` / `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
- Sibling install beads (global + project install must exist to seed update targets).
title: update: `ddx update --global` updates global tree only; `ddx update` is mode-aware

2. The test failure path reports per-worker `ExecuteBeadLoopResult` status/detail, closed bead count, bead lifecycle summary, and relevant git refs so a future failure identifies the missing transition.
3. If the stress run exposes a production race, add or update a focused regression test for the specific claim/land bug and make it pass.

description:
PROBLEM
While validating the landed `ddx-7eb97e4e` side-worktree reporting fix on 2026-05-15, the broad agent package gate failed once in `TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded`. The failure closed only 4 of 5 seeded beads and `git show HEAD:bead-0005.txt` failed with `fatal: path 'bead-0005.txt' does not exist in 'HEAD'`. A direct rerun of the same test immediately passed, so this is either a real concurrent worker race or a nondeterministic integration-test assumption. Either way, this test currently cannot serve as a trustworthy gate for concurrent bead landing safety.

Observed failing command:
`cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1`

Observed focused rerun:
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded -count=1 -v` passed in 0.483s.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:408-423` starts 5 workers concurrently with `Once:false` against one store and shared project root.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:432-451` asserts all 5 beads are closed and all bead files are reachable from HEAD, but the failure showed one bead missing from both the tracker terminal state and landed git state.
- The current test does not dump per-worker `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, claimed bead IDs, terminal statuses, or preserved refs on failure, which makes it impossible to distinguish an actual claim/land race from a test harness timing assumption using only CI output.

PROPOSED FIX
Reproduce under stress, then either fix the underlying concurrent claim/land behavior or make the test harness deterministic without weakening the invariant. Add failure diagnostics that print each worker result, bead lifecycle summary, and git refs when the invariant fails. The invariant must remain: five concurrent workers over five ready beads eventually close and land all five distinct beads exactly once.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not skip, delete, or loosen the concurrent landing invariant.
- Do not hide the test behind short mode.
- Do not change production queue ordering or retry policy except where a real race is proven and fixed.

DEPS
None.
title: agent: stabilize concurrent script-harness landing test

description:
PROBLEM
Claude readiness runs can return classification "executable" even when the prompt asks for "ready". During the server-managed DDx canary on worker-20260615T172921-149f for ddx-cf0cafef, the worker logged: readiness schema drift: unknown classification "executable": expected one of ready, needs_refine, needs_split, operator_required, system_unready. The worker continued, but this makes Claude readiness less reliable and can prevent ready beads from claiming cleanly.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:179 normalizeReadinessClassification maps several ready synonyms (ready/actionable_atomic/atomic/ok/actionable/pass) but omits the common Claude wording "executable" / "executable_as_written". cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go lacks a regression test for that provider wording.

PROPOSED FIX
Normalize "executable" and "executable_as_written" to ReadinessClassificationReady, and add a decoder-level regression test proving the payload maps to PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic with no system_unready/schema_drift reason.

NON-SCOPE

acceptance: 1. A goal/stop-predicate set once persists in run-state and is reloaded on a simulated worker restart without re-supplying it — covered by a Go test in cli/internal/agent/ (e.g. run_state round-trip + work-loop honors persisted goal). 2. With no goal set, behavior is unchanged (default mechanical stop conditions) — covered by test. 3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... passes; go build ./... succeeds.
description:
PROBLEM
The #1 source of repeated operator prompts by volume: the operator re-pastes a ~2000-char goal/stop directive ('drain the ready queue by priority... do not stop until specs met and perf re-baselined... AskUserQuestion only for irreducible forks') every time a worker restarts or context compacts. The work-drive has no durable place to keep the operator's goal, stop-predicate, or autonomy posture, so it is re-injected by hand each iteration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/run_state.go (RunState) persists only mechanical per-attempt fields (BeadID, AttemptID, WorktreePath, PID, candidate-cycle phase). No goal/stop-predicate/autonomy field.
- cli/cmd/work.go loop stop conditions are mechanical only (drained/blocked/deferred/no_progress/signal via ExitReason). There is no persisted operator goal that is re-evaluated each iteration.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a durable, optional goal object to the work-drive state (set once, e.g. `ddx work --goal <text|file>` or a persisted .ddx/run-state field): operator goal text + an explicit stop-predicate + autonomy posture.
- Re-load and surface it each work iteration so a restarted/compacted worker honors it without re-injection. This subsumes the old 'make the autonomy rule stick under a declared drain' item — the autonomy/stop posture lives here, not in HELIX docs.
- Keep it ORTHOGONAL to the bead queue: the queue is WHAT work; the goal is the stop condition + posture. Document why both exist.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing or power selection.
- Do not change bead lifecycle statuses.
- Do not implement natural-language goal evaluation beyond a simple, testable predicate (e.g. 'queue empty AND <named gate> green').

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
title: Persist a durable goal + stop-predicate on the work-drive (survives restart)

description:
PROBLEM
Genuinely undecomposed epics (IssueType==epic, totalChildCount==0, no closure-pending state) need automated decomposition into child task beads so ddx work can drain them. Today the only decomposition path is operator-invoked (bead-breakdown skill) or post-TooLarge recovery (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go). FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires the work loop to auto-dispatch decomposition for this specific case (RC-6) with strict preconditions to avoid the v1-plan duplicate-child bug.

ROOT CAUSE
No handler exists that bridges the diagnoser's genuinely_needs_decomposition reason code to runPreClaimDecomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:48). The existing post-TooLarge hook (NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook at recovery_decompose.go:107) gates only on TooLarge failure class, not on idle-scan diagnosis.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go exporting AutoDecomposeEpic(ctx, store, runner, rcfg, projectRoot, beadID) (DecomposeResult, error). It calls runPreClaimDecomposer (reused unchanged) after asserting every precondition. Preconditions (each must hold; first failing precondition produces a typed error AND increments the per-bead auto_decompose_attempted event count):
- store.Get(beadID).IssueType == "epic"
- totalChildCount(beadID) == 0 (use the same index ReadyExecutionBreakdown uses)
- bead is not currently in the EpicClosureCandidate bucket
- bead has no open dep_blocked_by or closed_or_missing_parent diagnoses (call the new Diagnose function on the bead; if it returns either, return ErrDepsBlockDecompose)
- bead carries no label in {manual-hold, no-auto-decompose, container}
- bead description is non-empty AND contains PROBLEM AND PROPOSED FIX sections AND has at least one acceptance criterion AND has at least one label matching ^(spec|area):
- per-bead event count of auto_decompose_attempted < 3
- bead has no active execution cooldown
- (operator override --no-auto-epic-decompose checked at the caller, not here)

On success: parent epic's execution_eligible is set to false (mirrors recovery_decompose.go behavior), child beads are created via store.Create, the auto_decompose_attempted event is emitted with body {child_ids: [...], cost_usd: N}.

On failure: SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, now+15m, "auto_decompose_failed", reason, baseRev), emit auto_decompose_failed event. On the 3rd cumulative attempt failure also emit auto_remediation_exhausted so the diagnoser surfaces it.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring this into the work loop (separate bead).
- Changing the runPreClaimDecomposer prompt (reused as-is).
- Surfacing the operator override flag (work-loop bead handles flag plumbing).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose function) and ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle fix — the closure-bucket check needs the correct classifier).
title: agent: auto_decompose_epic — wrap runPreClaimDecomposer for genuinely-undecomposed epics

description:
PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
Non-global ddx install / ddx update assume an in-tree .ddx/. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the plugin must land under the XDG project dir (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/) with project-tier skill links pointing into it. Today install writes to the literal relative .ddx/plugins path.

ROOT CAUSE
- registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir) and the default target defaultPackageRootTarget (installer.go:128) build a project-relative '.ddx/plugins/<name>' string; they do not route through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) which already resolves in-tree vs convention (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75).
- update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) and commitPluginChanges (install.go:210) likewise assume .ddx/ in-tree.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Resolve the project plugin root through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> for the non-global path so convention mode lands under the XDG project dir and in-tree mode stays under <project>/.ddx/plugins/.
2. Create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into whichever plugins root resolved.
3. ddx update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE: --global (ddx-aedb009d); init behavior; runtime precedence (ddx-18e55cd2).

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2.
notes: Superseded by ddx-eaf02420 and ADR-027. This child still asks for project/convention plugin payload copies under plugins/<name>; the forward model is registry lock + XDG cache + generated adapters, not copied marketplace payload trees.
title: install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path()

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-43b6b5fe is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 4 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode m
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestModeMatrix_TwoClonesShareXDG asserts two project worktrees pointing at the same git remote share the XDG project dir AND register both in worktrees.json with one auto-pinned as master.
- TestModeMatrix_OperatorExpectationDocumented asserts the suite's package godoc clearly states the cross-mode equivalence invariant.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_ClaimSuccessRateWarnsBelowThreshold` simulates a full rolling window of non-successful claim attempts and asserts exactly one `loop.operator_attention` event containing `claim_success_rate_below_threshold`, window size, successes, non-successes, success rate, and threshold.
2. The same test, or a companion subtest, proves a later successful claim clears the warned state so a later below-threshold window can warn again.
3. `ddx work --help` exposes configurable `--claim-rate-window` and `--claim-rate-threshold` flags with defaults 10 and 0.0.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestExecuteBeadLoop_ClaimSuccessRateWarnsBelowThreshold` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestWork.*Claim|TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop'` passes if CLI flags or result schema are touched.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can appear healthy while repeatedly skipping claims on a ready queue. A top-level operator-visible signal is needed when the worker's recent claim success rate drops to zero or below a configured threshold over a rolling window.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The work loop owns claim-attempt telemetry and operator-visible events. Runtime configuration for claim throughput belongs with `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:86-92`, and operator-visible loop events are emitted from the loop. Without a rolling claim-success-rate guard, repeated non-successes only appear as low-level intake warnings or idle events instead of a clear stuck-worker signal.

PROPOSED FIX
Track claim-attempt outcomes in a rolling window, default window=10 and threshold=0.0, and emit a single `loop.operator_attention` event plus stderr/log text when the full window is at or below threshold. Reset the warned state after a successful claim so a later regression can signal again. Expose CLI knobs on `ddx work --watch` for window and threshold.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change verdict decoding, same-fingerprint escalation, shared schema files, README documentation beyond CLI help if needed, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not make low claim rate close, park, or mutate individual beads by itself. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
None.
title: Surface low claim-success-rate warnings from ddx work watch

description:
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
title: evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace

acceptance:
1. `TestWorkStartupReapsProjectScopedOrphanedExecuteBeadHarness` plants a fake execute-bead harness process associated with the current project/workspace whose recorded owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1, runs the startup reaper, and asserts the fake orphan process group is terminated within a bounded grace.
2. `TestWorkStartupReaperDoesNotKillLiveOwnedHarness` plants a fake execute-bead harness process associated with a live worker owner and asserts the reaper leaves it running.
3. `TestWorkStartupReaperDoesNotKillOtherWorkspaceHarness` plants a fake execute-bead-like harness associated with a different workspace/project and asserts it is not killed.
4. `TestWorkStartupReaperRecordsOperatorAttentionEvidence` verifies the cleanup records durable operator-visible evidence including bead or attempt identity when available, PID/process-group information, workspace scope, and diagnosis.
5. `TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree` or the existing graceful-shutdown regression guard remains green, proving the startup reaper did not replace or break normal executor cleanup.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
If a previous `ddx work` worker died abnormally before executor cleanup ran, execute-bead harness children can remain running under PID 1 and continue consuming harness sessions or anchoring leaked subprocess trees. Kernel parent-death signaling reduces future leaks on Linux, but prior or non-Linux orphans still need a bounded startup cleanup path.

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 only kills the harness process group from in-process cleanup paths.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 handles graceful os.Interrupt and SIGTERM but cannot handle SIGKILL/crashes, so no cleanup runs after abnormal parent death.
- There is no startup reaper in the `ddx work` registration/claim path that detects execute-bead harness processes whose owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a `ddx work` startup reaper, invoked before claiming/registering work, that identifies stale execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace and kills their process groups. The scope must prevent killing unrelated harness processes or harness children owned by live workers. Tie the event to existing lease-release/operator-attention style evidence so operators can inspect what was reaped, cross-referencing ddx-8f2e0ebf.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.
- Linux `Pdeathsig` executor hardening is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.
- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.
- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.
title: fix workers: reap stale execute-bead harness orphans on ddx work startup

acceptance:
1. Add `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesWaiversAppliedObjectScalarCriteria` proving classification-shaped readiness JSON with `"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"fixture"}]` decodes without an unavailable-readiness error, normalizes criteria to `[]string{"docs-only"}`, and still applies the classification result.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesWaiversAppliedObjectScalarCriteria' -count=1` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After `ddx-c9b6fb51` landed, the next live readiness check still produced a warn-only decode failure on 2026-05-15 while evaluating `ddx-5cc92777`: `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied.criteria of type []string`. The previous fix tolerated scalar `waivers_applied` and flat string arrays, but not a waiver object whose `criteria` field is a scalar string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:223-229` defines `preClaimReadinessWaiver.Criteria` as `[]string`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:272-279` unmarshals object entries directly into that strict struct before `normalizeReadinessStringList` can run, so legacy/model output like `{"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"..."}]}` fails the entire readiness check. Existing coverage at `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go:291-304` only covers object waivers with `criteria` already encoded as an array.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small custom unmarshal path for waiver object entries so `criteria` accepts either a JSON string or a JSON string array and normalizes to `[]string`. Keep `criteria` requested as an array in prompts and skill text; this is compatibility-only tolerance for legacy/model output. Do not loosen decisive readiness fields.

NON-SCOPE
Do not use waiver metadata for routing, power inference, ordering, or bead metadata. Do not add another readiness schema path. Do not change warn-only readiness policy, timeout behavior, or canonical readiness classifications.
title: readiness: tolerate scalar waiver criteria

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Operators need `ddx doctor` to surface which install layers are present, and the skill-install topology (global vs project, precedence) must be documented in the governing artifacts so the behavior is discoverable and durable.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:447 and cli/cmd/doctor.go:448 print the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines; these must render correct status for each layer (ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global) using ddxroot.GlobalDir() and ddxroot.Path().
- The skill-install topology is not yet captured in the governing artifacts: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027 (skill install topology), library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if present), and the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure `ddx doctor` renders two lines: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".
2. Land spec updates: ADR-027 skill-install-topology, library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md install-locations section, library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md agent-tier paths (if the file exists), and CLAUDE.md Persona System install-precedence note — all in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling bead: registry three-layer lookup).
- `ddx install`/`ddx update`/`ddx init` --global behavior (sibling beads).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestTryResult_PrintsLandingCoordinates|TestTrySideWorktreeLanding_PrintsNoRewriteRescueCommand' -count=1` passes. The tests prove a successful side-worktree `ddx try` output includes `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `project_root`, and a `git merge --no-ff <branch>` rescue command.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLandResult_CarriesResolvedTargetBranch|TestApplyLandResult_PropagatesTargetBranchToReport' -count=1` passes and proves the resolved branch from `Land()` reaches `ExecuteBeadReport` / `agent/try.Report`.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestLandConflictUnresolvableEventIncludesRescueCommand -count=1` passes and proves the parked-conflict event body includes `preserve_ref` plus a no-rewrite rescue command using `git merge --no-ff`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-05-15 Fizeau grendel-off routing incident, the operator had to run a targeted `ddx try fizeau-a54e0299` from a clean side worktree because the active fizeau checkout was dirty. The worker succeeded and printed `✓ fizeau-a54e0299 → merged (0d70176c)`, but that success was branch-local to the side worktree branch `ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516`. The active fizeau `master` checkout later advanced independently and still did not contain the route-time health fix. The operator had to inspect git history manually, notice that `master` lacked the landed branch, create a rescue branch, and run `git merge --no-ff ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516` to make the successful bead reachable from current project history.

This is not a model/routing bug. It is DDx operator UX and audit-safety around side-worktree landings. A successful `ddx try` must make the landing coordinates and recovery command unambiguous when the code lands in a worktree branch that is not the operator's active target branch.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/try.go:503-514` writes the terminal try result but only prints `bead`, `status`, `detail`, `result_rev`, and `preserve_ref`. It omits existing `ImplementationRev` / `LandedRev` fields and has no branch/worktree/rescue summary, so `merged` looks complete even when it landed on a side branch.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1056-1068` resolves the target branch internally, and `LandRequest.TargetBranch` documents the branch contract at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:88-90`, but `LandResult` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:111-130` does not carry the resolved target branch back to reports.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-430` already has `ImplementationRev` and `LandedRev`, and `cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-47` already mirrors them. The data exists but the CLI result surface does not expose enough to distinguish worker commit, landed branch tip, and branch-local landing.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:212-220` records unresolvable land conflicts with `preserve_ref`, but it does not attach an operator-ready rescue command such as creating a clean worktree and merging the preserve ref with `--no-ff`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add resolved landing metadata to the success path: `LandResult.TargetBranch`, and propagate it through `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, `agent/try.Report`, and the try adapter.
- Update `ddx try` output to always print `implementation_rev` and `landed_rev` when they differ, and to print `landed_branch` / `project_root` for landed attempts.
- When `ddx try` is run from a side worktree or non-default branch, print a clear follow-up block naming the branch-local landing and the no-rewrite rescue command: `git merge --no-ff <landed-branch>` from the desired target worktree, or a `git worktree add ... && git merge --no-ff ...` variant when the active checkout is dirty.
- For `land_conflict_unresolvable` / `land_conflict_operator_required`, include a rescue command in the bead event body and terminal detail using the preserve ref, not just the ref name.
- Keep merge-policy intact: do not squash, rebase, or rewrite execute-bead histories. The rescue path must preserve the worker commits and tracker/evidence commits.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider health, or grendel-specific behavior.
- Do not auto-merge side branches into another checked-out dirty worktree.
- Do not change `ddx work` queue ordering or bead priority policy.
- Do not rewrite existing execute-bead branch history.

INTERSECTIONS
- `ddx-06cbaa90` will eventually add a per-project worktree registry. This bead is narrower: expose/rescue the landing coordinates for today's in-tree mode.
- `ddx-c191e91a` covers stale worktree cleanup. This bead is not cleanup; it is successful-result discoverability and manual rescue guidance.
notes: Additional rescue observation: after creating rescue branch rescue/a54e0299-route-health-235101, the committed file /tmp/fizeau-a54e0299-rescue.235101/.ddx/beads.jsonl contains fizeau-a54e0299 with status=closed and closing_commit_sha=0d70176c, but running ddx bead show fizeau-a54e0299 from that rescue worktree reported status=open because the installed ddx command resolved state from the active/canonical fizeau checkout instead of the command worktree's committed .ddx file. The fix should print the state root/worktree used by try/bead commands, or otherwise make side-worktree state resolution explicit; this reinforces the need for branch/worktree landing coordinates in the terminal result.
title: try: surface side-worktree landings with rescue commands

acceptance:
1. `grep -rn 'FetchOriginAncestryCheck' cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go cli/cmd/try.go cli/internal/server/workers.go` returns either no callsites or callsites guarded by a once-per-process preflight outside the drain loop.
2. New test `TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop` (in `cli/cmd/work_test.go`) constructs a fixture repo with an unreachable origin (file:// URL pointing at a deleted path) and asserts that `ddx work --once` claims and drains a ready bead without error.
3. New test `TestBuildCLIPreClaimHook_DoesNotCallGitFetch` (in `cli/cmd/shared_helpers_test.go` or sibling) injects a stub `preClaimGitOps` and asserts that no method calling `git fetch` is invoked from the hook.
4. Existing ancestry-divergence behavior preserved: a fixture where local main has diverged from `refs/remotes/origin/main` (with origin still pointing at file://) still returns the same skip-with-reason result it does today.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/agent/... -run 'PreClaim|NetworkFree|Land' -count=1` green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Reliability principle P9 (docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md:71-87) states "`git fetch` and `git push` are never called from `Land()` or from the `ddx work` queue loop." The CLI pre-claim hook violates this every iteration: it shells `git fetch origin <branch>` before each claim from the drain loop, which couples the queue's forward progress to network reachability and origin availability. This is one of the recurring blockers operators see (`pre-claim hook blocked queue: ...`) when origin is unreachable or the local branch has diverged.

ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go:51-69` (`buildCLIPreClaimHook`) calls `gitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck(projectRoot, branch)` for every iteration of the drain loop.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:806` is the actual `git fetch origin <branch>` invocation inside `fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked` (called from `RealLandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck` at line 782).
- The hook is wired into `ddx work` at `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:544` (`PreClaimHook: buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps)`) and into `ddx try` at `cli/cmd/try.go:480`.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove the `git fetch` from the per-claim hot path. Two acceptable shapes:
  (a) Drop the fetch entirely from `buildCLIPreClaimHook` and rely on local ancestry against `refs/remotes/origin/<branch>` as last-observed; FEAT-023 `ddx sync` is the operator-driven refresh path.
  (b) Move the fetch to a one-time pre-drain preflight that runs at the start of `runWork`/`runTry` before the loop begins (still violates P9 for `try`, but `try` is a single-bead path so it's bounded).

Recommend (a). The ancestry check itself (comparing local SHA vs `refs/remotes/origin/<branch>`) can stay; only the fetch is forbidden.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go` (buildCLIPreClaimHook)
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` (FetchOriginAncestryCheck, fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked)
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1677` if the server-side preclaim has the same defect — audit and apply parallel fix.
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go` (new TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop)
- `cli/cmd/try.go:480` (callsite parity)

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `ddx sync` semantics.
- Do not remove the ancestry comparison itself — only the fetch.
- Do not touch the `Land()` post-claim git push path (P9 also forbids it but that's separate, file a sibling bead if it's still present).

REFERENCES
- Helix align audit run-id: helix-align-20260518T074735Z (R2)
- Reliability principles: docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md (P9)
title: agent: stop calling git fetch from buildCLIPreClaimHook; restore P9 network-free drain

description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-06-16 DDx repo canary after rebuilding/installing 764fe0084, worker worker-20260616T215629-cc7c claimed ddx-7f5092f2, completed readiness, then was killed before an execution attempt started. Its status.json records action=index-lock-violation with detail operation=index.commit outcome=removed reason="managed worker exited; no live owner". Its worker.log shows tracker_lock section=pre_dispatch_commits wait=0s hold=10.017s followed by "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully". .ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl records pre_dispatch_commits for holder_pid=680596 from 2026-06-16T22:01:42.882688293Z to 2026-06-16T22:01:52.89652651Z, duration_ms=10013. This violates the worker/server reliability goal: a pre-dispatch checkpoint must not cross the cap boundary, leave a stale index.lock, or require server kill/restart before execution begins.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:842-860 runs commitTrackerLocked and checkpointPreDispatchDirt together under withTrackerLock(projectRoot, "pre_dispatch_commits", ...), so tracker commit plus checkpoint/index sync can hold the parent repo mutation path long enough to hit cap-boundary behavior before the isolated attempt exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1702-1713 stages and commits tracker changes through runGitWithIndexLockRecovery without a pre-cap timeout proof for the pre-dispatch path.
- cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:260-299 performs temp-index checkpoint work and then runGitWithIndexLockRecovery(..., "read-tree", "HEAD") while still inside the pre_dispatch_commits mutation window.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:833-867 recovers .git/index.lock after managed-worker exit, but the canary shows this is happening after the worker is killed rather than preventing the cap-boundary failure.

PROPOSED FIX
Bound pre-dispatch tracker/checkpoint git operations so ordinary checkpoint commits do not run to the index.lock cap or cause a managed worker kill before execution. Prefer splitting tracker commit and checkpoint/index sync into separately bounded mutation windows, using a temp-index path that avoids parent index.commit where possible, or timing out before DefaultIndexLockCap with clean stale-lock recovery and an operator-visible retryable event. Preserve the pre-execute-bead checkpoint and tracker audit history.

NON-SCOPE
Do not raise DefaultIndexLockCap, disable lockmetrics violations, remove checkpoint commits, change durable-audit behavior already covered by ddx-15fdc1e1, or change plugin topology.

PARENT
- ddx-d5dfa702

DEPENDENCIES
- None.
title: agent: prevent pre-dispatch checkpoint commits from killing managed workers

description:
PROBLEM
Lock-cap enforcement may remove the wrong tracker-lock path from linked worktrees. The real main-git lock is resolved through `sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot)`, but `lockmetrics.lockPathFor("tracker.lock")` currently uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, ".git-tracker.lock")`, which can point at the linked checkout-local `.ddx` path instead of the shared primary workspace lock.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:101-116 resolves `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` through `sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot)` so linked worktrees converge on the same primary lock.
- cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:216-221 resolves the cap-enforcement path directly through `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, ".git-tracker.lock")`.
- cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap_test.go:142-154 only asserts that a tracker path contains `.git-tracker.lock`; it does not prove linked-worktree parity with the real lock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move or expose a shared tracker-lock path resolver so cap enforcement and `withMainGitLock` use the same path for `tracker.lock`.
- Add a linked-worktree fixture test that enables cap enforcement from a linked worktree and asserts the resolved cap path equals the actual main-git lock path.
- Preserve existing `index.lock` cap behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change cap durations or violation JSON schema in this bead.
- Do not redesign lockmetrics event storage.
title: lockmetrics: resolve tracker cap path through shared main-git root

description:
PROBLEM
Operators editing bead queues from the web UI cannot create or update beads on spoke-owned projects when running in hub mode — beadCreate and beadUpdate only ever hit the per-request local working directory, so writes targeting a spoke silently land on the hub or fail.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:12 returns a bead store rooted only at the per-request working directory, with no federation owner lookup.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:68 creates beads through that local store regardless of the target project's owning node.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:112 updates beads through that same local store.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:353 requires bead queue mutations to be project-scoped and owner-targeted in hub-mode.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340 defines TC-018 for cross-project bead queue mutations, including federated writes.

PROPOSED FIX
- In resolver_mutation_beads.go, resolve the target project's owning node before mutating: when the target is local (standalone or same-node) use the existing local store; when it is a spoke owned by another node, forward beadCreate and beadUpdate through federation owner forwarding.
- Preserve local project isolation so a write targeting project A never lands in project B.
- Return typed errors for stale state, non-owned targets, and offline/read_only spokes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new lifecycle states or dependency-edit UI beyond already exposed mutation surfaces.
- Lifecycle mutations (approve/block/cancel/reopen) and audit-metadata propagation are handled in the sibling lifecycle bead.
- No Playwright/UI wiring in this bead.

Governing artifacts: FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:353, TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340 (TC-018).
title: server: forward beadCreate/beadUpdate to federation owner with project isolation

description:
PROBLEM
Operators have no documented contract for the pre-claim intake, no mapping of failure classes (warn-only vs operator-attention escalation vs hard error), and no playbook for diagnosing silent-idle on a full queue. During the Fizeau idle, the warn payload's suggested_action ("check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry") actively misdirected the operator: the real fault was DDx's own decoder, not the route or harness config.

ROOT CAUSE
The README lacks a pre-claim intake section, so the readiness_checks verdict forms, the failure-class mapping, the warn-fingerprint escalation threshold, the claim-rate window flags, and the silent-idle diagnosis steps are undocumented.

PROPOSED FIX (parent AC5)
Add a "Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis" section to README.md documenting: (a) the readiness_checks contract including the canonical verdict forms (bool/string/null); (b) the failure-class mapping warn-only -> operator-attention -> hard error; (c) the consecutive-warn-fingerprint escalation threshold and the --claim-rate-window / --claim-rate-threshold flags; (d) a step-by-step silent-idle diagnosis playbook (how to tell a stuck full queue from a legitimately empty one via the agent-loop log and status surfaces).

DEPENDS ON
All four code children (decoder coercion, schema lock, warn-fingerprint escalation, claim-rate window) must land first so the documented contract, flags, and thresholds match shipped behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Any code changes to the decoder, schema, loop, or CLI (covered by the four sibling code children this depends on).

CROSS-REF
Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads. Adjacent silent-failure bugs documented alongside: ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf.
title: Document pre-claim intake contract, failure-class mapping, and silent-idle diagnosis in README

After make install and worker restart, /proc/<provider-pid>/environ for the DDx canary provider contains no DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, or DDX_WORKER_ID.
description:
PROBLEM
After ddx-292c2aa9, the restarted DDx canary still showed DDX_AGENT_NAME=worker-20260615T183748-0ae4 and DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID=worker-20260615T183748-0ae4 in live Claude/Codex provider subprocess environments under /proc/<pid>/environ. DDX_PROJECT_ROOT and inherited DDX_WORKER_ID were gone from the local executor path, but the Fizeau service path still inherited parent worker control env from the ddx work process. A provider subprocess that launches nested ddx commands can still identify as the server-managed worker and report against the wrong worker/project.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:322 called svc.Execute while the parent ddx work process still had DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, and DDX_WORKER_ID in os.Environ. Upstream provider launchers read process env directly, bypassing OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:306 and cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:123 copied runtime.Env into service metadata and RunArgs without filtering parent worker routing keys.

PROPOSED FIX
Before service dispatch, scrub DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, and DDX_WORKER_ID from the current process environment for the duration of svc.Execute and event drain, restoring them afterward. Also scrub dangerous routing keys from runtime env overrides before they reach service metadata or RunArgs, while preserving explicit DDX_WORKER_ID attempt correlation and DDX_MODE.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove DDX_AGENT_NAME or DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID from the server-managed ddx work parent process. Do not change ordinary CLI project-root env semantics outside provider subprocess boundaries. Do not change Fizeau provider selection.
title: agent: scrub server-managed worker env from Fizeau service dispatch

description:
PROBLEM: ddx bead import can persist dangling dependency edges even after detecting them, allowing imported beads to block readiness permanently when dependencies[].depends_on_id references a bead absent from both the destination tracker and archive.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: cli/internal/bead/import.go:281 detects dangling dependencies and emits a warning containing 'dangling dep (skipped)', but the import path appends the bead with the dangling dependency still present. The warning text is false because the dependency is not actually skipped.

PROPOSED FIX: Change the import path so detected dangling dependencies are filtered out of bead.Dependencies before the bead is appended or written. Keep a warning, but make it accurately report that the dependency was stripped and include an accurate stripped count in the import result/reporting surface used by tests.

NON-SCOPE: The doctor dangling_dep finding and Store.updateBead guard are already landed; do not redo them. Do not change readiness semantics here. Do not address merge-time dangling deps or worktree-prefix ID contamination in this child bead.

CONTEXT: Cross-machine convergence already relies on doctor --fix bumping updated_at and the supervisor union resolver keeping newest-updated_at records, so pruned records propagate. This child is only about stopping new phantoms at the import layer.
title: Strip dangling deps during bead import

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:636-642 bind-mounts ws.DockerRun->/ddx-runtime, the shared/per-attempt gocache->/work/.gocache, and work-tmp->/work/.tmp from host paths. These are invisible to a de-nested/remote daemon. They exist for HOME/tmp and cross-attempt Go cache reuse, not for host visibility.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace these host binds in dockerRunArgs with daemon-managed named volumes (--mount type=volume,src=<stable-name>,dst=...). Use a deterministic volume name keyed by project + cache role so cross-attempt cache warmth is preserved without any host path. Keep ws.DockerSharedGoCache semantics by mapping the shared cache to a shared named volume.

IN-SCOPE
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go (dockerRunArgs runtime/gocache/work-tmp mounts)
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_test.go

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- workspace/ddx binary (keystone bead)
- credentials (separate bead)

ACCEPTANCE

2. Add TestDockerRunArgs_UsesNamedVolumesForRuntimeAndCaches asserting /ddx-runtime, /work/.gocache, /work/.tmp use type=volume not type=bind.

2. TestBeadMergeVerboseReportsStageReadCommand proves verbose failure output includes stage number, repo root, and repo-relative path when a stage cannot be read.
3. The fixed helper uses the same path semantics as `git show :1:.ddx/beads.jsonl` from the repository root.

description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-06-13 dogfood merge recovery, `.ddx/beads.jsonl` had real Git conflict stages visible via `git ls-files -u` and `git show :1:.ddx/beads.jsonl`, but `ddx bead merge` failed with `read Git stage 1 for .ddx/beads.jsonl: exit status 128`. That forced abandoning the worktree instead of using DDx's supported tracker merge surface.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go registers `newBeadMergeCommand`, and the merge implementation reads Git conflict stages for `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. The observed error means the helper's stage-read command is not equivalent to the working `git show :1:.ddx/beads.jsonl` path in a linked worktree or conflict state. This violates AGENTS.md's rule to prefer supported `ddx bead` operations over direct tracker edits.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reproduce the conflict with a test repository that has stages 1/2/3 for `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in a linked worktree.
- Fix the Git stage reader to use a working-tree-root-relative path and the correct repository/worktree directory.
- Add verbose diagnostics that print the exact stage/path being read when stage access fails.
- Keep the merge command tracker-only; do not introduce ad hoc JSONL editing as recovery.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change bead merge semantics for non-conflicted files. Do not bypass Git conflict stages by reading arbitrary temporary files. Do not rewrite existing execute-bead history.
title: beads: repair merge conflict stage reader

description:
PROBLEM
After ddx-7ad31d91, the DDx server-managed canary on worker-20260615T173813-7db3 received a valid Claude readiness JSON with classification "ready" for ddx-cf0cafef. The rationale mentioned the historical blocker phrase "unknown classification executable" only to say it had been fixed. DDx still logged readiness schema drift: system_unready and appended an intake.warn event, so the canary did not reach a clean claim boundary.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:227 classifyReadinessSystemReason scans the full readiness detail for substrings such as "unknown classification". ClassifyReadinessWithMode calls it before preserving the normalized ready classification, so a historical rationale sentence that mentions a cleared schema-drift warning is misclassified as a fresh ReadinessSystemReasonSchemaDrift. The recorded canary payload in .ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-1781545113815822251.jsonl had classification "ready" and a positive rationale.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep schema-drift system reasons for actual unknown-classification decoder errors, but do not let historical schema-drift wording inside an otherwise canonical ready/needs_refine/needs_split/operator_required classification override the classification. Add a regression test with classification "ready" and rationale mentioning "unknown classification executable" as a cleared historical blocker; it must remain actionable and not system_unready.

NON-SCOPE
Do not suppress real routing/quota/transport/resource system reasons. Do not change provider routing or worker stop behavior.
title: agent: ignore historical schema-drift text in ready rationale

acceptance:
1. TestWorkDetectsAndCancelsSelfMatchingMonitorShells simulates an execute-bead attempt with a real child shell whose command includes `pgrep -f` matching its own command; after the useful child exits, DDx terminates only the stale monitor shell and the attempt continues.
2. TestAttemptBackgroundMonitorEvidenceRecordsCommandAndAge proves `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` contains a structured record with PID, command, age, action=terminated, and reason=self_matching_monitor or stale_monitor.
3. TestBackgroundMonitorCleanupDoesNotKillProviderProcess proves the primary provider process and unrelated same-user processes survive monitor cleanup.
4. TestWorkStatusSurfacesStaleAttemptChildren proves `ddx work status --json` includes stale/cleaned attempt-child counts or equivalent evidence while the attempt is active.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkDetectsAndCancelsSelfMatchingMonitorShells|TestAttemptBackgroundMonitorEvidenceRecordsCommandAndAge|TestBackgroundMonitorCleanupDoesNotKillProviderProcess|TestWorkStatusSurfacesStaleAttemptChildren' passes.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During dogfood of ddx-3b721804 on 2026-06-13, a Claude execute-bead attempt repeatedly spawned shell monitor commands such as `until ! kill -0 $(pgrep -f "lefthook run pre-commit") ...`. After the real `lefthook`/`go test` process exited, the watcher shells remained alive because `pgrep -f` matched the watcher command text itself. The worker made no progress until the operator killed only those watcher PIDs. DDx must not let provider/tool monitor children create an unbounded hidden stall.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-366 arms idle and tool-call timers around service events, but it does not track or bound shell children that remain alive after the useful tool process exits.
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1992-1996 tells agents to run tests and pre-commit, but the execute-bead contract does not prohibit self-matching background watchers or require bounded monitor commands.
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2010 tells the agent to stop after the commit, but DDx has no enforcement that stale background monitor shells are terminated and reported before the provider resumes.

PROPOSED FIX
Add attempt-scoped supervision for background shell/monitor children launched under the execution worktree. Detect stale monitor commands whose watched process has exited or whose command pattern matches themselves, terminate them without killing the main provider process, and record durable evidence. Expose the stale command count/oldest age in worker status or attempt evidence so operators do not have to inspect `ps`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not ban all background commands. Do not assume only Claude can produce this pattern. Do not rely on real provider CLIs in tests.
title: agent: supervise background monitor shells during execute-bead

acceptance:
1. `grep -nE 'Worktree resource expectations|resource expectations|build cache|CARGO_TARGET_DIR' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` returns matches for the new subsection.
2. `grep -nE 'environment passthrough|CARGO_TARGET_DIR|GRADLE_USER_HOME|toolchain cache' docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md` returns matches for the new subsection.
3. `git diff --stat HEAD~1 HEAD -- docs/` shows changes only under `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`, optionally `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`, and optionally `CLAUDE.md`.
4. `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` does not show any file under `cli/`.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: `ddx try` creates a fresh git worktree under
`~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attempt>/` per attempt
(FEAT-010 layer 2). For projects with native build toolchains (Rust + cargo,
JVM + gradle, Go + module cache, etc.), the fresh worktree starts with an
empty toolchain build cache, so the agent's first build inside is a cold
build from scratch. FEAT-010 §Layer 2 and TD-010 §Storage Model are silent
on this — they specify worktree creation and evidence capture but make no
statement about toolchain build-cache sharing or per-worktree I/O budgets.

OBSERVED COST (2026-05-28, niflheim project):
- A single in-flight `ddx try` attempt accumulated `target/` = 14 GB in the
  execute-bead worktree.
- Two concurrent workers + the project's own niflheim recovery run
  collectively wrote ~23 GB to the same SSD over ~30 minutes.
- The project's WAL recovery (read-bound, ~2 MB written) was visibly
  starved by worker cargo build I/O.
- Each fresh attempt repeats this cost — the worktree is destroyed at
  attempt end (or preserved with `--no-merge`), so the cache is lost.

ROOT CAUSE (spec-level): FEAT-010 treats worktree resource cost as a
non-concern. The feature explicitly lists "workspace creation, bead
attempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle" but lists no resource-budget
or cache-reuse hook surface. There is no ADR governing per-worktree I/O,
no documented hook for projects to opt into shared toolchain caches, and
no operator-visible counter that would surface this contention in
`ddx work status` or `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED EVOLUTION (the executing agent should choose how to slot these
into the artifact stack; below is the recommended shape):

1. **Amend FEAT-010 §Layer 2** (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`):
   - Add a "Worktree resource expectations" subsection acknowledging that
     execute-bead worktrees do not, by default, share native toolchain
     build caches with the parent checkout or with sibling attempts.
   - State the operator's responsibility (set per-project env like
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `GOPATH/pkg/mod`, etc., or
     project-level config) versus DDx's responsibility (pass through
     environment unchanged, do not invent toolchain-specific knobs).
   - Document that `ddx work` makes no concurrency guarantee for native
     build steps inside attempts; concurrent workers may contend.

2. **Amend TD-010** (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`):
   - Add a §Worktree environment passthrough subsection naming
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `MAVEN_OPTS`, `GOPATH`,
     `GOMODCACHE`, `npm_config_cache`, `PIP_CACHE_DIR`, etc. as
     environment variables that flow unmodified from the `ddx try`
     caller into the agent process inside the worktree.
   - Specify the invariant: DDx never sets, overrides, or unsets any
     toolchain cache env var. Operator/project owns the policy.

3. **Optionally add an ADR** (e.g., `ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`)
   recording the decision to keep build-cache policy outside DDx, with the
   rationale (cross-language, single-responsibility, prevents lock-in to a
   specific toolchain). If the executing agent decides one ADR is overkill,
   adding the same paragraph as a §Rationale block at the end of TD-010 is
   acceptable.

4. **Update CLAUDE.md "Execution Evidence Convention" section** if needed
   to cross-reference the new TD-010 environment-passthrough subsection
   so agents working in execute-bead worktrees know they may set
   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or equivalent) per project policy.

NON-SCOPE:
- Implementing build-cache mounting, sccache integration, or any
  toolchain-specific knob inside ddx itself. This bead is documentation
  only — it locks down the boundary so future implementation work has
  a clear policy to refer to.
- Changing `ddx try` worktree creation semantics or evidence capture.
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Adding `ddx doctor` or `ddx work status` instrumentation for disk
  contention (potential follow-up bead, not this one).
- Project-side fixes (niflheim has its own bead niflheim-e5cd7c69 for
  the cargo-target-dir workflow documentation).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md
- Optionally: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md
  (new) — only if the agent chooses the ADR path over TD-010 §Rationale.
- Optionally: CLAUDE.md (cross-reference paragraph only, no behavior change).

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- Any cli/ code — this is a docs-only evolution.
- Other FEAT/TD/ADR documents — touch only the listed artifacts unless
  the agent discovers a direct conflicting claim in another doc, in
  which case file a follow-up bead rather than expanding scope here.
title: specs: worktree resource-isolation expectations (build-cache reuse, I/O budgets) for ddx try/work
