description:
PROBLEM
Plan/work review can hang on Claude even when trivial Claude prompts work. A stalled review must not block implementation indefinitely or leave unmanaged claude processes behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The server-managed worker plan records four bounded Claude review attempts on 2026-06-13 that produced no output before timeout, while a trivial sonnet prompt returned. The same failure recurred after ddx was installed at afc8b6408 using timeout 180s claude -p --model sonnet --permission-mode plan against docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md. Existing worker cleanup in cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 and :1453-1457 only kills known worker process groups; review subprocesses launched outside that supervision are not represented as managed worker children.

PROPOSED FIX
Route review dispatches that can be part of ddx work/review gates through the same bounded supervisor used for managed workers. Every review attempt must have a wall-clock deadline, phase heartbeat/progress evidence, process-group ownership or child registration, and an operator_attention/review-error event on timeout. Timeout cleanup must kill the review process tree and leave no claude/codex descendants owned by that attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Do not make real Claude success required for unit tests. Do not change model routing names in this bead. Do not kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex sessions.
title: agent: bound review dispatches and clean hung Claude processes

acceptance:
1. TestCloseWithEvidence_AutoClosesParentEpicWhenLastChildCloses: an issue_type=epic parent with two children; closing child A via CloseWithEvidence leaves the epic open; closing child B closes the epic and records an epic_auto_close event.
2. TestCloseWithEvidence_DoesNotCloseEpicWithOpenChildren: one of two children closed leaves the epic open.
3. TestCloseWithEvidence_RejectedCloseDoesNotWalkUp: a child whose closure gate rejects the close does not transition the parent.
4. TestCloseWithEvidence_CancelledChildCountsTerminal: epic with one closed + one cancelled child auto-closes; TestCloseWithEvidence_AllCancelledChildrenCancelsEpic: epic whose children are all cancelled is set to cancelled (not closed).
5. FEAT-004-beads.md states the cancelled-counts-as-terminal rule in the closure-cascade section.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestCloseWithEvidence' green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: Epic auto-close exists but the autonomous factory bypasses it, so epics linger open forever during real drains. The walk-up-and-close logic (walkUpClosureCandidate, cli/internal/bead/store.go:1639-1697; entered via cascadeAndWalkUp at store.go:1588) is wired ONLY into the manual Store.Close path (store.go:1554). The path EVERY worker close uses — CloseWithEvidence (store.go:1939-1953), called from internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3059,3148, execute_bead_post_review.go:101,318, try/conflict_recovery.go:87,112 — does NOT call cascadeAndWalkUp. So when ddx work/ddx try closes the last child of an epic, the epic stays open. This violates FEAT-004 §Queue Semantics For Epics (docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:122-163), which mandates the closure cascade.

DECISION (operator-ratified): a CANCELLED child counts as terminal/done for epic rollup (cancelled = "work that will not run", types.go:72-73 — a deliberate terminal, never retried), matching the existing walkUpClosureCandidate non-terminal test (store.go:1651: status != closed && != cancelled). Refinement: if ALL children are cancelled (zero closed), the epic is CANCELLED, not closed-as-done. The dependency-DAG "does not satisfy dependents" rule is a separate axis and is unaffected.

ROOT CAUSE: the walk-up is hooked only into Store.Close, not CloseWithEvidence (store.go:1939).

PROPOSED FIX: In Store.CloseWithEvidence, after the bead confirms it transitioned to closed (existing b.Status == StatusClosed check ~store.go:1945), run the same single-hop walk-up Store.Close runs: visited := map[string]bool{id:true}; s.cascadeAndWalkUp(id, visited). Guard so a REJECTED close (gate refusal, bead still open) does NOT trigger walk-up. Add the all-cancelled-children -> cancel-the-epic refinement to walkUpClosureCandidate. Update FEAT-004:134-136 to state cancelled-counts-as-terminal explicitly.

NON-SCOPE: idle-loop cascade (separate bead); reconcile pass (separate bead); multi-hop recursion (separate dependent bead).
title: epic-autoclose: wire walk-up closure into CloseWithEvidence (factory close path) + ratify semantics

acceptance:
1. `unitTemplate` in cli/internal/service/systemd.go contains `StartLimitIntervalSec=` and `StartLimitBurst=` lines within the [Unit] section.
2. New test TestSystemdUnit_HasStartLimit in cli/internal/service/systemd_test.go installs the unit using the existing fake-systemctl harness (see TestSystemdInstall_RestartsActiveServiceAfterUnitRewrite at line 12 for the pattern), reads the written unit file, and asserts strings.Contains for both `StartLimitIntervalSec=` and `StartLimitBurst=`.
3. Existing tests TestSystemdInstall_RestartsActiveServiceAfterUnitRewrite and TestSystemdInstall_StartsInactiveServiceWithoutDoubleRestart still pass unchanged.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/service/... green
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes
description:
PROBLEM: On 2026-05-27 the installed ddx-server systemd user unit was found in a crash-loop with NRestarts/StartCounter at 4382. Its ExecStart was failing persistently (a stale singleton lock at ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock held dead pid 724, and the legacy unit also had a nonexistent WorkingDirectory). Because the unit sets Restart=on-failure + RestartSec=5 with NO start-limit, systemd restarted it forever (every 5s), spamming the journal and burning CPU, never giving up.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/service/systemd.go:15-31 — the `unitTemplate` constant defines Restart=on-failure (line 23) and RestartSec=5 (line 24) but declares no StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst in the [Unit] section. The systemd --user manager's default start-limit is not effective here, so a persistently-failing ExecStart loops indefinitely instead of entering `failed` state.

PROPOSED FIX: Add StartLimitIntervalSec= and StartLimitBurst= to the [Unit] section of `unitTemplate` (suggested: StartLimitIntervalSec=300, StartLimitBurst=5). Keep Restart=on-failure and RestartSec=5. After N failures within the interval, systemd enters `failed` (operator can `systemctl --user reset-failed ddx-server` after fixing the cause) instead of crash-looping.

NON-SCOPE: Do NOT modify singleton stale-lock detection in cli/internal/server/singleton.go (separate concern). Do NOT touch the launchd/macOS backend. Do NOT change WorkingDirectory or the XDG paths set by buildServerServiceConfig.
title: server systemd unit: add StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst so persistent startup failure stops crash-looping

acceptance:
1. TestWalkUpClosure_RecursesToGrandparentEpic: epic A -> epic B -> leaf child; closing the leaf auto-closes B then A in one close operation, each with its own epic_auto_close event.
2. TestWalkUpClosure_StopsAtOpenAncestor: a three-level chain where the grandparent has another OPEN child stops recursion at the grandparent (it stays open).
3. TestWalkUpClosure_CycleGuardTerminates: a malformed parent cycle does not infinite-loop (visited set bounds it).
4. FEAT-004 states the nested-epic convergence guarantee.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestWalkUpClosure' green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: walkUpClosureCandidate (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1639-1697) is single-hop by explicit design (comment store.go:1638: "Single-hop only; does not chase the grandparent"). An epic-of-epics (Diagnose has a dedicated ReasonEpicOfEpic, diagnose.go:106-116) whose last leaf closes will auto-close the immediate parent epic but leave the GRANDPARENT epic open until an unrelated second close happens to fire. Operator decision: closure must RECURSE the full ancestor chain so one leaf-close settles the entire nested-epic chain in a single operation.

ROOT CAUSE: the deliberate single-hop design in walkUpClosureCandidate (store.go:1683 closes the parent but never recurses to parent.Parent).

PROPOSED FIX: When walkUpClosureCandidate closes a parent epic (store.go:1683), RECURSE to parent.Parent using the existing visited set (store.go:1682) to bound recursion and prevent cycles. Apply the same cancelled-as-terminal + all-cancelled-cancels semantics at each level. Update FEAT-004 to state the nested-epic convergence guarantee (one child-close settles the full ancestor chain). Depends on the CloseWithEvidence walk-up bead so both the manual and factory close paths inherit recursion.

NON-SCOPE: idle-loop/reconcile beads (they converge via re-scan anyway, but the recursion makes them prompt).
title: epic-autoclose: recurse closure up the full parent chain for nested epics

acceptance:
1. TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal (durable_audit_test.go:172) gains cases "signal_killed_deadline" (...: signal: killed) and "tracker_lock_timeout" and asserts result.OperatorAttention is nil and result.ExitReason != "operator_attention".
2. New unit test TestIsTransientGitContention_SignalKilledAndDeadline asserts isTransientGitContention returns true for "... : signal: killed", "context deadline exceeded", "tracker lock timeout (...)", and an error wrapping context.DeadlineExceeded.
3. Regression preserved: TestWork_NonLockAuditCommitFailureStillStopsWorker (durable_audit_test.go:191) — an "insufficient permission" commit failure still sets OperatorAttention reason durable_audit_commit_failed.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal|TestWork_NonLockAuditCommitFailureStillStopsWorker|TestIsTransientGitContention' -count=1 green.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/gitlock/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (captured live): Two `ddx work --watch` workers on the same checkout /Users/erik/Projects/ddx collided. Worker 1 ran ddx-69a9ecd3 to completion (closed, 1h11m) but its durable-audit commit was SIGKILLed and the worker exited "operator attention required", leaving .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, .ddx/attachments dirty. Memory was healthy (31Gi free) — NOT OOM. Error: `commit durable audit outputs: committing durable audit outputs: : signal: killed`.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:88 wraps the WHOLE commit body (status/add/diff/commit) in context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second); that ctx flows to exec.CommandContext (cli/internal/git/exec.go:38). While the git commit was blocked behind the concurrent worker's hold on the shared main-git lock (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock via withTrackerLock, tracker_lock.go:133-152) / .git/index.lock, the 30s deadline fired and Go sent SIGKILL to git -> CombinedOutput() returns empty output + *exec.ExitError("signal: killed") (durable_audit.go:125-127). The classifier isTransientGitContention (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1026-1041) matches index.lock / "unable to write new index file" / "cannot lock ref" / "unable to update the ref" but NOT "signal: killed"/context-deadline, so finalizeDurableAuditOrStop (execute_bead_loop.go:1311-1336) sets OperatorAttention and exits. The commit is ALREADY idempotent on re-entry (durable_audit.go:98-122), so a retry would succeed — only the misclassification-driven EXIT blocks the idempotent re-run.

PROPOSED FIX: Make isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026) a SUPERSET that returns true for: (a) errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) and substrings "signal: killed" and "context deadline exceeded" (the SIGKILL/deadline class — defense-in-depth since the exec.ExitError from a ctx kill may not retrievably wrap context.DeadlineExceeded), AND (b) the "tracker lock timeout (...)" string from tracker_lock.go:283 (the lock-acquire-wait-exhausted class). This SUPERSEDES ddx-2189a07f (which adds only the tracker-lock-timeout string and is currently wedging on a routing issue) — folding both transient forms into one coherent classifier change avoids two beads racing to edit the same function. Keep all existing matches.

NON-SCOPE: changing the 30s deadline value or decoupling it from the lock wait (that is the sibling bead B2); worktree-leak cleanup (ddx-274943fc); classifier call sites other than the durable-audit branch.
IN-SCOPE FILES: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (classifier), cli/internal/agent/durable_audit_test.go (extend the transient table ~lines 175-185 via runAuditWithFinalizeErr).
title: execute-bead: classify SIGKILL/context-deadline durable-audit commit (signal: killed) + tracker-lock-timeout as transient, not operator-attention

2. One child bead filed per violation cluster (grouped sensibly); each child bead has explicit decision (WIRE or DELETE) with rationale.

description:
Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).

acceptance:
1. `TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks` asserts `ddx init --global` creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`.
2. `TestInitGlobal_WritesGlobalConfig` asserts `ddx init --global` writes `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.
3. `TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins` and `TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution` assert plain `ddx init` creates project state but does not write a project `plugins/` tree.
4. `TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal` asserts that when both paths have a plugin, the project copy wins.
5. `TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal` asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
6. `TestPluginLookup_BakedInDefaultOnly` asserts baked-in fallback is used only for the default `ddx` plugin.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... -run 'TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks|TestInitGlobal_WritesGlobalConfig|TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins|TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution|TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal|TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal|TestPluginLookup_BakedInDefaultOnly' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

First-time machine setup and runtime plugin lookup need a separate, testable implementation unit. `ddx init --global` should install the default `ddx` plugin globally and wire agent-tier skill links, while plain `ddx init` should create project state without installing plugins. Runtime lookup must prefer project installs, then global installs, then the baked-in default `ddx` plugin.

ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/cmd/init.go:73` owns the `ddx init` command path and must branch between global setup and project initialization.
- `cli/cmd/init.go:132` creates project `.ddx/` state and must not materialize plugin content in plain project init.
- `cli/cmd/init.go:314` is the global setup hook that must install the default plugin under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, link skills, and write global config.
- `cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:25` is the runtime plugin lookup boundary and must encode project > global > baked-in precedence.
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:70` and `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90` define the project and global roots this behavior depends on.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify `ddx init --global` as the umbrella machine setup command: materialize the bundled default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, create `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into that plugin tree, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults. Keep plain `ddx init` project-only: create `<project>/.ddx/` and project config, but do not write `plugins/`; plugin content lazy-resolves through runtime lookup. Runtime lookup must prefer `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` over global, and only fall back to baked-in content for the default `ddx` plugin.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a.
- Depends on ddx-06cbaa90: project lookup uses `ddxroot.Path()` and global lookup uses `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/`.
- ADR-027 skill install topology or equivalent must document the precedence.

PARENT
- ddx-602ae31a

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely; global and per-project modes coexist.
- Migrating `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths if any exist.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex-facing `.agents/skills`; add others only when those harnesses define their skill paths.
title: init and registry: resolve project, global, and baked-in plugin layers

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The ddx skill is the gateway most interactive agents read before deciding what to do. Without explicit mode guidance there, the agent can still interpret broad queue questions as implementation requests even after AGENTS.md and specs are updated.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92 and ddx-3c6ca63f.
notes: SUPERSEDED by ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238. The interactive-steward routing bead now owns the first-stop interactive.md work, existing status/work/review reference cleanup, route fixtures, eval-driver schema update, and shipped-copy verification. Do not dispatch this bead independently.
title: ddx skill: route broad prompts to queue stewardship

Move to Drain startup: run once before the for-loop. If it fails, return one worker-level execution_failed record and exit (legitimate startup failure). Never run again per iteration.

INTERSECTIONS

- P4: a preflight failure on one bead must not affect later beads.

2. The comment at lines 438-440 explains the 1h cap, references the wedge-detection rationale, and notes that idle timer (2h) and wall-clock (1h) are separate triggers.
3. TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured wall-clock budget (use a much shorter budget for the test, e.g., 200ms, to keep it fast).

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/types.go:441 sets DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000
  (3 hours). This is the per-attempt absolute wall-clock cap on a
  harness invocation. Two drain wedges observed in the 2026-05-09
  drain consumed the full 3 hours before timing out:

    ddx-c113a116: silent from 16:21 to 19:13:54 (3h0m0.018s),
                  then "do response complete 8472 tok in 3h0m0.018s,
                  0.8 tok/s" → timeout: context deadline exceeded.

    ddx-94a368b6: silent from 19:36 onward (drain killed before
                  natural timeout fired).

  3 hours is too long: a wedged worker burns operator time and budget
  before the timeout fires. 1 hour catches the same wedge class
  before they accumulate substantial cost.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441
        DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
  - Comment at line 438: "Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout
    — so normal long tasks still complete while genuinely hung workers
    free themselves."
  - The 1.5x ratio against the 2h idle timeout (DefaultTimeoutMS at
    line 434) is preserved by halving both proportionally OR by
    halving wall-clock alone (idle timer protects against true silence
    independently).

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441: change DefaultWallClockMS to
    3600000 (1 hour).
  - Update the comment at line 438 to reflect the 1h cap and explain
    the wedge-detection rationale (citing this bead).
  - DO NOT change DefaultTimeoutMS (idle timer); it remains at 2h,
    which now exceeds wall-clock — but that's fine, idle and
    wall-clock are separate triggers (whichever fires first wins).
    Document this in the comment.
  - Verify nothing in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:45-46 or
    cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:158-159 hardcodes the 3h value
    elsewhere; they should pull from DefaultWallClockMS.
  - Add a regression test: TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget
    in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go (or extend an existing
    test) that asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured budget.

NON-SCOPE
  - Per-tool-call timeout (sister bead handles that as a structural
    prevention; this bead is the quick symptom guard).
  - Loop detection via stream-json watcher (separate concern).
  - Removing the underlying source of long Bash calls (sister beads
    ddx-1fda33d2, ddx-150758e7, ddx-f1c476c3 reduce per-push test
    cost; ddx-1f774edf removes per-bead pushes entirely).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent: per-tool-call wall-clock for
    Bash..." — structural prevention; this bead is the immediate
    symptom mitigation.
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf — once landed, drain stops pushing per
    bead, eliminating the 168s test tax that fuels wedges.
notes: Cancelled per user direction 2026-05-09: wall-clock cap is wrong policy. Reducing 3h to 1h would kill legitimately-hard-but-still-active work along with wedges. Right policy: detect 'no real progress' (loops + true idle) rather than capping absolute wall-clock. See sister bead ddx-1c3cad6f (reshaped to cover loop detection + meaningful-event idle reset).
title: fix(agent): reduce DefaultWallClockMS from 3h to 1h (faster wedge detection)

description:
PROBLEM
The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.
- Axon server-side schema work.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx-0793ac75` is stale. The current read-federation coverage in `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation_test.go:77-80,315-320,474-487` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already proves the hub/spoke read path, but `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-350` still leaves owner-targeted writes planned. The bead still points at cancelled `ddx-387a0178`, so the queue treats it as dependency-waiting instead of narrowing it to the remaining write-routing gap.

ROOT CAUSE
`TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-350` splits the work into TC-017 worker control and TC-018 bead writes, while the current test tree already covers the read side (`resolver_federation_test.go:77-80,315-320,474-487` and `federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307`). The stale dependency edge to cancelled `ddx-387a0178` keeps the bead parked on work that can no longer execute.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reconcile `ddx-0793ac75` against current coverage and either close/supersede it or rewrite it so it names only the missing owner-write coverage.
- Remove the blocking dependency on cancelled `ddx-387a0178` unless a note proves it is still load-bearing.
- Preserve the FEAT-026 / Story 14 references and explicitly cite the current read coverage in `resolver_federation_test.go` and `federation-2node.spec.ts`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement new federation code.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- Do not rewrite execution-audit commits.
- Do not change the live read-federation behavior.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-a038a090`. Dependency to reconcile: `ddx-387a0178`.

Per the TD from B_TD: implement the axon backend in cli/internal/bead/backend_axon.go (or per the existing backend.go shape). Subscription support: implement a subscription client (genqlient supports subscriptions via separate library, or use a simple WebSocket GraphQL subscriptions client) so that DDx server can subscribe to Axon ChangeEvents on ddx_beads / ddx_bead_events for live UI updates.

title: Sample: write tests

description:
# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:server, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-002, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049 (axon backend prototype umbrella).
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (interface refinement, which adds `BeadReader` and the LifecycleSubscriber ctx-aware signature).

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.
2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.
3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.
4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.
5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.
6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.
7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.
8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).
- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.
- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.
- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.
- Frontend display of stop reasons.
- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.

acceptance:
1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
5. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
6. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
7. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.
8. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
9. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
10. ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.
11. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
13. Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this lands.
notes: Superseded by ddx-eaf02420 and ADR-027 (2026-06-15 amendment). Do not execute the older global/home-directory install model; forward plugin topology is project lock metadata + XDG cache payloads + generated adapters, with baked-in fallback only for the default ddx package.
title: skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and ddx plugin commands honor both

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-ca5cbafb is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Observed live on 2026-06-29. Found 28 orphaned codex processes (ppid=1, parented to systemd) on this host, ages 50min to 2 days, each consuming ~90% CPU. The current ddx worker (pid 4014720) only owned 1 active codex; all 28 others were ghosts from prior ddx worker incarnations that died without reaping their codex children. Each orphan also had a still-running `exo-open --launch WebBrowser https://auth.openai.com/oauth/authorize?...` child stuck on the OAuth callback (codex hadn't received the auth completion, so it kept the spawn alive).

Cumulative effect: load average sustained at 50-80 (on 8-core host) for >24h, choking every ddx work attempt and producing repeat `transport: exit status 1` outcomes because the new codex child gets starved/killed before it can complete the durable_audit phase. Operator must manually `kill -TERM` the orphans to recover.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The executor at cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 sets `Pdeathsig: SIGTERM` and Setpgid for direct harness children. Inspect this path: either (a) codex specifically is forking a detached daemon that escapes the original process group, or (b) the `ddx work --watch` foreground path uses a different executor (not OSExecutor) for the codex spawn, so the Pdeathsig is never wired in.
- The pre-claim cleanup hook landed in ddx-6ee2b0c3 (`cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go`) scans for DDx-owned stale processes by cwd/worktree ownership. Once codex reparents to systemd it loses its DDx-execution-worktree cwd marker (codex's actual cwd may stay valid but the lineage check may rely on parentage). Investigate `staleAttemptProcessScanner` in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go — what makes a process "DDx-owned"? If it requires PID lineage that doesn't survive reparenting, this is the gap.
- The closed beads ddx-0d7d5f0e (parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness on Linux) and ddx-3113d524 (docker-clone backend group cleanup) and ddx-418c3849 (work startup reap) cover sibling cases but evidently do not cover the `codex --no-alt-screen` invocation path executed by the current `ddx work --watch` foreground worker against Fizeau-routed providers.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Wire `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` (Linux) at every site that spawns a provider subprocess from the watch worker — not just OSExecutor — so the kernel kills the child when its immediate paren
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the remaining bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: duplicate of ddx-ca5cbafb / decomposition artifact
title: ddx work: codex provider subprocesses orphan to PID 1 when worker dies,: part 2

acceptance:
1. A ddx maintainer confirms or refutes the root-cause hypothesis (isolated worktree missing a gitignored input path) against ddx try's actual implementation.
2. Some AC-command-verification gate (either automatic exact-command re-run, or a required explicit executing-agent acknowledgment before declaring success) exists so a bead cannot close task_succeeded while its own literal AC command is failing.
3. A regression test reproduces the cayce-8acffc83 shape: an attempt whose own newly-added test file asserts something the attempt's own diff didn't actually deliver.
description:
PROBLEM: A bead executed via `ddx try`/`ddx work` can land, close with outcome=task_succeeded, and be merged -- while the exact test command its own acceptance criteria named as a required gate is left failing on the merged result.

CONCRETE EXAMPLE (project: cayce, bead cayce-8acffc83, "Microsite: render Tier 1 real-agent-conversation demos from IP-008 captures"): the bead's AC required `cd website && npm run test:e2e` to pass. It closed task_succeeded. Independently re-running that exact command on the merged commit showed 4 real failures -- two Playwright specs asserting content on two new pages the bead's own diff added references to (website/e2e/sections.spec.ts, website/e2e/visual-snapshots.spec.ts), because the two referenced pages (website/content/demos/basic-single-send.md, website/content/demos/multi-step-sequence.md) were never actually generated and committed. The bead built the renderer script (website/scripts/render-flow-demos.py) and its own unit tests using isolated fixtures (which passed), and separately updated a landing page to link the two pages that don't exist, but the actual generate-and-commit step for the real deliverable never happened.

ROOT CAUSE (most likely): `ddx try` runs each attempt in an isolated worktree. The renderer needed `target/cayce-agent-flow-transcripts/*.json`, a project-gitignored build artifact that only existed in the requesting session's own long-lived main checkout (from manual verification runs earlier in that session), not in the fresh isolated worktree the attempt actually ran in. The executing agent had no real input to render its actual deliverable against, so it could only validate via isolated-fixture unit tests -- and apparently never treated "the exact AC-named integration test command actually passes against my own worktree's real build" as a precondition for declaring success, or didn't notice its own newly-added Playwright specs were failing before finishing.

IMPACT: an operator (or a supervising agent) who trusts a closed/task_succeeded status without independently re-running the bead's own AC commands will ship broken links / failing test suites to production. This defeats a core value proposition of AC-gated execution.

PROPOSED FIX (not investigated in depth -- filed by an external observer, not a ddx maintainer):
- Before a `ddx try` attempt reports success, if the bead's acceptance criteria contains a literal, extractable shell command (the "command"/"mechanical" AC categories `ddx bead lint` already recognizes), consider re-running that exact command against the attempt's own resulting worktree state as a final gate, not just whatever ad hoc verification the executing agent chose to run.
- Separately/alternatively: when an attempt's worktree lacks a gitignored path a script under test needs (detectable if the script's own execution fails with a clear "file/dir not found" error), surface that as a structural blocker to the executing agent rather than letting it silently fall back to isolated-fixture-only validation without flagging the gap to the operator.

This report is filed from observed behavior in one external project's session, not from reading ddx's own source -- the actual root cause and fix should be confirmed against `ddx try`'s real implementation.
title: Beads can close task_succeeded while their own named AC test gate is left failing

description:
PROBLEM
The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.

acceptance:
1. Child bead for root SIGINT/SIGTERM context and user-facing shutdown message exists and is closed.
2. Child bead for interrupted attempt cleanup, unclaim, liveness cleanup, and regression tests exists and is closed.
3. `ddx work` and `ddx try` both print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` on first Ctrl-C/SIGTERM.
4. Interrupted non-terminal beads are open/re-claimable after cleanup and are not parked behind `work-retry-after`.
5. Second-signal hard-abort behavior is documented and tested or explicitly documented as best-effort.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestSignal|TestInterrupt|TestCancel|TestShutdown|TestDisrupted|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators routinely stop long-running `ddx work` / `ddx try` with Ctrl-C. The governing specs now require the first SIGINT/SIGTERM to cancel work cooperatively, print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`, preserve available evidence, release non-terminal claims, and leave unfinished beads re-claimable. The implementation beads need to land as one coherent interrupt story.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/main.go:31 calls cmd.Execute, and cli/cmd/root.go:31 calls rootCmd.Execute without a signal-backed context, so terminal SIGINT/SIGTERM kills the process instead of driving cooperative cancellation.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-40 documents signal as a stop condition, but there is no root signal handler that turns Ctrl-C into that condition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 can classify context cancellation as disrupted, but normal Ctrl-C does not currently reach this path.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:423-434 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:177-190 now define the intended first-signal behavior and user-facing message.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate two implementation slices: root CLI signal context/user messaging, and work/try attempt cleanup/reclaim tests. When both children close, verify `ddx work` and `ddx try` behave consistently under Ctrl-C.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement resumable worktrees by default. Interrupted work may be preserved as evidence, but the safe default is cleanup and retry from scratch.
- Do not change queue ordering, routing policy, review policy, or cost-cap policy.
- Do not remove legacy work/execute-bead compatibility commands.
notes:
RELATED QUEUE ALIGNMENT
- ddx-9228a484 (StopCondition enum) now depends on this epic so Signal semantics are implemented after first-signal cooperative cancellation is defined.
- ddx-9f20a4bd (external claim liveness) intersects with interrupted heartbeat/liveness cleanup; do not reintroduce beads.jsonl heartbeat writes while implementing the cleanup child.
- ddx-eccc6efb (server-spawn ddx work subprocess) should rely on the same root signal behavior once server workers exec ddx work; keep server-specific SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation as a supervisor layer, not a second queue-drain implementation.
title: work: graceful interrupt shutdown for work and try

acceptance:
1. Add a regression test where a `ddx work --watch` attempt subprocess exits/cancels mid-implementation; the bead is unclaimed or closed with committed tracker evidence, and `ddx work status --json` reports no stale active work when no worker is alive.
2. Add a regression test where an implementation produces no product commit but DDx records success; DDx must classify it as `already_satisfied` with explicit rationale/evidence or leave the bead open/unclaimed, not silently close as generic success with only metadata.
3. Add a regression test for stale `.ddx/run-state/*.json` plus no live worker: startup cleanup or status reconciliation removes/ignores stale active work so the queue exposes ready beads again.
4. Add a test or guard proving pre-claim readiness/lifecycle dispatch does not mutate the project root; if mutation is intentionally blocked, the warning includes a stable code and does not repeat as operator noise for every bead.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/workerstatus/... -run "Work|RunState|Readiness|Lifecycle|Status" -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
Incident observed in sibling repo `/home/erik/Projects/axon` on 2026-06-13 while running `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s --request-timeout 45m` from Codex.

Symptoms:
1. Two background launches of `ddx work --watch` exited immediately with empty logs: PID 819208 `/tmp/axon-ddx-worker-20260612-232505.log`, then PID 819691 `/tmp/axon-ddx-worker-20260612-232537.log`. `ddx work plan --json` showed a valid next bead, so the queue was not empty.
2. Foreground `ddx work --once --request-timeout 45m` succeeded on Axon bead `axon-5c6af8f0`, proving the route/queue could execute.
3. Foreground `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s --request-timeout 45m` repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness warnings: `readiness route unavailable: lifecycle dispatch: project root mutation rejected for bead <id> (continuing)`. Seen for Axon beads `axon-5c6af8f0`, `axon-7e75384c`, and `axon-a8e564c1`. The warning was recorded as `intake.warn` with `rule_id=pre_claim_intake.system_unready`.
4. For Axon bead `axon-7e75384c`, watch/attempt output included `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`; DDx recorded `status: success` and closed the bead even though the landed implementation commit trail contained only execution evidence / harness metadata, no product code diff. The acceptance tests happened to pass independently, but `.ddx/beads.jsonl` closure was left dirty and had to be committed manually (`7351979f`).
5. For Axon bead `axon-a8e564c1`, the watch session exited with code -1 during implementation after reading CI/package files. After exit, `ddx work status --json` reported no live workers but still reported active work from `.ddx/run-state/20260613T034907-3d00f60f.json`; the bead remained open/claimed and queue status was `ready=0`, `worker_ready=0`, `active_work.count=1`. That jams the queue until an operator manually unclaims/cleans/restarts.

Expected behavior: if the worker process exits or its harness subprocess is cancelled, DDx should either complete/close with committed tracker evidence, or release the bead and clear stale active run-state. `ddx work --watch` should not leave the project with zero workers, a claimed open bead, stale active run-state, and no ready work. The readiness route should not attempt a project-root mutation during pre-claim analysis, or the warning should include a precise non-blocking explanation and not recur on every bead.

Reproduction context from Axon:
- project: `/home/erik/Projects/axon`
- ready beads: `axon-5c6af8f0`, then `axon-7e75384c`, then `axon-a8e564c1`
- stale files after crash: `.ddx/run-state/20260613T034907-3d00f60f.json`, `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-1781322253776691221/status.json`
- command: `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s --request-timeout 45m`

In-scope files: DDx worker/watch loop, run-state/liveness cleanup, pre-claim readiness/lifecycle dispatch, and tests around process exit/cancel handling.
Out-of-scope: Axon product code, model routing policy, and general host process cleanup not tied to DDx run-state.
notes: Additional Axon evidence from 2026-06-14: after manually implementing and verifying Axon bead axon-cd532283, ddx bead close axon-cd532283 produced no output for roughly 93 seconds before exiting 0. This repeated the prior slow-close behavior seen on Axon tracker-only closes. The command eventually updated the tracker, but the silent delay is operationally ambiguous for agents because it looks like a stuck close while holding the tracker workflow.
title: fix: keep ddx work watch from leaving stale active run-state

acceptance:
1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when both the project plugins/<name> dir and ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> exist, ResolvePlugin returns the project path with layer=="project".
2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer=="global".
3. A test asserts ResolvePlugin returns layer=="baked-in" with empty path for name=="ddx" when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name.
4. The skill-install-topology ADR file exists and documents the project→global→baked-in precedence; the "Persona System" precedence text in CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md matches it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2 — "Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows")

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-local and machine-global). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy, fall back to the global copy, and only then use the baked-in default ddx plugin. This resolution function is the foundation every install/update/doctor surface depends on, so it is split out first.

ROOT CAUSE
- The resolution helper lives at cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)`. It must return path + layer ("project"|"global"|"baked-in") with the documented precedence and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name. The project path is computed via `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` (resolve.go:30) which already handles in-tree vs convention mode; the global path via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (resolve.go:35). Baked-in is valid only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:40).
- The governing decision (skill install topology) and the precedence documentation in CLAUDE.md must match this behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin` implements precedence project → global → baked-in exactly, returning empty path for the baked-in layer (callers use defaultplugin.FS()).
2. Land/refresh the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md or the equivalent ADR referenced by CLAUDE.md) describing the three-layer precedence.
3. Confirm the "Persona System" sections of CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md document the precedence (project-local → ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx → baked-in default).

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update CLI surface (sibling child).
- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on before this lands.
title: registry: plugin lookup precedence project→global→baked-in + skill-install-topology spec

acceptance:
1. `TestLandEvidenceCommit_IndexLockCapDoesNotLeaveStaleLock` or the nearest existing land/evidence test name simulates a capped/slow evidence `git commit` and asserts no stale `.git/index.lock` remains after recovery.
2. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_IndexCommitLockViolationReleasesAndContinues` or the nearest existing loop/lockmetrics test name proves the worker records the lock violation, releases/retries or parks cleanly, and a subsequent tracker-only commit can proceed without manual lock removal.
3. The fix distinguishes verified-stale lock cleanup from live-owner locks; tests cover the live-owner case is not removed.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/lockmetrics/... -run 'IndexLockCap|LockViolation|LandEvidence|StaleLock' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the same live canary window, `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` recorded an `index.lock` hold-time violation for `operation=index.commit`: duration 10027ms against the 10000ms cap. After the server restart/worker claim cycle, `.git/index.lock` remained with no live owner and blocked a tracker-only commit until manually verified with `lsof` and removed. This violates the lock lifetime contract and is a direct blocker to reliable server-managed workers.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:990-1003` documents `landEvidence` as a short ref-advancing commit boundary, then calls `CommitStaged` to create the trailing evidence commit.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1458-1460` runs `landEvidence` inside `withMainGitLock(projectRoot, "land_post_land_finalize", ...)`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:772-780` implements `CommitStaged` with a real `git commit -m ...` subprocess.
- `cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:18-24` treats `index.lock` holds over 10s as hung and force-releases the lock. When the cap fires during the `git commit` subprocess, DDx can record the violation but still leave a stale `.git/index.lock` / interrupted commit state behind.

PROPOSED FIX
Make evidence finalization resilient to capped index commits. Either split the expensive evidence `git commit` out of the over-strict critical section or add explicit recovery/retry around cap-triggered commit interruption. The worker must remove only verified-stale `.git/index.lock` files, record durable violation evidence/operator diagnostics, and leave the landing worktree resumable so the next tracker-only or evidence commit succeeds without manual cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not raise the default 10s cap as the only fix.
- Do not remove lock metrics or suppress cap violations.
- Do not change unrelated land-coordination failure classification beyond the stale-lock recovery path.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`.
Deps: none. Related to the closed lock-contention work, but this is a newly observed stale-lock recovery failure from the Claude TUI canary.
title: agent/land: recover stale index locks after capped evidence commits

acceptance:
1. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
2. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
3. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
- TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
- TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Review-block and post-review escalation still use the old power-hint metadata path. TD-037 requires escalation decisions to be execution policy/evidence, not durable bead difficulty or power metadata.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:633` writes `TriagePowerHintKey` for `ActionEscalatePower`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:9` aliases `TriagePowerHintKey` to the old escalation hint key.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove review-block and post-review writes to `TriagePowerHintKey`. Keep operator-required cleanup of stale legacy hint keys if needed, but do not create or rely on bead power hints for escalation. Record escalation decisions in events/evidence and let dispatch policy use current attempt state, not bead metadata.

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go`

description:
PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

description:
PROBLEM
Operators currently keep DDx queue-drain workers alive with tmux windows, shell jobs, or direct ddx work processes. That makes it hard to monitor, restart, stop, reconcile, and clean worker process trees across helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as an in-process goroutine manager. cli/internal/server/workers.go:361 starts server-owned workers, but desired worker count and restart policy are not durable. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214 can only kill a process group when WorkerRecord.PID is set, which is often zero for goroutine workers. cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1931 and :3271 expose start/stop, not durable desired-state supervision.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md as a sequence of child beads: desired-state reconciliation, managed process cleanup, API/CLI/UI controls, Docker no-leak verification, review timeout supervision, existing worker-test regression repair, durable audit ignored-path repair, and dogfood migration.

NON-SCOPE
Do not make bead claiming server-authoritative. Do not adopt arbitrary already-running external ddx work processes as fully managed. Do not change cross-host federation or Fizeau routing semantics.
title: server: supervise project workers from durable desired state

description:
PROBLEM
go test -short -race ./internal/server fails deterministically (3/3 runs) with a data race in cli/internal/server/provider_probe_cleanup.go package globals. Independent of any feature work; red at base HEAD 6c6be0b13 (verified by stashing unrelated changes). It blocks the lefthook pre-commit go-test gate for ANY change touching internal/server.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go, deferred cleanupCurrentProcessProviderProbesSettled) and cleanupCurrentProcessProviderProbes (cli/internal/server/provider_probe_cleanup.go:45,50) schedule background follow-up sweep goroutines via scheduleProviderProbeFollowupCleanup (provider_probe_cleanup.go:78-93) that outlive the test that started them. Three tests reassign the package globals reapCurrentProcessProviderProbes, providerProbeCleanupFollowupDelays, providerProbeCleanupSettleQuiet/Deadline/Interval (cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:217,259,263,293,303-306) and the leaked goroutines read them concurrently -> WARNING: DATA RACE.

PROPOSED FIX
Bound the follow-up/settle cleanup goroutines to a cancelable lifetime owned by the WorkerManager (so they cannot outlive the test/manager), OR guard the mutable provider-probe globals + func hooks behind a sync.RWMutex with getter/setter helpers used by both the read sites and the three tests. Either approach must eliminate the race without changing production provider-probe reaping behavior.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change managed-worker process-tree termination (ddx-24a3da84). Do not change which providers are reaped, only the concurrency-safety of the test hooks / goroutine lifetimes.
title: server: eliminate provider-probe cleanup data race from leaked runWorker goroutines
