acceptance:
1. TestWorkRequestTimeoutCancelsActiveToolSession uses a fake service/provider that emits regular tool events past a 2s request timeout; DDx cancels at the absolute timeout instead of resetting the cap on activity.
2. TestRequestTimeoutReapsProviderAndAttemptChildren proves the provider process and its attempt-scoped children are gone after timeout and process-cleanup evidence is written.
3. TestRequestTimeoutEvidenceIncludesWallClockFields proves evidence includes configured_timeout, elapsed, phase, provider_pid, cleanup_result, and reason=request_timeout.
4. TestServerManagedWorkerForwardsRequestTimeoutToAbsoluteCap proves the server worker path in cli/internal/server/workers.go honors the same cap.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWorkRequestTimeoutCancelsActiveToolSession|TestRequestTimeoutReapsProviderAndAttemptChildren|TestRequestTimeoutEvidenceIncludesWallClockFields|TestServerManagedWorkerForwardsRequestTimeoutToAbsoluteCap' passes.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During dogfood of ddx-3b721804 on 2026-06-13, the worker was started with `ddx work --once --request-timeout 25m`, but the primary Claude provider process stayed alive for roughly 56 minutes while tool activity and watcher stalls continued. The operator expectation for `--request-timeout` is a bounded provider/attempt wall-clock cap with cleanup. DDx currently allowed the provider leg to run more than twice the configured timeout, which makes unattended burn-down unreliable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:24-33 documents `DefaultProviderRequestTimeout` and says `--request-timeout` applies to execute-bead/work one-off debugging.
cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:112-125 resolves and forwards `ProviderTimeout` into the service request.
cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-366 resets idle/tool-call timers on service events, but there is no absolute wall-clock timer that cancels a provider session that remains active through endless tool events.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:734-787 forwards server worker request timeout into loop overrides, so server-managed workers will inherit the same ambiguity unless the boundary is made explicit and tested.

PROPOSED FIX
Make `--request-timeout` an absolute provider-session wall-clock cap for execute-bead/work attempts, distinct from idle-read and per-tool-call timers. On expiry, cancel the provider context, reap the attempt process tree, release/park the lease according to existing timeout policy, and write durable evidence naming configured_timeout, elapsed, phase, provider PID, and cleanup result.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove idle-read timeout or tool-call timeout. Do not call real providers in tests. Do not make long-running project tests impossible; if a longer cap is needed, the operator can set one explicitly.
title: agent: enforce request-timeout as provider wall-clock cap

description:
PROBLEM
Stop currently performs ad hoc cleanup inside WorkerManager.Stop, releasing the claimed bead and then killing only the recorded pid when pid > 0. That path does not prove that a managed worker tree with fake claude/codex descendants is fully terminated, and the cleanup logic is not centralized for reuse by other lifecycle paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1312 contains Stop, including the current pid-gated terminateProcessGroup call.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234-1286 computes pid and only escalates when pid > 0, so cleanup is limited to the recorded leader process.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1298-1312 finalizes the worker after the ad hoc kill path, which makes idempotence depend on repeated copies of the same logic rather than a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a shared managed-worker tree cleanup helper that sends SIGTERM to the managed process group, waits WatchdogKillGrace, then SIGKILLs any remaining descendants.
- Make Stop call that helper for server-managed workers before terminalizing the record.
- Preserve the bead.stopped event, claim release, and terminal status writes so repeated Stop calls remain idempotent.
- Add fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children so the test can prove the full descendant tree is gone after Stop.
- Add a second Stop call assertion so the test proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change watchdog reaping in this bead.
- Do not implement server shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or interactive sessions.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent" passes.
4. make install is run after the code change lands.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

acceptance: 1. A repo-root .gitattributes exists with union (or documented custom) merge for .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl. 2. A test or scripted repro that creates two divergent branches each appending a distinct line to .ddx/beads.jsonl merges them with 'git merge' WITHOUT a conflict and retains both lines. 3. cd cli && go build ./... succeeds.
description:
PROBLEM
Concurrent ddx workers/syncs pushing to the same branch hit merge conflicts on the append-only tracker files (.ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl). The operator had to hand-resolve them (git 2.54 lacks -X union on these paths; manual `awk '!seen[$0]++'` dedupe was used). The repo currently has NO .gitattributes file at all, so no merge driver is configured.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- No .gitattributes exists at the ddx repo root. Append-only JSONL tracker files therefore use git's default 3-way text merge, which conflicts on near-simultaneous appends.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a repo-root .gitattributes assigning a union merge to the append-only tracker files:
    .ddx/beads.jsonl merge=union
    .ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl merge=union
- Confirm union is the correct semantic for these files (order-independent line set). If a record can legitimately change in place (not pure append), a custom dedupe driver may be needed instead — document the choice in the commit message.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change how beads.jsonl is written or the tracker-lock serialization in cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go.
- Do not add merge drivers for non-tracker files.

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
title: Add .gitattributes union merge driver for append-only DDx tracker files

acceptance:
1. Add or update `TestPluginInstall_RegistryWritesLockNotProjectPayload` and a HELIX-specific equivalent proving `ddx plugin install helix --force` writes `.ddx/plugins.lock.yaml`, populates `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/cache/plugins/helix/<version>/`, materializes `.agents/skills/*` and `.claude/skills/*` as generated shims, and does not create `.ddx/plugins/helix` as a copied payload directory.
2. Add or update a local-overlay test proving `ddx plugin install helix --local <path> --force` creates `.ddx/plugins/helix` as a symlink to the checkout, materializes agent skill shims to that checkout, and preserves any existing registry lock version pin.
3. Remove or rewrite stale registry/install tests whose comments or assertions require marketplace registry installs to copy real plugin files into `.ddx/plugins/<name>` or require generated skill adapters to be real copied files rather than shims. At minimum reconcile `cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:247-306` and `cli/cmd/install_test.go:249-306` with the new contract.
4. Ensure no production `ddx init`, `ddx plugin install <name>`, `ddx plugin sync`, or `ddx doctor --plugins` path calls legacy project-payload copy APIs for marketplace installs. Legacy `InstallPackage*` helpers may remain only if renamed/commented as compatibility/test helpers and not used by forward CLI flows.
5. Update `cli/internal/registry/registry.go` comments/default semantics so builtin `ddx` and `helix` package metadata no longer describes `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as the registry payload destination except for explicit local-overlay/compatibility language.
6. If public docs drift remains, update FEAT-015/FEAT-018/SD-018 or website plugin docs so they consistently say marketplace payloads live in the XDG cache and generated adapters should not be committed.
7. Focused gate passes: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... -run 'PluginInstall|PluginSync|InstallPackage|LocalOverlay|RegistryWritesLock|NoSymlinks|ProjectPayload|InitProject_MaterializesBuiltinAdapters' -count=1`.
8. Package-level gate passes: `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... -count=1`.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
HELIX is now available as a marketplace plugin, so DDx should behave like an npm/npx-style plugin runner: projects record plugin intent and lock metadata, plugin payloads resolve into the shared XDG cache, and only generated agent adapter shims are materialized locally. FEAT-015 already states this forward model, and `ddx plugin install` mostly uses it through `CachePackageFromRemote` + `SyncProjectPlugin`. However, legacy registry installer code and tests still describe or exercise copying plugin payloads into `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, which keeps the old mental model alive and makes it too easy for future changes to reintroduce checked-in marketplace assets.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/registry/installer.go:125-151` defaults a missing install root to `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and then copies the plugin tree into that project-local target. This contradicts FEAT-015's marketplace update that registry payload bytes live in `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/cache/plugins/<name>/<version>/` and project trees contain only lock metadata plus generated adapters.
- `cli/internal/registry/registry.go:73-80` and `cli/internal/registry/registry.go:91-103` still describe builtin `ddx` and `helix` registry entries in terms of `.ddx/plugins/<name>` roots and copied skill targets. Those fields may remain as manifest compatibility metadata, but the comments/default semantics should not imply registry installs copy payloads into the repository.
- `cli/internal/registry/registry_test.go:247-306` encodes the stale invariant that project-local plugin installs produce real files under `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and no symlinks. That is now backwards for marketplace installs: registry installs should produce cache-backed shims, while only `ddx plugin install --local` should create a `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink overlay.
- `cli/cmd/install_test.go:249-306` still names in-tree/convention install behavior around `.ddx/plugins/<name>` for local installs. The local overlay part is valid, but the test names/comments blur marketplace installs with local overlays.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split the registry package APIs explicitly: marketplace/registry installs must go through cache + lock + `SyncProjectPlugin`; legacy `InstallPackage*` copy APIs should either be renamed/marked as test-only compatibility helpers or made impossible to call from `ddx plugin install`/`ddx init` paths.
- Update builtin registry comments and manifest/default handling so `.ddx/plugins/<name>` is described only as a local overlay or legacy compatibility copy target, not the marketplace payload location.
- Replace stale tests with assertions matching the forward contract: `ddx plugin install helix` and a sample registry package write `.ddx/plugins.lock.yaml`, populate the XDG cache, materialize `.agents/skills/*` and `.claude/skills/*` as generated shims, and do not create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` payload directories. Keep separate tests proving `--local` creates a symlink overlay under `.ddx/plugins/<name>` and never updates the registry pin.
- Update docs if needed so FEAT-015, FEAT-018, SD-018, website plugin docs, and command help consistently explain the npx-style model and do not instruct users to check in marketplace payloads or generated adapter trees.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove support for local plugin overlays; `.ddx/plugins/<name>` remains valid when it is an intentional symlink to a developer checkout.
- Do not implement dependency solving or a new marketplace hosting service.
- Do not change the `SKILL.md` frontmatter contract.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; close this bead with `ddx bead close` after the implementation commit lands.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-602ae31a` (XDG-rooted DDx state / touchless init).
Deps: none.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-ca5cbafb is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Observed live on 2026-06-29. Found 28 orphaned codex processes (ppid=1, parented to systemd) on this host, ages 50min to 2 days, each consuming ~90% CPU. The current ddx worker (pid 4014720) only owned 1 active codex; all 28 others were ghosts from prior ddx worker incarnations that died without reaping their codex children. Each orphan also had a still-running `exo-open --launch WebBrowser https://auth.openai.com/oauth/authorize?...` child stuck on the OAuth callback (codex hadn't received the auth completion, so it kept the spawn alive).

Cumulative effect: load average sustained at 50-80 (on 8-core host) for >24h, choking every ddx work attempt and producing repeat `transport: exit status 1` outcomes because the new codex child gets starved/killed before it can complete the durable_audit phase. Operator must manually `kill -TERM` the orphans to recover.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The executor at cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 sets `Pdeathsig: SIGTERM` and Setpgid for direct harness children. Inspect this path: either (a) codex specifically is forking a detached daemon that escapes the original process group, or (b) the `ddx work --watch` foreground path uses a different executor (not OSExecutor) for the codex spawn, so the Pdeathsig is never wired in.
- The pre-claim cleanup hook landed in ddx-6ee2b0c3 (`cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go`) scans for DDx-owned stale processes by cwd/worktree ownership. Once codex reparents to systemd it loses its DDx-execution-worktree cwd marker (codex's actual cwd may stay valid but the lineage check may rely on parentage). Investigate `staleAttemptProcessScanner` in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go — what makes a process "DDx-owned"? If it requires PID lineage that doesn't survive reparenting, this is the gap.
- The closed beads ddx-0d7d5f0e (parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness on Linux) and ddx-3113d524 (docker-clone backend group cleanup) and ddx-418c3849 (work startup reap) cover sibling cases but evidently do not cover the `codex --no-alt-screen` invocation path executed by the current `ddx work --watch` foreground worker against Fizeau-routed providers.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Wire `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` (Linux) at every site that spawns a provider subprocess from the watch worker — not just OSExecutor — so the kernel kills the child when its immediate paren
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Implement the first bounded slice of the parent bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:61-62 defines 21a 'ddx runs <subcommand>' (cross-layer evidence introspection: log, metrics, history) and 21b 'ddx tries <subcommand>' (layer-2 worktree start/end + merge/preserve records); :77-78 routes metrics under 'ddx runs metrics' (FEAT-014). Add a 'ddx runs' cobra command namespace exposing cross-layer run evidence (log/metrics/history) over the unified run substrate, and 'ddx tries' for layer-2 worktree records (start/end, merge-or-preserve outcomes). Back them with the existing evidence stores rather than new persistence. description:
PROBLEM
Worker runs leave zombie/orphan helper processes (observed: orphan 'find' processes 23+ days old, plus orphan harness procs) that the operator had to hunt and kill manually ('kill the zombie claudes', 'get rid of the zombies and prevent them from happening').

WHY THE NAIVE FIX FAILS (from adversarial review)
- cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go acts only on processes where proc.PPID==1 AND looksLikeHarnessProcess(cmd) matches a fixed harness allowlist (claude|codex|gemini|opencode|pi) AND InferBead can attribute a bead+worktree from the cmdline.
- The harness is started with Setpgid (own process group) and the reaper does killGroup(proc.PID), so a 'find' still inside the harness group is already reaped when the harness dies.
- A 'find' that ESCAPED the group is not PPID-attributable to a harness and a bare 'find' cmdline cannot be mapped to a bead, so it is UNREACHABLE by the current reaper model. Extending looksLikeHarnessProcess to match 'find' would NOT fire.

WHAT TO PRODUCE (design, not implementation)
- A short design doc (under docs/dev/ or docs/helix/) specifying a leftover-PID/leftover-process scan keyed off the LEASE WORKTREE (e.g. processes with cwd or open files under a known execute-bead worktree root that outlive the lease), rather than a cmdline allowlist. Cover: detection signal, safety (never kill a live, legitimately-owned process), Linux-specificity, and how it composes with the existing group-kill + execution_cleanup_loop.go.
- Decompose into child build beads with command-based ACs and depend them on this epic.

NON-SCOPE (for THIS bead)
- No implementation in this bead — design + child beads only.
- Do not change Fizeau routing.
title: DESIGN: leak-proof helper-process reaping (leftover-PID scan)

description:
PROBLEM
DDx cleanup can delete stale execution worktrees and run-state files but still leave descendant shell, cargo, or test-server processes burning CPU or holding ports after the original attempt is gone. The operator sees clean files, but the host still carries DDx-owned work.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` only scans temp dirs, scratch roots, run-state files, evidence bundles, and worker dirs; it has no process census or process-group reaper.
`cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` only reaps PID-1 children whose argv basename matches a top-level harness (`claude`, `codex`, `gemini`, `opencode`, `pi`) and whose lease owner PID is gone, so it misses shell/cargo/test-server descendants under a stale DDx worktree.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a conservative DDx-owned attempt-process scanner/reaper seam under `cli/internal/agent/` that can inspect /proc-style facts (pid, ppid, pgid when available, command, cwd, start time, ownership/worktree context) and classify stale attempt descendant process groups.
- Extend `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with structured process findings and warnings so callers can report `would_kill` in dry-run and terminated groups in apply mode.
- Add Linux and unsupported-platform implementations plus injected fake scanner/killer tests. Use DDx ownership, cwd/worktree, liveness, and recorded run-state evidence to decide staleness.
- Reuse `/proc` parsing helpers already in `cli/internal/workerstatus/` where practical, but keep the cleanup-specific ownership rules in the agent package.

NON-SCOPE
- CLI JSON/text shaping in `cli/cmd/cleanup.go` is handled by a sibling bead.
- Startup/pre-claim wiring in `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go` is handled by a sibling bead.
- Documentation updates in `docs/` are handled separately.
- No general-purpose host process manager, no command-name-only matching, and no killing outside the current project execution root.
title: agent: add conservative stale attempt-process census and reaper

description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-06-13 dogfood run for ddx-d94f1857, `ddx work --request-timeout 20m` did not bound the wall-clock provider attempt. The worker exceeded the timeout, then SIGINT printed "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully" but left descendant work active long enough that the operator had to manually kill the worker/test process group.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work.go wires request timeout into the work/try path, but the observed execution path through cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and cli/internal/agent/service_run.go did not ensure the timeout context owned every subprocess descendant. The cleanup contract in docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 requires DDx-owned execution resources and subprocesses to be reclaimed; the observed attempt violated that contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit the work/try context propagation from `--request-timeout` through execute-bead and service-run so the deadline cancels the provider process group and any post-run test child.
- Add process-tree cleanup that targets only descendants owned by the attempt/worktree and records cleanup evidence.
- Make graceful shutdown wait for bounded cleanup and then hard-kill remaining attempt descendants before returning.
- Surface a durable operator-attention event if cleanup had to escalate from graceful cancellation to kill.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not rely on tmux/session cleanup. Do not remove provider-specific harness support.
title: work: enforce request-timeout process-tree cleanup

2. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves exactly one bead.stopped transition, one claim release, and one terminal worker status write.
3. TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers proves Stop does not terminate an external reported worker PID, an interactive Claude/Codex session, or an unrelated process in another process group.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent|TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers' passes.

description:
PROBLEM
Operator Stop must fully clean a server-managed worker, including claude, codex, shell, and sleep descendants, without killing unrelated processes or duplicating bead transitions when Stop is called more than once.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1227 has an idempotence guard for in-memory handles, but the cleanup path still depends on a single recorded PID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 calls terminateProcessGroup only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1288-1289 then cancels the goroutine, which cannot guarantee cleanup of harness descendants when the server-owned worker lacks a process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:22-32 documents that callers must register only processes spawned in their own process group.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce or reuse one cleanup helper that terminates only server-owned managed worker process groups by PGID when available, falling back to registered child process groups for any transitional in-process harness path. Wire WorkerManager.Stop through that helper after the existing bead.stopped event and Unclaim call. The helper must send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, send SIGKILL to remaining descendants, report what it killed, and make repeated Stop calls no-ops for bead transitions and process cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.
title: server: stop managed worker process trees idempotently

2. Existing prune and managed-stop claim release tests still pass.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWorkerManagerReconcileStaleWorkersReleasesOwnedClaimWithoutCurrentBead|TestWorkerManagerPruneReapsDeadPID|TestWorkerManagerManagedStopReleasesClaimWithoutCurrentBead|TestRunWorkerFinalCleanupSkipsUnscopedSweepWhenAnotherWorkerIsLive' -count=1 passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The server restart canary on 2026-06-15 left an active_work entry for worker-20260615T201953-b17e on ddx-2b15342f after the server marked that worker exited with reap_reason=server-restart. The new worker then claimed ddx-82a0664c, so work status showed two active DDx claims with only one live DDx worker.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:2636-2653 ReconcileStaleWorkers only released rec.CurrentAttempt/rec.CurrentBead. Server-managed workers can hold a durable claim lease owned by worker id even when the persisted WorkerRecord lacks current_bead/current_attempt, so restart reconciliation marked the worker exited but left the bead claim and lease active.

PROPOSED FIX
Use releaseWorkerClaims during server-restart reconciliation, matching the stop path: release the primary bead when present and also any beads/leases owned by the stale worker id. Apply the same owner-aware release to Prune so disk-only stale workers cannot leave claims behind when current_bead is empty.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change provider routing. Do not alter activework collection. Do not change worker desired-state semantics.
title: server: release owned claims when reconciling stale workers

acceptance:
1. `TestServerStartupReconcileDesiredWorkersAcrossRegisteredProjects` is strengthened or paired with a new integration-style `TestServerStartupReconcileLaunchesDesiredWorkersAfterListen` that uses the real reconcile path and proves desired workers for two registered projects are started after server startup without a manual reconcile request.
2. The regression covers the `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` symptom shape: desired_count=1 saved, previous worker terminal/failed, server restarted, and exactly one fresh managed worker appears for that project.
3. Startup reconcile failures are observable through logs/status or a server state endpoint; the test asserts a skipped desired project cannot be silent.
4. `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/... -run 'StartupReconcile|DesiredWorkers|WorkerSupervisor' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After rebuilding/installing `ddx` from commit `2193aa658` and restarting `ddx-server.service` on 2026-06-16, the server came up healthy but with no child workers. Desired workers for `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` and `/Users/erik/Projects/sesame` did not all restart until manual `ddx worker start` / `ddx worker reconcile` calls. `ddx worker start --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` saved desired state but printed only `{"desired_count":1,...}`; a separate `ddx worker reconcile --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` was needed before `worker-20260616T084349-8105` appeared. Server-managed workers are not reliable if boot requires an operator nudge.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:349-350` writes the address file and calls `reconcileDesiredWorkersAfterStartup()` before `http.ListenAndServeTLS` enters Serve.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:364-381` performs delayed reconcile attempts in a goroutine, but the live service still had a window/state where no desired workers were running after restart.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:397-429` discovers project roots only from `s.WorkingDir` and `s.state.GetProjects(false)` plus existing desired-state files; a missing/stale project registration or delayed state load can skip desired projects at startup.
- `cli/internal/server/worker_supervisor.go:125-137` treats absent desired-state as manage-nothing, so any skipped root produces no worker and no visible startup failure.
- `cli/cmd/worker.go:135-176` proves the manual `ddx worker start` path saves desired state then explicitly calls the reconcile endpoint, which succeeded where startup reconciliation did not.

PROPOSED FIX
Make startup reconciliation deterministic and observable. On server startup, every project with `.ddx/workers/desired.json` or a registered desired state must be reconciled after the listener is accepting requests, and failures must be surfaced in server logs/status rather than silently leaving desired workers absent. Add an integration-style regression that starts a real server instance with desired state for at least two projects, waits through startup reconciliation, and asserts workers are launched without calling the reconcile endpoint manually.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change worker claim authority or make the server own bead claims.
- Do not disable manual `ddx worker start` / `ddx worker reconcile`; they remain operator controls.
- Do not change federation worker forwarding.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-d5dfa702`.
Deps: none. Related to `ddx-c713d75e`, but this is specifically restart/desired-state reconciliation, not count caps or audit fields.
title: server: restart must restore desired managed workers

description:
PROBLEM
Story 14 federation follow-up beads are stale relative to the current implementation and test suite. ddx-0793ac75, ddx-28f3cf37, and ddx-359e812b still block the multi-node/federation queue, but current tests already cover several of their original read-federation and UI smoke requirements. The stale state hides the real remaining gap: live owner-targeted writes across hub/spoke processes.

ROOT CAUSE
- ddx-0793ac75 still depends on cancelled/superseded ddx-387a0178, so the queue treats the federation GraphQL follow-up as dependency-waiting even though that dependency is not executable.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338 still marks TC-017 owner-targeted worker control as planned.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-363 still marks cross-project bead and document federated writes as planned.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307 already covers hub/spoke listing, scope=federation reads, offline badge, and restart recovery, so remaining beads must be narrowed to missing owner-write coverage rather than duplicate existing smoke tests.

PROPOSED FIX
- Inspect ddx-0793ac75, ddx-28f3cf37, and ddx-359e812b against current code/tests.
- Map each existing AC to concrete tests, including resolver_federation_test.go federation tests and federation-2node.spec.ts smoke coverage.
- For satisfied ACs, close or supersede the stale bead using DDx tracker commands.
- For unsatisfied ACs, rewrite or create narrower child beads that name the missing owner-targeted write tests only.
- Remove or replace the stale dependency edge from ddx-0793ac75 to cancelled ddx-387a0178 if it is no longer load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement new federation code or tests in this bead.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.
- Do not rewrite history or alter existing execution-audit commits.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. No deps.

acceptance:
1. `TestFederationE2EHermeticity` or a Playwright/wrapper test with equivalent behavior snapshots `git status --short` before and after the live federation e2e run and fails on newly introduced repo dirt.
2. The guard verifies live federation tests use per-node temp `XDG_DATA_HOME` and `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, temp project roots, and dynamically allocated loopback ports.
3. The guard proves hub/spoke child processes are stopped on success and failure; no orphan `ddx server --hub-mode` or spoke process remains after the test.
4. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` passes under the guard, or the new wrapper command documented in the test passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... -run 'Federation|Hermetic' -count=1` passes if Go-side helper code is added.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The live multi-node federation e2e tests must be safe to run autonomously on a developer machine or worker host. They should not leak server processes, fixed ports, XDG state, project registration, or git dirt into the DDx checkout. Without an explicit guard, future federation e2e additions can silently depend on developer state or leave artifacts that block `ddx work` pre-claim checks.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:104-125 already shows the correct pattern for persisted spoke identity and per-node `XDG_DATA_HOME`, but there is no explicit guard that the suite leaves the repository state unchanged.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:116-118 starts hub/spoke processes using `--hub-mode` and `--hub-address`; new tests must keep using random loopback ports and cleanup.
- Recent queue operation was blocked by staged `.ddx/executions/**` and `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` runtime artifacts, proving autonomous tests/workers need stronger cleanup assertions before they can be trusted in continuous queue drain.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a hermeticity guard around the live federation e2e harness or a dedicated test that snapshots git state before/after the federation suite.
- Assert hub/spoke tests use temp project roots, temp `XDG_DATA_HOME`, temp `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, and dynamically allocated loopback ports.
- Ensure all spawned hub/spoke processes are terminated on failure and no `.ddx/workers`, `.ddx/executions`, or project registration artifacts are written under the repository root except pre-existing dirt.
- If the guard cannot run inside Playwright reliably, add a small script/test wrapper that runs the e2e file and verifies `git status --short` is unchanged modulo pre-existing paths captured before the run.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not require a clean worktree at test start; compare before/after so the guard tolerates pre-existing operator dirt.
- Do not add visual screenshot checks.
- Do not run real ts-net/MagicDNS in this autonomous gate.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. Depends on live owner-targeted write coverage because the guard should protect the live federation suite that includes those writes.
title: federation: guard live multi-node e2e hermeticity

2. If implementing: TestExecuteBeadResultParsesDiscoveredSubtasks proves the field round-trips from result.json into the durable record, and absent field stays nil/omitted.
3. If implementing: no code path calls ddx bead create from discovered_subtasks (assert gating preserved).
4. Spec and implementation agree after this change (no remaining "chosen" mechanism that is unimplemented).

description:
PROBLEM
FEAT-006 §374-434 records a firm design decision (option b): execute-bead workers surface mid-run discovered work as a structured discovered_subtasks array in result.json, and option (c) creating beads in-band is marked "former behavior" that "violates workflow ownership boundary." Neither the result.json schema nor the capture path emits discovered_subtasks, so spec and implementation disagree.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:374-434 specifies the discovered_subtasks array (schema at :406-426) as the chosen mechanism. The result.json struct ExecuteBeadResult at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:36 has no DiscoveredSubtasks field, and no DiscoveredSubtask type exists anywhere in cli/ (verified by grep, 2026-06-19 audit). Meanwhile execute-bead prompts still instruct in-band 'ddx bead create' for Step-0 decomposition. The originating design bead ddx-d3a4f89c is closed; no open bead tracks the implementation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a DiscoveredSubtask type and an optional DiscoveredSubtasks []DiscoveredSubtask field (json:"discovered_subtasks,omitempty") to ExecuteBeadResult, parse it from the worker-written result.json, and preserve it through landing into the durable record. If the team has since reversed the decision, the alternative acceptable outcome is to amend FEAT-006 §374-434 to document the constrained-(c) decomposition behavior actually shipped — pick one and make spec and code agree.

NON-SCOPE
Do not auto-create beads from discovered_subtasks (the whole point of option b is to gate tracker mutation behind an explicit supervisor decision). Do not change Step-0 decomposition behavior.
title: FEAT-006: implement discovered_subtasks in result.json (option b) or reconcile spec

acceptance:
1. `TestReadyCommandHonorsCreateSetQueueRank` creates two same-priority beads through command/store APIs with one `queue-rank=0`; `ddx bead ready --json` returns the ranked bead first.
2. `TestReadyExecutionHonorsQueueRankOnActiveBackend` proves `ddx bead ready --execution --json` and `Store.ReadyExecution()` return explicit ranks before unranked beads for both JSONL and Axon/current backend fixtures.
3. `TestPreviewQueueAndWorkPickerHonorQueueRank` proves `PreviewQueue` and `ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate` select the lowest numeric queue-rank before older unranked beads in the same priority bucket.
4. `TestQueueRankRoundTripsThroughBeadShowAndStoreExtra` proves `ddx bead show --json` and the in-memory `Bead` used by sorting agree on rank value and type for ranks created with `--set queue-rank=0`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestReadyCommandHonorsCreateSetQueueRank|TestReadyExecutionHonorsQueueRankOnActiveBackend|TestPreviewQueueAndWorkPickerHonorQueueRank|TestQueueRankRoundTripsThroughBeadShowAndStoreExtra'` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM

Operators need to explicitly order the DDx queue. Today `ddx bead create --set queue-rank=0` stores the rank (verified by `ddx bead show`), but `ddx bead ready --json` still lists older priority-0 beads ahead of the newly ranked bead. That means a custom field is not enough in the live backend: `ddx work` can ignore the operator's intended top-of-queue item. This directly blocks forcing urgent dogfood findings, such as provider-process failure classification, to the front of the queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/bead/operation.go:8-31` defines `QueueSetTop` / `QueueSetPosition` as `Extra["queue-rank"]` mutations, so the intended model is a numeric rank within the priority bucket.
- `cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go:58-73` and `:104-120` prove JSONL store ready and execution-ready sorting honors `Extra["queue-rank"]`, but the live queue did not place `ddx-ef9df563` (priority 0, `queue-rank=0`) ahead of unranked priority-0 beads.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:2715-2716` sorts from `beads[i].Extra["queue-rank"]`; if the active backend exposes custom fields as top-level JSON/show output but does not hydrate `Bead.Extra`, sorting silently treats ranked beads as unranked.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4997-5011` delegates worker picking to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, so any backend rank hydration bug affects both `ddx bead ready` and `ddx work`.

PROPOSED FIX

Make queue rank a first-class numeric field in the ready/work ordering contract across storage backends. Either hydrate `queue-rank` into `Bead.Extra` for every backend before sort, or add explicit accessors that read both Extra and backend/custom-field storage. Add command-level coverage proving `ddx bead create --set queue-rank=0`, `ddx bead update --set queue-rank=N`, and any queue helper command affect `ddx bead ready --json`, `ddx bead ready --execution --json`, `PreviewQueue`, and `ddx work` candidate selection.

NON-SCOPE

Do not replace priority buckets; rank sorts within priority unless the operator also changes priority. Do not hand-edit tracker storage. Do not rely only on JSONL tests; cover the active Axon/current backend path used by this repository.
title: queue: honor explicit numeric rank in ready and work ordering

acceptance:

2. 'ddx runs log' and 'ddx runs history' surface run evidence read from the existing executions/attachments stores, equivalent to the data exposed by 'ddx exec history'/'ddx log'.
3. The 'ddx runs' root command is registered such that a sibling bead can attach a 'metrics' subcommand without modifying runs.go's existing subcommands.
4. TestRunsCommandHelp and TestRunsCommandLogHistory exercise the surface against a fixture repo via testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, ...).
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.

description:
PROBLEM
FEAT-001 documents 'ddx runs <subcommand>' as an imperative (non-deprecated) cross-layer evidence-introspection surface, but no such command exists. Run evidence is scattered across 'ddx exec history/log/result' and 'ddx log', so the documented unified introspection entry point (log + history) is unmet.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:61-62 defines §21a 'ddx runs <subcommand>' as cross-layer evidence introspection (log, metrics, history) over the unified run substrate. No cli/cmd/runs*.go exists (verified: no matches, 2026-06-19 audit). The pieces it must read from already exist under 'ddx exec'/'ddx log' and the executions/attachments evidence stores.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a 'ddx runs' cobra root command in cli/cmd/runs.go registering 'log' and 'history' subcommands that read cross-layer run evidence from the existing evidence stores (executions dirs + attachments events stream). Reuse the data access already backing 'ddx exec history'/'ddx log'; do not duplicate it. The 'metrics' subcommand is delivered by a sibling bead and is registered onto this same root; keep the root extensible so that bead can attach to it.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove or alter the existing 'ddx exec'/'ddx log'/'ddx bead metrics' surfaces — deprecation is tracked separately. Do not add new persistence; read only from existing executions/attachments/metrics stores. Do not implement 'ddx runs metrics' here (sibling bead) or 'ddx tries' (sibling bead).
title: FEAT-001: add 'ddx runs' namespace with log and history subcommands

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` adds self-contained Niflheim evidence cases for both observed details: `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`; it asserts the selected action is reconcile, retry-land, preserve-evidence, or operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run.
2. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDoesNotRaiseMinPower` proves land/ref/index/evidence-staging outcomes do not raise requested min power/profile and do not request stronger model escalation.
3. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, land/reconcile status, and any review status available for the land coordination scenarios.
4. Existing behavior from `ddx-2965925e` and `ddx-b2846610` remains covered by reuse/alignment with their helpers or by waiting on those dependencies; this bead does not add a duplicate low-level index-contention or timeout implementation.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|LandCoordination|RetryEscalation|LandCoordinationDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

acceptance:
1. TestCmdTestsDoNotSpawnRealProviderCLIs wraps representative cmd tests and asserts no descendant process named real codex, claude, or gemini remains after the test completes.
2. The assertion only inspects descendants of the test process and does not kill or fail on unrelated interactive provider sessions.
3. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestCmdTestsDoNotSpawnRealProviderCLIs'` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/...` green and leaves no real codex/claude/gemini descendants.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes without leaving provider descendants.
description:
PROBLEM
During ddx-0be85773 dogfood on 2026-06-13, cmd tests left real provider CLI children (codex --no-alt-screen, claude, gemini) alive. There is no leak assertion that fails when a cmd test leaves a descendant provider process running, so leaks go undetected.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd has no descendant-process leak guard around its tests. Tests such as TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim and TestWorkDoesNotSpawnProviderAfterUnderSpecifiedRouting can spawn provider children, and nothing in cli/cmd/testutils_test.go enumerates and asserts on surviving descendants after a test completes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add TestCmdTestsDoNotSpawnRealProviderCLIs that runs a set of representative cmd tests and, after each, enumerates descendant processes of the test process and fails if any process named real codex, claude, or gemini remains alive.
- Scope the assertion to descendants of the test process only, so unrelated/interactive provider sessions on the machine are ignored.
- Build on the hermetic discovery guard from the sibling bead 'tests: add hermetic provider-discovery guard for the cmd suite' (depends on it).

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken production provider discovery. Do not skip the affected tests. Do not kill unrelated interactive provider sessions as part of test cleanup. The hermetic PATH/discovery guard itself and the zero-config/underspecified-routing test rewrites are handled in sibling beads.
title: tests: assert cmd suite leaves no real provider CLI descendants

acceptance:
1. Go test TestDocumentWrite_RejectsStaleExpectedHash proves stale saves fail without overwriting current content.
2. Go test TestDocumentWrite_PathConfinementStillRejectsEscape proves absolute paths and ../ traversal remain rejected after the expected-hash contract is added.
3. GraphQL schema and resolver tests prove documentWrite exposes and consumes the expected hash/version input with deterministic conflict response semantics.
4. cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/graphql/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Local document saves overwrite current file content immediately, so a browser tab can silently clobber a newer document version for any registered project.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34 defines DocumentWrite with only path and content, so callers cannot provide an expected document version or hash.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:55 writes content directly with os.WriteFile and no expected hash/version check.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3263 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without an expected version/hash input.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:356 requires spec/document edits to be project-scoped and path-confined.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:353 defines TC-019 stale-write refusal coverage for cross-project spec editing.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the GraphQL documentWrite input/arguments with an expected hash or equivalent version token.
- Compute the current document hash before writing and reject the mutation with deterministic conflict semantics when the expected hash does not match.
- Preserve the existing local ResolveDocumentPath confinement path for writes.
- Keep successful local writes behavior-compatible except for requiring or honoring stale-safe expected-hash semantics.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full collaborative editor or arbitrary filesystem browser. Keep the primary workflow scoped to project documents, especially docs/helix/**. Do not implement hub-to-spoke federation forwarding in this bead. Do not implement graph/staleness refresh behavior in this bead. Parent bead: ddx-d5dfa702.
title: server: reject stale local document writes

acceptance: 1. A prompt-snapshot test (cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/...) shows .ddx/guardrails.md content present in the dispatched prompt when the file exists. 2. A test shows the assembled prompt is unchanged when the file is absent. 3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes; go build ./... succeeds.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators re-paste hard per-project invariants ('do not delete anything from redpanda', 'never run perf tests on GitHub runners', 'do not touch the NAT') as prose every dispatch and every compaction, because there is no per-project guardrails file spliced into the load-bearing guardrail block the executor controls. Guardrails are currently static global consts.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (~1945-2065): guardrails are hardcoded const prompt strings (instrCoreConstraints, instrBeadOverride, the numbered load-bearing guardrails). No reader splices a per-project file. The only per-project surface (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) is harness-loaded prose, not part of the executor-controlled guardrail block.

PROPOSED FIX
- Read an OPTIONAL project-root file (.ddx/guardrails.md) and splice its contents into the execute-bead guardrail assembly so per-project invariants are injected on every dispatch (and therefore survive compaction automatically, since they are re-injected each iteration).
- Absent file = no behavioral change.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the existing static guardrail consts' wording.
- Do not add AGENTS.md parsing or change how the harness loads AGENTS.md.
- Do not implement per-bead overrides (this is per-project only).

PROVENANCE
Derived from a session-harvest of local Claude/Codex operator prompts (2026-06-27), classified into deficiency-papering vs intent, then put through two adversarial-review rounds that dropped every item already shipped. This item survived as a verified, non-redundant gap. See docs/session-harvest-playbook.md.
title: Inject per-project .ddx/guardrails.md into the execute-bead guardrail block

acceptance:
1. `TestAttemptProviderChildrenAreRouteScoped` uses fake provider child processes for codex/claude/gemini and proves that after an active route is selected only children belonging to that route remain alive.
2. `TestSupersededProviderChildrenAreReapedWithEvidence` simulates a route/probe child that outlives route selection; DDx terminates it and records pid, provider, age, action=terminated, and reason=superseded_provider_child.
3. `TestWorkerStatusReportsProviderChildren` proves `ddx work status --json` includes attempt provider_children with pid, provider/harness, route_owner, phase, and age while an attempt is active.
4. `TestAttemptEndReapsAllProviderChildren` proves successful, failed, and interrupted attempts leave no provider child processes under the worker process tree.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestAttemptProviderChildrenAreRouteScoped|TestSupersededProviderChildrenAreReapedWithEvidence|TestWorkerStatusReportsProviderChildren|TestAttemptEndReapsAllProviderChildren'` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM

During dogfood on 2026-06-13, one `ddx work --once` attempt for bead ddx-0be85773 reported active route `claude/sonnet`, but the worker process retained multiple direct provider CLI children at the same time: `codex --no-alt-screen` (PID 1875100, older than 7 minutes), `claude --print ... --model sonnet` (PID 1882389, active implementation route), and later `gemini` (PID 1890368). A single attempt should not leave unrelated provider CLIs resident under the worker unless they are explicitly part of the active route and supervised. This burns resources, makes process status ambiguous, and creates exactly the cleanup risk server-managed workers must solve.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:140-142` treats Fizeau `Execute` as one event stream after process setup; DDx does not currently expose per-provider child process ownership/phase in worker status.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-385` supervises service events with idle/tool timers, but it does not assert that only active-route provider children remain alive or that superseded provider processes are reaped.
- `cli/internal/agent/provider_failure.go:1-17` states untyped provider boundary failures are product bugs; the same boundary also needs process ownership evidence so stray provider CLIs do not become invisible worker state.
- Observed command: `ddx work --once --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx-provider-failure-20260613 --request-timeout 25m --route-resolution-timeout 90s`; status showed attempt `20260613T202003-18b8bd7e`, phase `running`, route logs `do route claude/sonnet`, while `ps --ppid <worker-pid>` listed Codex, Claude, and Gemini children.

PROPOSED FIX

Track provider subprocesses per attempt with route/phase metadata. When a route is selected, DDx/Fizeau should either avoid starting unrelated provider CLIs or immediately terminate any provider child not owned by the active route. Worker status/evidence should surface provider_children with provider, pid, phase, route_owner, age, and cleanup_result. At attempt end, DDx must reap every provider child and fail or flag the attempt if cleanup is incomplete.

NON-SCOPE

Do not ban legitimate short-lived helper probes if they are bounded and recorded. Do not kill unrelated same-user provider processes outside the worker process tree. Do not rely on real provider CLIs in regression tests; use fake long-lived child processes.
notes:
Dogfood evidence 2026-06-13: ddx work --once for ddx-ef9df563 exited cleanly after merge, but left Gemini provider process group 2677024 orphaned under PPID 1; broader scan found 11 stale PPID=1 gemini process groups aged 3h to 2d with no TTY. Operator killed stale orphan groups. Fix must ensure worker-owned provider descendants are process-group scoped and reaped on normal exit, timeout, review, and cancellation.

Reopened after post-install canary worker worker-20260615T140645-9d8f for ddx-c5c755f6. Requested route was claude-tui/opus-4.7 and the worker succeeded, but after attempt-end cleanup wrote .ddx/executions/20260615T140747-d0d32f0e/provider-children.json and process-cleanup.json, fresh Codex/Gemini provider children remained under ddx server PID 4025758: codex PID 4137945 and gemini PIDs 4138006/4138212. Operator killed those stale provider children manually. Acceptance for route-scoped/no-lingering provider children is not satisfied until post-attempt/post-land hooks cannot spawn or retain non-route providers.
title: agent: prevent unrelated provider CLIs from lingering under one worker attempt

acceptance:
1. TestRunWithConfigViaServiceScrubsParentWorkerRoutingEnvForServiceFactory proves service factory construction does not inherit DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, or DDX_WORKER_ID.
2. TestRunWithConfigViaServiceScrubsParentWorkerRoutingEnvForService still proves svc.Execute sees the scrubbed process env and restored parent env.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestRunWithConfigViaServiceScrubsParentWorkerRoutingEnvForServiceFactory|TestRunWithConfigViaServiceScrubsParentWorkerRoutingEnvForService|TestOSExecutorScrubsRuntimeWorkerRoutingEnvOverrides|TestOSExecutorScrubsParentWorkerRoutingEnv|TestOSExecutorAllowsAttemptWorkerIDOverride|TestOSExecutorIncludesDDXModeEnv|TestScrubbedGitEnvScriptScrubsParentWorkerRoutingEnv'` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
6. After make install and worker restart, /proc/<provider-pid>/environ for the DDx canary provider/helper contains no DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, or DDX_WORKER_ID.
description:
PROBLEM
After ddx-81350f3e was installed and the DDx canary restarted, /proc/<pid>/environ for the live `codex --no-alt-screen` provider child still contained DDX_AGENT_NAME=worker-20260615T184658-c0f6 and DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID=worker-20260615T184658-c0f6. That child appeared before the Claude intake Execute call, which means the provider/helper process can be spawned during Fizeau service construction or preflight inventory, not only during svc.Execute.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:183 constructed the preflight Fizeau service before the scoped process-env scrub added around svc.Execute at service_run.go:322. Fizeau service construction and foreground inventory calls can read os.Environ and spawn provider/helper processes directly, so scrubbing only inside executeOnService is too late.

PROPOSED FIX
Move the scoped process-env scrub outward in RunWithConfigViaService so it starts before ResolvePreflightServiceFromWorkDir and remains active until dispatch returns. Keep the inner executeOnService scrub for direct callers with prebuilt services. Add a test that captures process env inside the service factory and inside Execute to prove both service construction and execution see no DDX_PROJECT_ROOT, DDX_AGENT_NAME, DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID, or DDX_WORKER_ID, and that the worker env is restored afterward.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove DDX_AGENT_NAME or DDX_SERVER_MANAGED_WORKER_ID from the ddx work parent process. Do not change provider routing or Fizeau internals. Do not change ordinary user-shell DDX_PROJECT_ROOT behavior outside provider/helper subprocess boundaries.
title: agent: scrub worker env during Fizeau service construction

acceptance:
1. `TestPreClaimReadiness_AcceptsRewriteAcceptanceArray` or the nearest existing preclaim intake test decodes a payload with `rewrite.acceptance` as a JSON string array and asserts it normalizes to the multiline acceptance string used by `ValidatePreClaimRewrite`.
2. `TestBeadLifecycleSkillContract_AllowsRewriteAcceptanceArray` updates the current strict contract test so array `rewrite.acceptance` no longer fails solely due to shape; a sibling invalid-shape test still fails with a `rewrite.acceptance` diagnostic.
3. The live canary shape from `worker-20260616T084349-8105` is covered: `classification` ready/rewritten plus `rewrite.acceptance: ["1. ...", "2. ..."]` decodes without `system_unready`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'PreClaimReadiness|PreClaimIntake|BeadLifecycleSkillContract|RewriteAcceptance' -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The live server-managed ddx worker `worker-20260616T084349-8105` claimed `ddx-67cdc109` on 2026-06-16, ran Claude readiness intake, and then logged: `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal array into Go struct field preClaimIntakePromptRewrite.rewrite.acceptance of type string (continuing)`. The bead remains claimed with an `intake.warn` event even though the provider route was viable and produced a syntactically useful rewrite shape. This blocks reliable `ddx work` because provider/schema drift in an optional rewrite field is treated as system-unready instead of normalized or ignored.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:47-50` defines `preClaimIntakePromptRewrite.Acceptance` as `string`, so JSON arrays for `rewrite.acceptance` fail the entire intake decode before policy can use the classification.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:255-281` already has `decodeReadinessAcceptanceList` for `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance`, tolerating either a JSON string or an array of numbered criteria.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:674-684` tells providers that suggested child acceptance may be an array but says rewrite acceptance should be `acceptance`; live Claude still returned an array for rewrite acceptance.
- `cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go:132-134` currently asserts array `rewrite.acceptance` is an error, which conflicts with live Claude TUI canary behavior and the forward-compatible worker reliability goal.

PROPOSED FIX
Normalize `rewrite.acceptance` using the same string-or-array decoder used for suggested child acceptance. Arrays of numbered criteria should be joined into the canonical multiline acceptance string before `ValidatePreClaimRewrite` runs. Malformed non-string/non-array shapes should still produce a structured decode warning/error. Update prompt/contract tests so arrays are accepted for rewrite acceptance while preserving strictness for invalid shapes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not make arbitrary rewrite fields untyped.
- Do not accept lossy rewrites that drop or alter acceptance criteria; existing `ValidatePreClaimRewrite` must still enforce preservation.
- Do not change provider routing or model selection.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`.
Deps: none. This is a live canary blocker for server-managed `ddx work` reliability.
title: agent/intake: normalize rewrite.acceptance arrays

acceptance:
1. Go test `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner` proves hub saves for spoke projects persist on the spoke and not locally.
2. Go test `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_PreservesOriginAndTargetMetadata` proves origin/forwarding metadata and target identity survive the forward.
3. `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Hub-mode already has a generic owner-targeted mutation transport, but documentWrite still follows the local filesystem write path. A spoke-owned project edited from the hub would therefore persist on the hub filesystem instead of the owning spoke.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 writes document content locally after path resolution.
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go:81-198 already implements ForwardMutation with origin identity, forwarding path, target node/project, expected version, and typed refusal handling.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:7-9 documents that the federation provider surface already includes owner-targeted write forwarding.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without a routing-aware input.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect hub-mode spoke-owned document targets in the resolver and route them through Federation.ForwardMutation instead of local os.WriteFile.
- Carry origin identity, forwarding path, request id, idempotency key, target node/project IDs, and expected version through the forward request and returned response.
- Preserve local path confinement for same-node writes and for non-hub requests.
- Surface typed refusal errors for offline, stale, read-only, missing-owner, or broadcast-like targets instead of silently falling back to local state.

NON-SCOPE
- Stale-content conflict checking and graph rebuild behavior are owned by sibling bead ddx-3c399bec.
- Playwright spec-editing coverage stays with ddx-e951255c.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go
- cli/internal/server/graphql_mutation_docs_test.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider_test.go
- cli/internal/server/federation_hub_test.go

Out-of-scope (file a separate bead):
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts
- cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go
title: server: forward documentWrite to owner-spoke federation

acceptance:
1. The git repo health detection/fix logic currently in `cli/cmd/doctor.go` is available as a reusable helper for work-loop preflight/remediation without changing `ddx doctor --fix` behavior.
2. When a project root has `core.bare=true` but has a real working tree on disk, `ddx work` unsets `core.bare`, emits/logs the repair with issue type `git_core_bare_corruption`, rechecks git status successfully, and continues the same drain cycle.
3. When a project root has a stray local `core.worktree`, `ddx work` unsets it, emits/logs the repair with issue type `git_stray_core_worktree`, rechecks git status successfully, and continues.
4. When a project root has local `core.hooksPath`, `ddx work` unsets it, emits/logs the repair with issue type `git_local_hooks_path`, rechecks git status successfully, and continues.
5. `ddx work` does not auto-enable `extensions.worktreeConfig`; that remains warning-only unless a separate existing path explicitly enables it.
6. If known git config repair fails or git status still fails after repair, `ddx work` releases the claimed bead, emits `loop.operator_attention` with reason `pre_dispatch_git_repair_failed`, includes git stderr, and stops before claiming a second ready bead.
7. Real dirty implementation files still use the existing checkpoint-dirty behavior: `TestLoop_DrainCheckpointDirtyStopsQueue` and `TestLoop_WatchCheckpointDirtyStopsWithoutRetry` pass unchanged, including watch-mode dirty preservation behavior.
8. Add `TestWorkRepairsCoreBareAndContinuesSameCandidate` (or equivalent) proving a worker repairs `core.bare=true` and proceeds with the originally selected bead rather than failing it or moving to another bead.
9. Add `TestWorkGitRepairFailureStopsBeforeSecondClaim` (or equivalent) proving failed/unresolved repair stops after releasing the first bead and leaves the second bead unclaimed.
10. Add/extend a doctor test proving the extracted helper still powers `ddx doctor --fix` for `core.bare`, stray `core.worktree`, and local `core.hooksPath`.
11. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'CoreBare|GitRepair|CheckpointDirty|WatchCheckpointDirty|GitRepoHealth|Doctor' -count=1` passes.
12. `cd cli && go test -short -race -timeout 30m ./internal/agent` passes, or any pre-existing failure is documented with exact failing test names and shown unrelated to this bead.
13. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can burn through multiple unrelated beads and then exit with opaque operator attention when the project root's git config is corrupted, for example when `.git/config` contains `core.bare=true`. In that state `git status` fails with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`, but the worker output currently degrades to `exit status 128` and repeated pre-claim warnings across distinct bead IDs. Operators should not have to stop the factory, run a separate diagnosis, and restart for a known safe DDx-era corruption mode: `ddx work` should repair the known safe git config corruptions and continue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:179-183` only checks whether `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` exits successfully. For `core.bare=true`, git can exit 0 and print `false`, so the checkpoint path proceeds as if the project root were valid instead of triggering a repair.
- `cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:292-293` and `cli/internal/agent/predispatch_dirty.go:377-381` call git with `Output()` and wrap only `exit status 128`, dropping git stderr such as `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`.
- `cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:104-106` has the same stderr-dropping pattern for readiness/lifecycle project-root status snapshots.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1153-1174` only recognizes the implementation-dirty checkpoint refusal as a project-level stop. Other pre-execute checkpoint failures fall through normal attempt failure handling, so the loop can move on to the next ready bead and eventually trip the unrelated repeated-warning operator-attention path.
- `cli/cmd/doctor.go:990-1127` already detects and fixes the known safe local git config corruptions: `core.bare=true`, stray `core.worktree`, and local `core.hooksPath`. That logic is trapped behind `ddx doctor --fix` (`cli/cmd/command_factory_commands.go:98`) instead of being available to the work-loop preflight/remediation path.
- Existing coverage around `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:916-962` proves dirty implementation files stop after one claim, but there is no equivalent test for project-root git config corruption being repaired and retried. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:426-430` covers ordinary dirty-file refusal, but not `core.bare=true` / non-worktree project roots.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extract/reuse the `doctor --fix` git repo health remediation as a reusable internal helper, rather than duplicating ad hoc config unsets in `ddx work`. The helper must preserve the existing safe set: unset `core.bare` when a working tree exists, unset stray `core.worktree`, unset local `core.hooksPath`; do not auto-enable `extensions.worktreeConfig` because doctor currently treats that as warning-only.
- Add a work-loop project-root git health preflight/remediator before readiness dispatch and before pre-execute checkpointing. When the known safe corruption is detected, repair it, emit durable/structured evidence naming the issue types and commands applied, then re-run the git health/status operation and continue the same drain cycle.
- Ensure a repaired first bead is not counted as a failed attempt and does not cause the worker to claim the next bead. The expected path is: detect corruption, repair, continue readiness/checkpoint for the same candidate, and then execute normally if the checkout is healthy.
- If repair fails, or if git remains unusable after the known repairs, then and only then release the claimed bead, emit `loop.operator_attention` with a stable reason such as `pre_dispatch_git_repair_failed`, include git stderr, and stop before claiming another bead.
- Preserve the existing implementation-dirty checkpoint behavior unchanged, including watch-mode preservation behavior for real dirty files.
- Add regressions for `core.bare=true` repair, failure-after-repair operator attention, and stderr preservation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign execute-bead loop extraction or the unrelated `ddx-f2a366db` code-health work.
- Do not auto-repair arbitrary git repository corruption. Keep remediation limited to the known safe local config keys already handled by `ddx doctor --fix`: `core.bare`, stray `core.worktree`, and local `core.hooksPath`.
- Do not silently hide repair. The operator must see a concise log/event that DDx repaired project git config before continuing.
- Do not prove the original writer of `core.bare=true` in this bead. If the corruption source recurs after this repair path lands, file a separate prevention/investigation bead.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider/model selection, or readiness classification semantics except for preserving git stderr in the existing lifecycle status error.
- Do not land the existing local branch `fix/pre-dispatch-git-corruption-stop` as-is. It is a diagnostic/stop-first prototype and must be revised to repair-and-continue.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-2a1ece2b`.
Deps: none.
title: work: repair pre-dispatch git config corruption and continue

1. TestAttemptIntegrityRejectsVerificationWrongCWD feeds a transcript/tool record where a required Go verification command returns `go.mod file not found`; DDx classifies it as wrong_cwd_verification and does not accept it as passing evidence.
2. TestAttemptIntegrityRejectsMissingExecutionBundleLookup feeds a command/output pair that tries `.ddx/executions/` from the wrong cwd and records evidence_path_wrong_cwd.
3. TestAttemptIntegrityRejectsTmpRoundTripForSourceFiles feeds transcript commands that move tracked source/test files through `/tmp`; DDx records tmp_roundtrip_tracked_file and preserves or rejects the attempt.
4. TestAttemptIntegrityAllowsBenignBuildToolTempUsage proves build cache/temp paths not moving repo source or evidence are not rejected.

description:
PROBLEM
During dogfood of ddx-3b721804 on 2026-06-13, the execute-bead attempt ran a Go verification command from the wrong directory and got `go: go.mod file not found in current directory`, then later attempted to inspect `.ddx/executions/` from the wrong location and got `No such file or directory`. The same attempt temporarily moved new source files through `/tmp` while debugging. DDx instructions already say to work inside the execution worktree and keep evidence under the bundle path, but instructions alone did not prevent wrong-cwd verification or `/tmp` round-trips.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1925-1934 defines the no_changes rationale path in prose but does not make evidence-path checks mechanical for ordinary verification commands.
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1936-1941 says reports must go under the bundle path and never `/tmp`, but DDx does not reject transcript/tool activity that uses `/tmp` as an implementation or evidence staging area.
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1961-1967 says work only inside the execution worktree, but DDx does not validate that shell commands affecting verification ran from the repo/module root or execution worktree.
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_validation.go:147-161 validates commit/gate integrity after the attempt, but it skips checks it cannot observe and does not classify wrong-cwd verification or `/tmp` file round-trips as attempt-integrity defects.

PROPOSED FIX
Add structured transcript/session validation for execute-bead attempts that detects verification commands run from the wrong cwd, evidence-path lookups outside the execution bundle, and source/evidence file moves through `/tmp`. When detected, preserve the attempt or require repair before closure with a typed integrity reason and durable evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not ban temporary compiler caches or harmless OS temp usage by build tools. Do not parse arbitrary shell perfectly; target high-signal patterns DDx can observe from tool command arguments, cwd, and output.
title: agent: enforce execution cwd and evidence-path contracts

2. `TestInstall_InTreeMode_WritesProjectPluginsAndLinks` asserts plain `ddx install <name>` in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and creates `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`.
3. `TestInstall_ConventionMode_WritesXdgProjectTreeAndLinks` asserts plain `ddx install <name>` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and project-tier links point into that XDG plugin path.
4. `TestPluginListGlobal_EnumeratesFromGlobalTier` and `TestPluginShowGlobal_ReportsFromGlobalTier` assert `ddx plugin list --global` and `ddx plugin show <name> --global` read global state, while unflagged commands read project state.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... -run 'TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTreeAndAgentLinks|TestInstall_InTreeMode_WritesProjectPluginsAndLinks|TestInstall_ConventionMode_WritesXdgProjectTreeAndLinks|TestPluginListGlobal_EnumeratesFromGlobalTier|TestPluginShowGlobal_ReportsFromGlobalTier' -count=1` passes.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Operators need `ddx install <name> --global` for machine-wide plugin and skill installs while plain `ddx install <name>` remains project-local in both in-tree and convention modes. The parent bead bundles install routing with init, update, lookup, doctor, and docs, making it too large for one execution.

ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/cmd/install.go:22` owns the `ddx install <name>` command surface and must carry both project-local and `--global` routing.
- `cli/cmd/install.go:181` is the install-directory decision point that must choose `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/` for global installs or `ddxroot.Path()` for project installs.
- `cli/cmd/install.go:270` and `cli/cmd/install.go:308` define `ddx plugin list/show`; they need the same `--global` state selection as install.
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90` defines the global DDx state root required by ddx-06cbaa90.

PROPOSED FIX
Add or verify `--global` on `ddx install`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx plugin show`. Global installs write plugin content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`, record global installed state, and create agent-tier links under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`. Plain installs write to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/`; in in-tree mode that resolves to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, and in convention mode to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/`. Plain installs create project-tier skill links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`, with convention-mode links pointing into the XDG project plugin tree.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a.
- Depends on ddx-06cbaa90: install paths route through both `ddxroot.Path()` and `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/`.
- ADR-027 skill install topology or equivalent must stay consistent with this behavior.

PARENT
- ddx-602ae31a

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely; global and per-project modes coexist.
- Migrating `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths if any exist.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex-facing `.agents/skills`; add others only when those harnesses define their skill paths.
title: install: route global and project plugin installs to correct skill tiers
