title: test bead

description:
PROBLEM
Observed live on 2026-06-29. Found 28 orphaned codex processes (ppid=1, parented to systemd) on this host, ages 50min to 2 days, each consuming ~90% CPU. The current ddx worker (pid 4014720) only owned 1 active codex; all 28 others were ghosts from prior ddx worker incarnations that died without reaping their codex children. Each orphan also had a still-running `exo-open --launch WebBrowser https://auth.openai.com/oauth/authorize?...` child stuck on the OAuth callback (codex hadn't received the auth completion, so it kept the spawn alive).

Cumulative effect: load average sustained at 50-80 (on 8-core host) for >24h, choking every ddx work attempt and producing repeat `transport: exit status 1` outcomes because the new codex child gets starved/killed before it can complete the durable_audit phase. Operator must manually `kill -TERM` the orphans to recover.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- The executor at cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29 sets `Pdeathsig: SIGTERM` and Setpgid for direct harness children. Inspect this path: either (a) codex specifically is forking a detached daemon that escapes the original process group, or (b) the `ddx work --watch` foreground path uses a different executor (not OSExecutor) for the codex spawn, so the Pdeathsig is never wired in.
- The pre-claim cleanup hook landed in ddx-6ee2b0c3 (`cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go`) scans for DDx-owned stale processes by cwd/worktree ownership. Once codex reparents to systemd it loses its DDx-execution-worktree cwd marker (codex's actual cwd may stay valid but the lineage check may rely on parentage). Investigate `staleAttemptProcessScanner` in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go — what makes a process "DDx-owned"? If it requires PID lineage that doesn't survive reparenting, this is the gap.
- The closed beads ddx-0d7d5f0e (parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness on Linux) and ddx-3113d524 (docker-clone backend group cleanup) and ddx-418c3849 (work startup reap) cover sibling cases but evidently do not cover the `codex --no-alt-screen` invocation path executed by the current `ddx work --watch` foreground worker against Fizeau-routed providers.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Wire `prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)` (Linux) at every site that spawns a provider subprocess from the watch worker — not just OSExecutor — so the kernel kills the child when its immediate parent dies. Mirror the pattern from `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go`.
2. Set Setpgid on the provider spawn so SIGTERM/SIGKILL to the worker's process group reaps codex AND any descendant (exo-open, browser handoffs, etc.) together.
3. Extend the pre-claim stale-process classifier to recognize codex/claude/etc. processes whose cwd is `/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/...` (regardless of current parent pid) as "DDx-owned attempt descendants", and reap them.
4. Add a non-Linux fallback that uses periodic poll-and-reap by cwd marker.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go (verify Pdeathsig is set; if not, add)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go (verify all provider spawn sites go through the unix executor; if any bypass, route them)
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process.go (broaden "DDx-owned" classifier to include reparented children whose cwd is under .cache/ddx/exec-wt/)
- cli/cmd/startup_process_cleanup.go (ensure the broader classifier is invoked)

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau's provider-side cleanup.
- Do not implement a general-purpose orphan reaper for non-DDx processes.
- Do not add a periodic "kill all codex" sweeper — only reap by lineage+cwd ownership.

AC
1. Add unit test `TestExecutor_ProviderSpawnSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` proving every code path that spawns a harness child sets PR_SET_PDEATHSIG + Setpgid on Linux. Verify by inspecting cmd.SysProcAttr.
2. Add integration test in cli/cmd/work_test.go that launches `ddx work --watch` against a stub harness, kills the worker with SIGKILL mid-attempt, and asserts the harness child is dead within 5s (proves Pdeathsig fires).
3. Add unit test `TestStaleAttemptProcessScanner_DetectsReparentedDescendantsByCwd` proving the classifier detects codex-like processes whose cwd is under `.cache/ddx/exec-wt/` even when ppid=1.
4. Add test that `ddx cleanup --apply` (and the new pre-claim pass) reaps such orphans on the test host.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
notes: decomposed into ddx-01b89378, ddx-54d9c455, ddx-50f5696f
title: ddx work: codex provider subprocesses orphan to PID 1 when worker dies, accumulate as 90% CPU zombies for days

description:
PROBLEM
Once GraphQL-backed operations exist, the backend needs parity coverage against JSONL so worker execution can trust AxonBackend without silently diverging on import/export, events, dependencies, and store behavior.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go contains Axon-specific tests, but the parent acceptance requires JSONL import/export round trip and package-level conformance.
- cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go and related store tests exercise backend behavior primarily through JSONL paths.
- Without a parameterized conformance pass, AxonBackend can pass targeted CRUD tests but still diverge from RawBackend expectations.

PROPOSED FIX
- Parameterize the existing backend/store conformance suite so JSONL and Axon exercise the same core operations where practical.
- Add Axon import/export round-trip coverage.
- Document any intentional Axon-only limitation in test names and bead notes, not hidden in implementation comments.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding new backend selection config plumbing.
- UI or server integration.
title: bead/axon: add Axon backend conformance and import/export coverage

description:
PROBLEM
Server.Shutdown only closes the bead hub and stops land coordinators. It does not stop the worker manager or sweep managed worker process trees, so server-owned descendants can outlive the server process when the server shuts down.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:269-283 has no worker-manager shutdown call at all; it only closes the bead hub and coordinator registry.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1323-1327 only stops the watchdog goroutine, so it does not clean live workers.
There is no manager-level shutdown entrypoint that iterates managed workers and stops them idempotently before the server returns.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a worker-manager shutdown method that stops all server-owned managed workers and waits for their terminalization.
- Call that method from Server.Shutdown before closing the hub and coordinators.
- Keep the operation idempotent so repeated shutdown calls do not double-transition beads or kill unrelated processes.
- Add TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees as the shutdown proof.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Stop/Reap semantics beyond reusing the shared helper.
- Do not implement UI controls or GraphQL mutations in this bead.
- Do not adopt external workers.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees proves server/manager shutdown cleans all server-owned worker process groups.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: server: stop managed workers during server shutdown

Persona bindings, meta-prompt sync, and command auto-commit routing.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-bced4e39 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Niflheim land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures were recorded as overall `execution_failed` even when the candidate had succeeded or landed. The worker retried implementation work instead of reconciling land/evidence state or sending the bead to operator attention, wasting route attempts and confusing system failure with model-quality failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative table that only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate, but execute-bead/work land outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add self-contained Niflheim-style land coordination fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` for `cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate or merge is already landed, and for `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s` caused by generated DDx evidence. Classify these as land/ref/index/evidence coordination outcomes, not implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree is dirty with generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to existing reliability policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this is typed classification and reconciliation behavior, not another timeout implementation.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineer
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|LandCoordination|RetryEscalation|LandCoordinationDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The web UI needs executable coverage for cross-project spec editing behavior, including save, path confinement, stale-write refusal, federated document write, and graph/staleness refresh scenarios.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3263 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without the expected version/hash input or response semantics the UI needs to exercise stale-safe saves.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:356 requires spec/document edits to be project-scoped, path-confined, and owner-targeted in hub-mode.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:353 defines TC-019 for cross-project spec editing, stale-write refusal, federated document write, and graph/staleness refresh.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the frontend spec editing workflow and GraphQL calls to send the expected document hash/version when saving.
- Surface stale-save conflict behavior without overwriting the editor buffer.
- Add Playwright coverage in spec-editing.spec.ts for local FEAT doc save, path confinement, stale-write refusal, federated document write, and graph/staleness refresh.
- Use the server-provided deterministic graph/staleness refresh path after a successful save.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full collaborative editor or arbitrary filesystem browser. Keep the primary workflow scoped to project documents, especially docs/helix/**. Do not implement server resolver, federation, or docgraph internals in this bead except test fixtures needed by Playwright. Parent bead: ddx-d5dfa702.
title: frontend: cover safe spec editing flows

acceptance: 1. cd cli && go test ./cmd/ -count=1 is GREEN (the ~30 listed tests pass). 2. The shared cmd-test setup helpers that create an in-tree .ddx seed a config.yaml (or otherwise make ddxroot.Path resolve in-tree) so tests are hermetic and do not write into the real $XDG_DATA_HOME. 3. A note/decision records whether DDX_BEAD_DIR is still an honored override (and a production fix if it regressed). 4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green. 5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description: PROBLEM: ~30+ tests in cli/cmd are RED (TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle, TestAcRun_*, TestBeadArchive*, TestBeadDoctor*, TestMigrate*, TestBeadQueue*, TestBeadMetrics, TestOperatorAttentionSurfacesWedgeReleases, etc.). The worker go-test gate over ./cmd is therefore red. Example failure: TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle (cmd/bead_acceptance_test.go:36) -> 'unable to find file .../001/.ddx/beads.jsonl'. ROOT CAUSE: the shared helper newBeadTestRoot (top of cmd/bead_acceptance_test.go) does os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(workingDir)) + sets DDX_BEAD_DIR=.ddx, but writes NO .ddx/config.yaml. After the XDG/presence-based root work (internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go Path/isInitializedInTree — an in-tree .ddx is authoritative ONLY when it has config.yaml; isInitializedInTree checks os.Stat(dir/config.yaml)), ddxroot.Path(workingDir) falls back to the XDG convention root (~/.local/share/ddx/projects/...). So bead commands read/write the XDG root while the tests assert files under the in-tree .ddx -> not found. Same root cause already fixed for the version-gate fixture in writeProjectVersion (commit 356a37f85, which seeds config.yaml). NOTE: the helper sets DDX_BEAD_DIR=.ddx, which was presumably the override mechanism that USED to force the in-tree path — verify whether ddxroot.Path should still honor DDX_BEAD_DIR (if so, this is also a PRODUCTION regression for anyone using that env var, not only stale tests). PROPOSED FIX: (a) seed a minimal .ddx/config.yaml in newBeadTestRoot (and any sibling cmd-test setup helpers that create an in-tree .ddx without it) so the in-tree root is authoritative AND the tests stay hermetic (bootstrapConventionRoot otherwise writes into the real XDG dir); AND/OR (b) make ddxroot.Path honor DDX_BEAD_DIR / an explicit in-tree marker if that override is still contractual. NON-SCOPE: do not change the presence-based resolution itself (it is intentional per the XDG-rooted-state epic). Audit ALL cmd test setup helpers, not just newBeadTestRoot.
title: cmd test suite broadly red: shared test helpers create in-tree .ddx without config.yaml so ddxroot.Path falls back to XDG root

description:
PROBLEM
The built-in DDx package still teaches new projects that the normal DDx package payload belongs under .ddx/plugins/ddx. That conflicts with the npx-style topology where projects record plugin intent in .ddx/plugins.lock.yaml, generated adapters appear under .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/, and payloads resolve from the shared cache or embedded fallback.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27 says the forward behavior is cache-backed and npx-like and generated adapters/payloads are not forward repository state.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:623-672 still defaults library.path to .ddx/plugins/ddx in DefaultNewConfig and ApplyDefaults.

PROPOSED FIX
Update DefaultNewConfig and ApplyDefaults so default configuration no longer presents .ddx/plugins/ddx as the normal built-in DDx package payload path. Preserve any explicit local-overlay or legacy path when the user already configured one, and make the default represent generated-adapter/cache-backed resolution.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for explicit local development overlays. Do not remove the embedded default plugin fallback needed for offline ddx init. Do not change HELIX marketplace packaging.

PARENT
ddx-602ae31a

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:18-27
title: config: stop defaulting built-in DDx library path to project payload

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_RepeatedPreClaimSystemWarningBecomesOperatorAttention` adds a self-contained Niflheim evidence case for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt and asserts the worker records an operator-visible system-unready/operator-attention state after the configured threshold.
- `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile; repeated pre-claim system warnings, land coordination, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence do not raise min power.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
After a successful spec/document save, callers need returned document or graph state to be fresh, or they need a deterministic refresh path. Without that, the UI can show stale graph/staleness state immediately after editing a project document.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:55 writes content directly with os.WriteFile and does not refresh or expose document graph/staleness refresh behavior.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3263 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without response semantics that guarantee fresh document/graph state or a deterministic refresh path.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:356 requires spec/document edits to be project-scoped for the dashboard workflow.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:353 defines TC-019 graph/staleness refresh coverage after spec editing.

PROPOSED FIX
- After a successful local or forwarded documentWrite, rebuild or invalidate the relevant document graph/staleness state for the affected project.
- Return fresh document/graph state from the mutation, or expose a deterministic follow-up refresh/read path that the UI can use immediately.
- Keep path confinement and stale-write conflict behavior unchanged.

NON-SCOPE
Do not build a full collaborative editor or arbitrary filesystem browser. Keep the primary workflow scoped to project documents, especially docs/helix/**. Do not implement the expected-hash conflict contract from scratch in this bead; consume the documentWrite result shape available at implementation time. Do not implement Playwright UI coverage in this bead. Parent bead: ddx-d5dfa702.
title: docgraph: refresh document state after writes

description:
PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE: internal/server/workers_resolve_route_test.go:174 waits up to 30*time.Second (waitForWorkerExit, internal/server/workers_test.go:282, 50ms poll). On the resolveRouteFailingService error path it burned the full 30s and FAILED under load. The worker should reach FinishedAt promptly on a failed route.

PROPOSED FIX: Confirm the failing-route worker path sets FinishedAt deterministically; reduce the deadline to ~5s and/or replace polling with a completion signal/channel from the WorkerManager. Do NOT change the assertion that ResolveRoute is never called for a pinned profile/model.

NON-SCOPE: keep the routinglint ResolveRoute-not-called assertion intact.
title: test-scrub: make the server worker-exit wait deterministic and bounded (30s -> ~5s)

description:
PROBLEM
The web UI must let an operator control spoke workers, mutate queues, and edit specs from one server surface once the backend exposes owner-targeted write support.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:12 defines local StartWorker/StopWorker mutations without owner-target routing inputs or federated target handling.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+layout.svelte:92 has create-form state, but TC-018 requires create, edit, lifecycle, invalid-state refusal, and federated write flows.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/documents/[...path]/+page.svelte:89 defines DocumentWrite with only path/content and cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/documents/[...path]/+page.svelte:311 saves without expected hash/version.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327 through docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:364 define the required federated worker, bead, and spec editing E2E flows.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add UI routing/state so hub views can select local or spoke owner targets and call the backend owner-targeted mutations.
- Complete bead create/edit/lifecycle forms with required-note validation, error display, refresh, and project isolation.
- Update document/spec editing to pass expected hash/version, show stale-write conflicts, keep path confinement errors visible, and refresh graph/staleness state after save.
- Ensure all mutation flows show node/project context and never fabricate success rows for offline/read_only spokes.

NON-SCOPE

description:
PROBLEM
DDx decomposition guidance and implementation currently frame decomposed parents as open, execution-ineligible containers. That leaves stale open parent beads in the queue and makes the safe operational answer unclear when an agent splits a bead: the correct answer is that a losslessly decomposed bead is completed by decomposition and should be closed. The downstream invariant is that children must still be work-ready: `parent` is hierarchy metadata, not a dependency gate, and generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:20-22 says `too_large_decomposed` leaves the parent `status=open` with dependency edges to children, and docs/triage/decomposition.md:63-73 says the parent remains open and depends on all children.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:162,169,171,180,197-207 says review/no_changes/decompose outcomes leave oversized or decomposed beads open with child dep edges or `execution-eligible=false`.
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 forbid `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the current bead and only allow a parent-note update during Step 0 decomposition, so agent-facing instructions do not say to mark a decomposed bead completed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2734-2743 logs successful pre-claim decomposition then releases the parent while explicitly commenting that it stays open until children close. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5248-5275 implements `applyPreClaimDecomposition` by setting `execution-eligible=false` instead of closing the parent.
- cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-133 and 207-210, plus cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go:182-204, use the same park-open pattern.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go:73-77 and cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose_test.go:20-22 assert the old open/ineligible behavior, so tests lock in the ambiguous framing.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the decomposition lifecycle contract in docs/triage/decomposition.md, docs/beads.md, TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, and FEAT-010 where applicable: a lossless child/sibling/replacement decomposition closes the decomposed bead as completed-by-decomposition. Historical open `execution-eligible=false` containers may remain as legacy/backfill cases, but they are not the normative path for new decomposition.
- Update .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go, and prompt snapshots so Step 0 says: agents may create child/sibling/replacement beads, wire only child-to-child or sibling/replacement deps, must not add the parent as a child dependency, and must close the decomposed parent once the split is lossless and durable.
- Update `applyPreClaimDecomposition`, `runDecomposer`, and `AutoDecomposeEpic` to close the parent with a lifecycle transition after child creation. Preserve traceability via `triage-decomposed` / `decompose-applied` events and child IDs in Extra or event body. Clear claim metadata/heartbeat so a closed parent cannot remain half-owned.
- Preserve downstream readiness semantics: children are created with `Parent=<decomposed-id>` but no dependency on the parent. Existing child-to-child dependency ordering remains allowed. Closing the parent must not be used as a substitute for adding or removing legitimate child-to-child deps.
- Update and add tests around the changed lifecycle behavior and the child readiness invariant.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
- Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b (ddx work idle-path diagnose and auto-remediate). This bead clarifies one remediation path so the queue advances instead of preserving dead intermediates.
- Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab handles parent/ancestor dependency back-edge prevention and repair. This bead handles the higher-level lifecycle contract: close the decomposed parent and prove `Parent` metadata does not block children.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Deps: No deps. The related ddx-130cf9ab work can land independently; this bead must include no-parent-dep assertions so it is safe either before or after that repair slice.

description:
PROBLEM
HELIX is available as a marketplace plugin, so DDx should stop treating plugin install as "copy all plugin assets into each repository." The desired operator model is npx-like: a project records plugin intent/version pins, plugin payloads resolve into an XDG cache or compatible global install, and local agent skill directories are generated shims/links that can be recreated at any time.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:60 still described `ddx init` as writing `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` project files.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:63 described registry plugin install as writing real files under `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:317 said `ddx init` and registry plugin installs copy real files for clone portability.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md:46 described default project install as plugin content under project/convention plugin trees with agent-tier outputs under the project.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-018-plugin-api.md:58 described `install.root` as copying the entire plugin to a target and had no materialization/cache contract.
- cli/cmd/install.go:150 calls `adjustInstallTargets` and `registry.InstallPackage`, which copies package payloads into the resolved install root.
- cli/cmd/install.go:216 rewrites package install targets to `plugins/<name>`, preserving the copy-tree model for registry installs.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement the marketplace materialization contract now documented in FEAT-015, FEAT-018, and ADR-027:
1. Add project plugin dependency/lock metadata for registry installs. The project records plugin name/version/source/checksum or equivalent durable lock data; the full plugin payload is not committed into the project worktree.
2. Resolve registry plugin payloads into an XDG cache/global package layer, with the baked-in ddx package as the offline fallback for the default ddx plugin.
3. Add `ddx plugin sync` or equivalent lazy sync path that materializes `.agents/skills/*` and `.claude/skills/*` as generated shims/links from the lock/cache, and make first plugin-consuming commands call it when shims are missing.
4. Update `ddx plugin install`, `ddx plugin list/show`, `ddx doctor --plugins`, and uninstall/upgrade behavior to distinguish project dependency metadata, cached payloads, generated shims, local overlays, and legacy copied payloads.
5. Preserve `ddx plugin install <name> --local <path>` as a developer overlay that may symlink directly to a checkout.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/install.go and install tests in cli/cmd/install_manifest_test.go or sibling tests.
- cli/internal/registry/* for package resolution, cache paths, lock metadata, and validation.
- cli/cmd/doctor*.go and doctor plugin tests for reporting cache/materialization state.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md, docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-018-plugin-api.md, docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md for any implementation-driven refinements.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove local overlay support.
- Do not make HELIX a built-in DDx payload.
- Do not require project repos to commit marketplace plugin payload trees.
- Do not solve plugin dependency graphs beyond a single plugin lock/materialization model.

description:
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim `too_large_decomposed` path creates child beads but keeps the decomposed parent open and execution-ineligible. That contradicts the lossless-decomposition lifecycle rule and risks leaving a half-owned parent while children should be ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2734-2743 logs successful pre-claim decomposition then releases the parent while explicitly documenting that the parent stays open until children close.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:5248-5275 implements `applyPreClaimDecomposition` by setting `Extra["execution-eligible"] = false` instead of closing the parent after children are created.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go:73-77 asserts the old open/ineligible behavior and does not prove children remain `ReadyExecution` through the `Parent` metadata field.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update `applyPreClaimDecomposition` in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go so, after child beads are durably created, it closes the decomposed parent through the lifecycle/store API as completed by decomposition.
- Preserve durable traceability by recording `triage-decomposed` with generated child IDs in the event body or extra evidence.
- Clear parent claim metadata and heartbeat fields so a closed parent cannot remain half-owned.
- Preserve child readiness semantics: each child may have `Parent=<decomposed-id>` metadata, but generated children must not depend on the decomposed parent; child-to-child ordering deps remain allowed when explicitly generated.
- Update/rename tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go to prove the close and readiness behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change post-attempt decomposer or auto-epic decomposition behavior in this bead; those are separate children under ddx-e9542e0d.
- Do not implement historical tracker cleanup or the broader ddx-130cf9ab parent/ancestor dependency repair slice.
- Do not change intake classification semantics other than the lifecycle state applied after a successful lossless split.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-e9542e0d.
Deps: No deps.

acceptance:
1. `TestWorkProviderProcessMissingExecutableClassified` uses a fake service or fake provider process startup failure equivalent to `exec.ErrNotFound`; `ddx work --once` records `typed_failure=provider_harness_unavailable`, does not print raw "routing under-specified" or generic "execution_failed", unclaims the bead, and leaves no provider child processes.
2. `TestWorkProviderProcessEarlyExitClassifiedWithEvidence` simulates a provider child that exits before emitting a final event; DDx records structured provider/process evidence including requested_harness, requested_provider, requested_model, typed_failure, retryable, fallback_attempted, fallback_stop_reason when applicable, exit_code/stderr excerpt or equivalent process detail, and cleanup_result.
3. `TestWorkProviderModelUnavailableFallsBackWhenUnpinned` simulates model-not-found/model-unavailable from the first route; an unpinned worker records the failed route and retries an alternate eligible route without operator intervention.
4. `TestWorkProviderFailureHonorsHardPins` pins harness/provider/model, simulates the same provider failure, and proves DDx does not widen the pin; it stops with `fallback_stop_reason=hard_pin_exhausted` and actionable operator output.
5. `TestWorkStatusAndOperatorAttentionSurfaceTypedProviderFailure` proves `ddx work status --json` and operator-attention/evidence surfaces show the typed provider/process failure instead of an underspecified worker failure.

description:
PROBLEM

Underspecified worker/provider-process failures are a DDx correctness bug. DDx/FIZ is the abstraction that should keep work moving across provider availability and local configuration variance. If a provider executable is missing, a harness process exits early, model routing fails, or Fizeau returns a pre-dispatch/provider-boundary error, `ddx work` must classify it with a typed provider/process reason, record fallback/stop evidence, clean up any children, and continue when an alternate route is eligible. It must not surface a generic worker failure, raw "routing under-specified" text, or leave the bead/operator unable to tell whether this is provider_auth, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_connectivity, no_viable_provider, provider_config_invalid, or unknown_provider_failure.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

- `cli/internal/agent/provider_failure.go:1-17` defines the intended DDx/Fizeau boundary contract: generic untyped provider failures are product bugs, and each provider failure needs typed reason, retryability, fallback decision, and durable evidence.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:140-142` still returns `fmt.Errorf("agent: execute: %w", err)` for Fizeau `Execute` setup failures before the event stream exists; every caller must prove this error is classified and does not fall through as generic execution/worker failure.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:337-385` bounds event-stream silence/tool loops, but provider-process startup/early-exit failures can occur before useful stream evidence, so they need a separate typed classification/cleanup path.
- `cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go:15-27` documents the prior "routing under-specified" regression and explicitly avoids real provider dispatch; coverage must be extended from "do not print under-specified" to "classify provider-process failure and choose fallback/stop semantics."
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go:18-60` and `:285-342` prove provider_connectivity fallback semantics after reports exist, but do not prove raw provider-process failures are converted into those reports before worker/operator surfaces see them.

PROPOSED FIX

Add an end-to-end worker/process-boundary classification path for provider process startup, missing executable, early child exit, model-unavailable, under-specified routing, and Fizeau pre-dispatch errors. Normalize them through the provider failure taxonomy, write `ProviderFailureEvidence`, include requested/resolved harness/provider/model plus retryability and fallback_stop_reason, and ensure unpinned workers automatically retry eligible alternate routes while pinned workers stop loudly with hard_pin_exhausted. Ensure cleanup reaps provider-process children before the worker continues.

NON-SCOPE

Do not move model catalog/routing ownership out of Fizeau. Do not call real Claude/Codex/Gemini providers in tests. Do not silently widen explicit operator pins. Do not weaken existing provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider fallback tests.
title: agent: type worker provider-process failures before generic failure

acceptance:
1. A regression test proves stopping a server-managed worker after it claimed a bead but before an attempt releases the claim and `ddx work status --json` reports active_work.count=0.
2. A regression test proves worker stop cleanup only releases claims owned by that managed worker ID and does not unclaim unrelated external worker claims.
3. A lock-scope regression test proves durable-audit finalization does not hold tracker.lock across index.add/index.commit and recorded hold durations stay under DDX_LOCK_CAP_INDEX_MS/DDX_LOCK_CAP_TRACKER_MS in a low cap test.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test.*Managed.*Stop.*Claim|Test.*Durable.*Audit.*Lock|Test.*Lock.*Cap"` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
6. After `make install` and restarting server-managed workers, stopping a just-claimed DDx worker leaves no stale active_work and `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` has no new lock violation entries for the fix canary.
description:
PROBLEM
The 2026-06-15 DDx server-managed Claude TUI canary completed ddx-cf0cafef successfully, then exposed two reliability violations. First, the land/audit path appended `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl` entries showing index.lock held for 20.177s during index.commit and tracker.lock held for 20.651s during durable_audit, exceeding the 10s/30s lock lifetime contract risk envelope for short-lived mutation locks. Second, when the watch worker immediately claimed ddx-2b15342f and was stopped through POST /api/agent/workers/<id>/stop, the worker process exited with status 130 but `ddx work status --json` still reported active_work for ddx-2b15342f until an operator manually ran `ddx bead update ddx-2b15342f --unclaim`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go land/durable-audit finalization still performs git index commit work while the durable_audit tracker lock remains held, and lock metrics recorded the over-cap hold in `.ddx/metrics/locks.jsonl`. cli/internal/server/workers.go stop handling terminates a managed worker process group but does not release any bead claim owned by that managed worker ID when the process exits before starting an attempt. `ddx work status` reads the claim as active even though the server record is stopped/pid_alive=false.

PROPOSED FIX
Split durable-audit finalization so git index add/commit work is not performed while tracker.lock is held, and keep index.lock hold below its cap. On managed-worker stop/process exit, release open bead claims whose owner/assignee is the stopped server-managed worker ID, append durable operator/worker lifecycle evidence, and make `ddx work status --json` report no active work for a stopped managed worker with no live process.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the successful implementation from ddx-cf0cafef. Do not weaken lock caps. Do not hide lock violations from metrics. Do not auto-release claims for unrelated external workers that the server does not own.
title: work: release stopped managed-worker claims and cap durable-audit locks

description:
# PROBLEM
Orphan `codex` processes (ppid=1, ~90-100% CPU) accumulate on any host running `ddx work --watch`, despite the Pdeathsig-shim fix landing in `851d9e2b3` / `c318e1934` / `623166130`. Verified in production 2026-07-01: fresh worker binary contains `__provider-launch` and `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` symbols, but 4-day-old orphan `codex` processes exist and new orphans continue appearing at ~1 per 1-2 hours on each active worker. Reaping by hand each loop tick is not tenable.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/provider_spawn.go:83` `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` installs the PATH shim (which wraps codex/claude in `ddx __provider-launch` so the kernel sets PR_SET_PDEATHSIG=SIGKILL on the provider before execve).

The shim install is called from ONE place only: `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:103` inside `runAgentViaService`. That function is **legacy** — reachable only from `RunAgent(r, opts)` in `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:5-7`, which per its own doc-comment is used by the virtual/script harnesses only.

The production provider-spawn entry point is `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:110` `RunWithConfigViaService`, called by:
- `cli/cmd/run.go:164` (ddx run)
- `cli/cmd/exec.go:101` (ddx exec)
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:312` (ddx try / ddx work smoke path)
- `cli/internal/server/server.go:2519` and `:5068` (server HTTP)
- `cli/internal/server/review_dispatcher.go:72` (review dispatch)

`RunWithConfigViaService` does NOT call `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH`. Every `ddx work` / `ddx try` / `ddx run` therefore constructs a Fizeau service whose `LookPath("codex")` finds the raw `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex`, spawns it with Fizeau's default `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}` (no Pdeathsig), and any SIGKILL/OOM of the worker leaks the codex child as an orphan.

# EVIDENCE
- Isolated Pdeathsig test PASSES: `ddx __provider-launch /bin/sleep 30 & sleep 0.5; kill -9 $!` → sleep dies within 1s. So the seam itself is correct.
- Fizeau `internal/harnesses/codex/runner.go:64,84,105` uses `osexec.LookPath("codex")` at spawn time — so a PATH prepend by DDx WOULD intercept.
- Grep confirms `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` has exactly one non-test caller: `agent_runner_service.go:103`. Nothing in the production flow reaches it.
- Production symptom: 5-28 orphan codex procs accumulate per day of drain activity.

# PROPOSED FIX
Install the shim inside `RunWithConfigViaService` before `resolveService` — best-effort, mirroring the pattern already at `agent_runner_service.go:102-106` verbatim. `EnsureProviderShimOnPATH` is idempotent (sync.Mutex + early-return when providerShimDirPath != ""), so calling it from both paths is safe.

# NON-SCOPE
- Zombie/orphan reaper strategy (`ddx-ae98c450`, `ddx-096129b9`, `ddx-24a3da84`) — separate reliability layer.
- Server-managed worker supervisor (`ddx-9d1af129` epic) — separate initiative.
- Windows/darwin — Pdeathsig is Linux-only; other platforms no-op via `provider_spawn_windows.go` etc.
- Reviewing/replacing the legacy `RunAgent` path — separate cleanup; keep both shim installs for safety.
title: agent: install provider shim in production RunWithConfigViaService path (currently only in legacy Runner path)

2. Test `TestWork_ClaudeSubprocessDeathSynthesizesTerminalOutcome` proves: if the claude harness subprocess exits without emitting a final event, the worker synthesizes an execution_failed (or transport:*) outcome within 30s AND writes a terminal worker.heartbeat with phase!=running.
3. `ddx work --watch` no longer enters the observed pattern (`phase=running`, `harness=claude`, no claude child, non_route codex alive, heartbeats forever, futex_wait) — verified by a scripted end-to-end test that kills the claude subprocess mid-attempt and observes the worker exiting the attempt within 60s.

description:
# PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` gets into an unrecoverable spawn-loop where the worker keeps launching codex subprocesses that its own status.json labels as "non-route provider ... terminated by running-phase guard" — but the guard does NOT terminate them. Result: no bead progress, stale claude/opus attempt state, infinite codex churn. Observed live 2026-07-02: worker pid 3893557 on bead ddx-68b48c9e, attempt 20260702T105934-18be73ef.

# EVIDENCE (captured)
Worker `status.json` reports:
- `harness: claude`, `model: opus`, `phase: running`
- `provider_children: [{pid: 1229443, provider: codex, non_route: true, diagnostic: "non-route provider codex terminated by running-phase guard (active route claude)"}]`

BUT `ps --ppid 3893557` shows only:
- `git` (age 14:41)
- `codex` (age 14:17, the "non-route" one)
- NO claude process

The claude/opus process that the status says is "running" is gone. The codex that the guard claims to have "terminated" is alive. When I manually `kill 1229443`, the worker immediately respawns a fresh codex (1416758) within seconds — same non-route state. Worker itself is in `futex_wait_queue` in kernel wait.

Log shows `worker.heartbeat` every ~30s with `phase=running` but no actual work events for 1h+. Zero bead outcome events (no ✓/✗/no_changes/timeout/etc.).

# ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
Two candidate bugs:

1. **Running-phase guard doesn't actually kill**: some code path emits the `terminated by running-phase guard` diagnostic string without calling kill/wait on the non-route pid. So the message is aspirational, not reflective.

2. **Claude harness dispatch died but the worker didn't detect it**: the ExecuteBeadLoop harness=claude route's process died (SIGKILL, panic, EOF on stream) without emitting a final event, so the worker's outcome detection never fires. Then something (readiness probe, review dispatch, model discovery?) keeps re-invoking codex as a background helper, hitting the guard, spinning.

Grep entry points:
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1483` (Stop/watchdog reap)
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (main execute loop)
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_process*.go` (reaper)
- Any code emitting the exact string `non-route provider ... terminated by running-phase guard` — that's the diagnostic source

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Locate the emission site of the `"non-route provider %s terminated by running-phase guard"` diagnostic. Verify it actually calls `syscall.Kill(pid, SIGTERM)` (or SIGKILL after grace) — NOT just labels the child. Add a wait-with-timeout to prove termination.
2. Add a watchdog: if `provider_children[].non_route=true` for a child that has been alive >N seconds AND the child's pid still exists AND no successor process replaced it via a legitimate route, kill it hard (SIGKILL + wait) and log at level ERROR.
3. When the claim's route harness (claude/opus) subprocess exits without emitting a final event, the worker MUST synthesize a terminal outcome (execution_failed or transport-classified) instead of remaining `phase=running` forever. Currently it appears to just sit in futex_wait indefinitely.

# NON-SCOPE
- The specific test-harness deadlock inside ddx-68b48c9e itself (that's a separate story if it exists).
- Server-managed workers (ddx-9d1af129 epic) — this bug affects both server-managed and standalone `ddx work`.

# IMPACT
Without this fix, any transient claude subprocess death mid-attempt puts a worker into a permanent stuck state with 100% CPU codex spawn churn. Queue drain stalls. On my machine this required manual `kill 1229443` + kill worker + restart to unstick.
title: worker: non-route provider spawn-loop when claude harness subprocess dies silently

description:
PROBLEM
Hub-mode needs to forward basic operator writes to exactly one owner node/project instead of remaining read-only or creating phantom local state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:150 specifies the owner-targeted write surface.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-007-federation-topology.md documents the 2026-06-08 amendment requiring owner-targeted basic writes.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:3 documents the current federation resolvers as read-only fan-out.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:23 defines FederationProvider with only Spokes and FanOut; there is no owner-targeted ForwardMutation path.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327 requires federated worker control and owner-targeted writes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a narrow federation forwarding interface and implementation for mutations that target one node/project/worker owner.
- Carry origin identity, forwarding path, request id/idempotency key, target node id, target project id, and expected version where supplied.
- Refuse offline, stale, read_only, missing-project, missing-owner, and broadcast-like requests with typed errors.
- Add idempotency storage or replay behavior for forwarded requests so retries do not duplicate workers/events/writes.
- Keep existing federated reads unchanged except for capability metadata needed by forwarding.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement write broadcast, distributed consensus, Axon backend, FEAT-029 managed-node outbound channels, or hub failover.
title: server: add owner-targeted federation mutation forwarding

description:
PROBLEM
Server-managed worker records can show a stale current_attempt from an old worker. During live dogfood on 2026-06-15, a newly started ddx server worker worker-20260615T165912-4cb5 had pid=1043072/pgid=1043072 and its log showed it starting ddx-cf0cafef, but ddx server workers show hydrated current_attempt from old run-state 20260615T135134-8c4db98d / bead ddx-f93e6ef9. The sesame canary similarly showed old sesame-4a08e922 while its log was idle. This makes the web UI and server status untrustworthy for live worker monitoring.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:860-905 refreshWorkerCurrentAttemptFromRunState calls agent.ReadRunState(projectRoot) for every running/stopping WorkerRecord and copies the returned attempt into rec.CurrentAttempt. It does not check that the run-state is unexpired, that the pid is alive, or that the run-state belongs to this WorkerRecord/PID/worker id. cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:204-223 ReadRunState returns a compatibility file or newest per-attempt record without filtering expired records; stale .ddx/run-state/*.json entries with expires_at hours in the past can therefore be reused.

PROPOSED FIX
Make server worker current_attempt hydration worker-specific and freshness-aware. Running WorkerRecord hydration must ignore run-state whose expires_at is before now, whose pid is dead, or whose pid/worker identity does not match the WorkerRecord PID/ID. Prefer in-memory ProgressEvent state when available. If no matching fresh state exists, leave CurrentAttempt nil rather than showing stale work. Add a cleanup or read-time filter so historical .ddx/run-state/*.json records do not poison server status.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change execute-bead claim semantics. Do not delete tracked execution evidence. Do not hide LastAttempt history for completed workers.
title: server: ignore stale run-state when hydrating worker attempts

Block profile: ~30s of a 35s test spent in bufio.Scanner.Scan + WaitGroup.Wait inside runAgentExecuteLoopImpl's three hooks. Three tests cost 35.5+35.5+19.1 ≈ 90s (~30% of the cmd package).

PROPOSED FIX: Set factory.AgentRunnerOverride to a deterministic stub runner in each of the three tests (factor a stubQualityRunner(t) helper). Audit other work/try cmd tests for the same nil-runner omission and fix in this bead.

NON-SCOPE: do NOT change what these tests assert (durable-audit commit behavior). Behavior/coverage preserved.

3. pgrep -af 'ddx work|claude|codex' is reviewed after migration and no retired attempt-owned ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendant remains.
4. Each project has fresh worker progress evidence from the server-managed surface after migration.
5. Queue status for helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq is recorded, including projected blockers if burn-down within 24 hours is not realistic.
6. docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md is updated with final migration evidence.
7. make install is run after any ddx code change lands during migration.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The server-managed worker feature is not done until active dogfood queues stop depending on tmux/shell workers and are monitored through the server-managed surface. The operator specifically needs helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq to each have one worker steadily burning down its bead queue, with all in-flight queues expected to burn down in less than 24 hours unless blocked by explicit beads.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The plan's Migration Plan requires replacing external workers with server-managed desired state. Existing server start/stop at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3271-3285 and worker UI controls at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:12-42 were one-shot controls, so earlier dogfood used tmux windows and external ddx work processes. After the dependency beads land, the server should be authoritative for routine worker lifecycle.

PROPOSED FIX
Inventory active workers and queues, stop retired tmux/shell workers gracefully, verify no leftover attempt-owned ddx work/claude/codex descendants, set desired_count=1 for helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq via ddx worker/server API, and monitor through server APIs until queues drain or blockers are filed. Update the plan document with migration evidence and any exception beads.

NON-SCOPE
Do not force-kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex/Codex sessions. Do not drain queues by manual implementation outside beads unless explicitly required to unstick a worker. Do not use tmux as the steady-state management mechanism.
title: ops: migrate active project queues to server-managed workers

description:
PROBLEM
During dogfood of ddx-3b721804 on 2026-06-13, `ddx work status --json` reported two different attempt IDs for the same live worker: `workers[0].attempt_id` showed the claim/liveness attempt `20260613T173837-18b8b4af`, while `active_work.records[0].attempt_id` and the execution worktree showed the canonical execution attempt `20260613T174040-2199519f`. Operators need a single stable attempt ID to inspect evidence, cleanup, logs, and process trees.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work_status.go:126-132 says run-state enrichment fills bead/attempt/worktree when liveness did not supply them.
cli/cmd/work_status.go:136-137 skips enrichment when bead_id, attempt_id, and execution_worktree are already present, even if the liveness attempt ID is stale and run-state has a fresher canonical attempt for the same PID.
cli/cmd/work_status.go:228-249 renders the worker attempt from the possibly stale liveness record while active_work renders run-state separately, causing conflicting JSON/text output.

PROPOSED FIX
Make work status choose a canonical attempt record per live worker. Prefer the freshest matching run-state for the PID/worktree when it exists, or explicitly mark stale liveness fields as stale. Ensure `workers[]`, `active_work.records[]`, and text status agree on bead_id, attempt_id, phase, and execution_worktree for a single live attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove liveness sidecars. Do not make all-projects status scan unrelated project run-state files.
title: work status: report canonical execution attempt id consistently

description:
PROBLEM
In a linked DDx worktree, `ddx bead show/export` reported ddx-d94f1857 as closed while `ddx work plan --json` still ranked the same bead as open and next claim. That can make a worker re-run already-completed work and breaks the queue as source of truth.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:286-320 resolves bead commands through beadWorkspaceRoot(), which calls git.FindNearestDDxWorkspace and can prefer a registered/master worktree. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:80-84 bypasses that path and opens bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot)), which prefers the local in-tree .ddx if present. In dogfood on 2026-06-13, the global worktree registry master pointed at an old execute-bead temp path, so bead commands and work plan observed different physical stores.

PROPOSED FIX
- Centralize queue store resolution for all bead/work/status commands behind one project-aware helper.
- Add a linked-worktree regression fixture where the local .ddx snapshot disagrees with the registered/master store and prove bead show, bead list, bead export, bead ready, work plan, and work status use the same store.
- Add a diagnostic when the worktree registry master points at a missing execute-bead path or a path outside the current repository family.
- Ensure the chosen store is visible in verbose/debug output so operators can identify split-brain state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl as the fix. Do not remove convention-root support. Do not change bead lifecycle semantics beyond consistent store selection.
title: work: unify bead store resolution across queue commands
